"{\"content\":\"1: use std::cmp::Ordering;\\n2: \\n3: use rustc_data_structures::intern::Interned;\\n4: use rustc_hir::def_id::DefId;\\n5: use rustc_macros::{HashStable, extension};\\n6: use rustc_type_ir as ir;\\n7: \\n8: use crate::ty::{\\n9:     self, DebruijnIndex, EarlyBinder, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeFlags, Upcast, UpcastFrom, WithCachedTypeInfo,\\n10: };\\n11: \\n12: pub type TraitRef<'tcx> = ir::TraitRef<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n13: pub type AliasTerm<'tcx> = ir::AliasTerm<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n14: pub type ProjectionPredicate<'tcx> = ir::ProjectionPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n15: pub type ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n16: pub type ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialTraitRef<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n17: pub type ExistentialProjection<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialProjection<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n18: pub type TraitPredicate<'tcx> = ir::TraitPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n19: pub type HostEffectPredicate<'tcx> = ir::HostEffectPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n20: pub type ClauseKind<'tcx> = ir::ClauseKind<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n21: pub type PredicateKind<'tcx> = ir::PredicateKind<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n22: pub type NormalizesTo<'tcx> = ir::NormalizesTo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n23: pub type CoercePredicate<'tcx> = ir::CoercePredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n24: pub type SubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ir::SubtypePredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\\n25: pub type OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, T> = ir::OutlivesPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>, T>;\\n26: pub type RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>>;\\n27: pub type TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>>;\\n28: pub type ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, ty::GenericArg<'tcx>>;\\n29: pub type PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n30: pub type PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n31: pub type PolyTypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n32: pub type PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, SubtypePredicate<'tcx>>;\\n33: pub type PolyCoercePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, CoercePredicate<'tcx>>;\\n34: pub type PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n35: \\n36: /// A statement that can be proven by a trait solver. This includes things that may\\n37: /// show up in where clauses, such as trait predicates and projection predicates,\\n38: /// and also things that are emitted as part of type checking such as `DynCompatible`\\n39: /// predicate which is emitted when a type is coerced to a trait object.\\n40: ///\\n41: /// Use this rather than `PredicateKind`, whenever possible.\\n42: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\\n43: #[rustc_pass_by_value]\\n44: pub struct Predicate<'tcx>(\\n45:     pub(super) Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>>>,\\n46: );\\n47: \\n48: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Predicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n49:     fn as_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Clause<'tcx>> {\\n50:         self.as_clause()\\n51:     }\\n52: \\n53:     fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\\n54:         self.allow_normalization()\\n55:     }\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n59:     type Kind = ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::PredicateKind<'tcx>>;\\n60: \\n61:     fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\\n62:         self.kind()\\n63:     }\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::Flags for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n67:     fn flags(&self) -> TypeFlags {\\n68:         self.0.flags\\n69:     }\\n70: \\n71:     fn outer_exclusive_binder(&self) -> ty::DebruijnIndex {\\n72:         self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\\n73:     }\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: impl<'tcx> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n77:     /// Gets the inner `ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>`.\\n78:     #[inline]\\n79:     pub fn kind(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>> {\\n80:         self.0.internee\\n81:     }\\n82: \\n83:     // FIXME(compiler-errors): Think about removing this.\\n84:     #[inline(always)]\\n85:     pub fn flags(self) -> TypeFlags {\\n86:         self.0.flags\\n87:     }\\n88: \\n89:     // FIXME(compiler-errors): Think about removing this.\\n90:     #[inline(always)]\\n91:     pub fn outer_exclusive_binder(self) -> DebruijnIndex {\\n92:         self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\\n93:     }\\n94: \\n95:     /// Flips the polarity of a Predicate.\\n96:     ///\\n97:     /// Given `T: Trait` predicate it returns `T: !Trait` and given `T: !Trait` returns `T: Trait`.\\n98:     pub fn flip_polarity(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Option<Predicate<'tcx>> {\\n99:         let kind = self\\n100:             .kind()\\n101:             .map_bound(|kind| match kind {\\n102:                 PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\\n103:                     trait_ref,\\n104:                     polarity,\\n105:                 })) => Some(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\\n106:                     trait_ref,\\n107:                     polarity: polarity.flip(),\\n108:                 }))),\\n109: \\n110:                 _ => None,\\n111:             })\\n112:             .transpose()?;\\n113: \\n114:         Some(tcx.mk_predicate(kind))\\n115:     }\\n116: \\n117:     /// Whether this projection can be soundly normalized.\\n118:     ///\\n119:     /// Wf predicates must not be normalized, as normalization\\n120:     /// can remove required bounds which would cause us to\\n121:     /// unsoundly accept some programs. See #91068.\\n122:     #[inline]\\n123:     pub fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\\n124:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n125:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::WellFormed(_)) | PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..) => {\\n126:                 false\\n127:             }\\n128:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(_))\\n129:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::HostEffect(..))\\n130:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(_))\\n131:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(_))\\n132:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(_))\\n133:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(..))\\n134:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_))\\n135:             | PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_)\\n136:             | PredicateKind::Subtype(_)\\n137:             | PredicateKind::Coerce(_)\\n138:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(_))\\n139:             | PredicateKind::ConstEquate(_, _)\\n140:             | PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..)\\n141:             | PredicateKind::Ambiguous => true,\\n142:         }\\n143:     }\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n147:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\\n148:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\\n149:             let pred = tcx.short_string(self, path);\\n150:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(pred))\\n151:         })\\n152:     }\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Clause<'tcx> {\\n156:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\\n157:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\\n158:             let clause = tcx.short_string(self, path);\\n159:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(clause))\\n160:         })\\n161:     }\\n162: }\\n163: \\n164: /// A subset of predicates which can be assumed by the trait solver. They show up in\\n165: /// an item's where clauses, hence the name `Clause`, and may either be user-written\\n166: /// (such as traits) or may be inserted during lowering.\\n167: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\\n168: #[rustc_pass_by_value]\\n169: pub struct Clause<'tcx>(\\n170:     pub(super) Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>>>,\\n171: );\\n172: \\n173: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clause<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n174:     fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n175:         self.as_predicate()\\n176:     }\\n177: \\n178:     fn instantiate_supertrait(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n179:         self.instantiate_supertrait(tcx, trait_ref)\\n180:     }\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Clause<'tcx> {\\n184:     type Kind = ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>;\\n185: \\n186:     fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\\n187:         self.kind()\\n188:     }\\n189: }\\n190: \\n191: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\\n192:     pub fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n193:         Predicate(self.0)\\n194:     }\\n195: \\n196:     pub fn kind(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>> {\\n197:         self.0.internee.map_bound(|kind| match kind {\\n198:             PredicateKind::Clause(clause) => clause,\\n199:             _ => unreachable!(),\\n200:         })\\n201:     }\\n202: \\n203:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n204:         let clause = self.kind();\\n205:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n206:             Some(clause.rebind(trait_clause))\\n207:         } else {\\n208:             None\\n209:         }\\n210:     }\\n211: \\n212:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n213:         let clause = self.kind();\\n214:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Projection(projection_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n215:             Some(clause.rebind(projection_clause))\\n216:         } else {\\n217:             None\\n218:         }\\n219:     }\\n220: \\n221:     pub fn as_type_outlives_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n222:         let clause = self.kind();\\n223:         if let ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n224:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\\n225:         } else {\\n226:             None\\n227:         }\\n228:     }\\n229: \\n230:     pub fn as_region_outlives_clause(\\n231:         self,\\n232:     ) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n233:         let clause = self.kind();\\n234:         if let ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n235:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\\n236:         } else {\\n237:             None\\n238:         }\\n239:     }\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clauses<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ty::Clauses<'tcx> {}\\n243: \\n244: #[extension(pub trait ExistentialPredicateStableCmpExt<'tcx>)]\\n245: impl<'tcx> ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\\n246:     /// Compares via an ordering that will not change if modules are reordered or other changes are\\n247:     /// made to the tree. In particular, this ordering is preserved across incremental compilations.\\n248:     fn stable_cmp(&self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\\n249:         match (*self, *other) {\\n250:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => Ordering::Equal,\\n251:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref b)) => {\\n252:                 tcx.def_path_hash(a.def_id).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(b.def_id))\\n253:             }\\n254:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref b)) => {\\n255:                 tcx.def_path_hash(*a).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(*b))\\n256:             }\\n257:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\\n258:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => {\\n259:                 Ordering::Greater\\n260:             }\\n261:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\\n262:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_), _) => Ordering::Greater,\\n263:         }\\n264:     }\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: pub type PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n268: \\n269: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::BoundExistentialPredicates<TyCtxt<'tcx>>\\n270:     for &'tcx ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>\\n271: {\\n272:     fn principal_def_id(self) -> Option<DefId> {\\n273:         self.principal_def_id()\\n274:     }\\n275: \\n276:     fn principal(self) -> Option<ty::PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>> {\\n277:         self.principal()\\n278:     }\\n279: \\n280:     fn auto_traits(self) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = DefId> {\\n281:         self.auto_traits()\\n282:     }\\n283: \\n284:     fn projection_bounds(\\n285:         self,\\n286:     ) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\\n287:         self.projection_bounds()\\n288:     }\\n289: }\\n290: \\n291: impl<'tcx> ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n292:     /// Returns the \\\"principal `DefId`\\\" of this set of existential predicates.\\n293:     ///\\n294:     /// A Rust trait object type consists (in addition to a lifetime bound)\\n295:     /// of a set of trait bounds, which are separated into any number\\n296:     /// of auto-trait bounds, and at most one non-auto-trait bound. The\\n297:     /// non-auto-trait bound is called the \\\"principal\\\" of the trait\\n298:     /// object.\\n299:     ///\\n300:     /// Only the principal can have methods or type parameters (because\\n301:     /// auto traits can have neither of them). This is important, because\\n302:     /// it means the auto traits can be treated as an unordered set (methods\\n303:     /// would force an order for the vtable, while relating traits with\\n304:     /// type parameters without knowing the order to relate them in is\\n305:     /// a rather non-trivial task).\\n306:     ///\\n307:     /// For example, in the trait object `dyn std::fmt::Debug + Sync`, the\\n308:     /// principal bound is `Some(std::fmt::Debug)`, while the auto-trait bounds\\n309:     /// are the set `{Sync}`.\\n310:     ///\\n311:     /// It is also possible to have a \\\"trivial\\\" trait object that\\n312:     /// consists only of auto traits, with no principal - for example,\\n313:     /// `dyn Send + Sync`. In that case, the set of auto-trait bounds\\n314:     /// is `{Send, Sync}`, while there is no principal. These trait objects\\n315:     /// have a \\\"trivial\\\" vtable consisting of just the size, alignment,\\n316:     /// and destructor.\\n317:     pub fn principal(&self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>> {\\n318:         self[0]\\n319:             .map_bound(|this| match this {\\n320:                 ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => Some(tr),\\n321:                 _ => None,\\n322:             })\\n323:             .transpose()\\n324:     }\\n325: \\n326:     pub fn principal_def_id(&self) -> Option<DefId> {\\n327:         self.principal().map(|trait_ref| trait_ref.skip_binder().def_id)\\n328:     }\\n329: \\n330:     #[inline]\\n331:     pub fn projection_bounds(\\n332:         &self,\\n333:     ) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\\n334:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| {\\n335:             predicate\\n336:                 .map_bound(|pred| match pred {\\n337:                     ExistentialPredicate::Projection(projection) => Some(projection),\\n338:                     _ => None,\\n339:                 })\\n340:                 .transpose()\\n341:         })\\n342:     }\\n343: \\n344:     #[inline]\\n345:     pub fn auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = DefId> {\\n346:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n347:             ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => Some(did),\\n348:             _ => None,\\n349:         })\\n350:     }\\n351: \\n352:     pub fn without_auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n353:         self.iter().filter(|predicate| {\\n354:             !matches!(predicate.as_ref().skip_binder(), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_))\\n355:         })\\n356:     }\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: pub type PolyTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>;\\n360: pub type PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>;\\n361: pub type PolyExistentialProjection<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>;\\n362: \\n363: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\\n364:     /// Performs a instantiation suitable for going from a\\n365:     /// poly-trait-ref to supertraits that must hold if that\\n366:     /// poly-trait-ref holds. This is slightly different from a normal\\n367:     /// instantiation in terms of what happens with bound regions. See\\n368:     /// lengthy comment below for details.\\n369:     pub fn instantiate_supertrait(\\n370:         self,\\n371:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n372:         trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>,\\n373:     ) -> Clause<'tcx> {\\n374:         // The interaction between HRTB and supertraits is not entirely\\n375:         // obvious. Let me walk you (and myself) through an example.\\n376:         //\\n377:         // Let's start with an easy case. Consider two traits:\\n378:         //\\n379:         //     trait Foo<'a>: Bar<'a,'a> { }\\n380:         //     trait Bar<'b,'c> { }\\n381:         //\\n382:         // Now, if we have a trait reference `for<'x> T: Foo<'x>`, then\\n383:         // we can deduce that `for<'x> T: Bar<'x,'x>`. Basically, if we\\n384:         // knew that `Foo<'x>` (for any 'x) then we also know that\\n385:         // `Bar<'x,'x>` (for any 'x). This more-or-less falls out from\\n386:         // normal instantiation.\\n387:         //\\n388:         // In terms of why this is sound, the idea is that whenever there\\n389:         // is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>`, it must show that `T:Bar<'a,'a>`\\n390:         // holds. So if there is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>` that applies to\\n391:         // all `'a`, then we must know that `T:Bar<'a,'a>` holds for all\\n392:         // `'a`.\\n393:         //\\n394:         // Another example to be careful of is this:\\n395:         //\\n396:         //     trait Foo1<'a>: for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b> { }\\n397:         //     trait Bar1<'b,'c> { }\\n398:         //\\n399:         // Here, if we have `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`, then what do we know?\\n400:         // The answer is that we know `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`. The\\n401:         // reason is similar to the previous example: any impl of\\n402:         // `T:Foo1<'x>` must show that `for<'b> T: Bar1<'x, 'b>`. So\\n403:         // basically we would want to collapse the bound lifetimes from\\n404:         // the input (`trait_ref`) and the supertraits.\\n405:         //\\n406:         // To achieve this in practice is fairly straightforward. Let's\\n407:         // consider the more complicated scenario:\\n408:         //\\n409:         // - We start out with `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`. In this case, `'x`\\n410:         //   has a De Bruijn index of 1. We want to produce `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`,\\n411:         //   where both `'x` and `'b` would have a DB index of 1.\\n412:         //   The instantiation from the input trait-ref is therefore going to be\\n413:         //   `'a => 'x` (where `'x` has a DB index of 1).\\n414:         // - The supertrait-ref is `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, where `'a` is an\\n415:         //   early-bound parameter and `'b` is a late-bound parameter with a\\n416:         //   DB index of 1.\\n417:         // - If we replace `'a` with `'x` from the input, it too will have\\n418:         //   a DB index of 1, and thus we'll have `for<'x,'b> Bar1<'x,'b>`\\n419:         //   just as we wanted.\\n420:         //\\n421:         // There is only one catch. If we just apply the instantiation `'a\\n422:         // => 'x` to `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, the instantiation code will\\n423:         // adjust the DB index because we instantiating into a binder (it\\n424:         // tries to be so smart...) resulting in `for<'x> for<'b>\\n425:         // Bar1<'x,'b>` (we have no syntax for this, so use your\\n426:         // imagination). Basically the 'x will have DB index of 2 and 'b\\n427:         // will have DB index of 1. Not quite what we want. So we apply\\n428:         // the instantiation to the *contents* of the trait reference,\\n429:         // rather than the trait reference itself (put another way, the\\n430:         // instantiation code expects equal binding levels in the values\\n431:         // from the instantiation and the value being instantiated into, and\\n432:         // this trick achieves that).\\n433: \\n434:         // Working through the second example:\\n435:         // trait_ref: for<'x> T: Foo1<'^0.0>; args: [T, '^0.0]\\n436:         // predicate: for<'b> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0>; args: [Self, 'a, '^0.0]\\n437:         // We want to end up with:\\n438:         //     for<'x, 'b> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n439:         // To do this:\\n440:         // 1) We must shift all bound vars in predicate by the length\\n441:         //    of trait ref's bound vars. So, we would end up with predicate like\\n442:         //    Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\\n443:         // 2) We can then apply the trait args to this, ending up with\\n444:         //    T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n445:         // 3) Finally, to create the final bound vars, we concatenate the bound\\n446:         //    vars of the trait ref with those of the predicate:\\n447:         //    ['x, 'b]\\n448:         let bound_pred = self.kind();\\n449:         let pred_bound_vars = bound_pred.bound_vars();\\n450:         let trait_bound_vars = trait_ref.bound_vars();\\n451:         // 1) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0> -> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\\n452:         let shifted_pred =\\n453:             tcx.shift_bound_var_indices(trait_bound_vars.len(), bound_pred.skip_binder());\\n454:         // 2) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1> -> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n455:         let new = EarlyBinder::bind(shifted_pred).instantiate(tcx, trait_ref.skip_binder().args);\\n456:         // 3) ['x] + ['b] -> ['x, 'b]\\n457:         let bound_vars =\\n458:             tcx.mk_bound_variable_kinds_from_iter(trait_bound_vars.iter().chain(pred_bound_vars));\\n459: \\n460:         // FIXME: Is it really perf sensitive to use reuse_or_mk_predicate here?\\n461:         tcx.reuse_or_mk_predicate(\\n462:             self.as_predicate(),\\n463:             ty::Binder::bind_with_vars(PredicateKind::Clause(new), bound_vars),\\n464:         )\\n465:         .expect_clause()\\n466:     }\\n467: }\\n468: \\n469: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PredicateKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n470:     fn upcast_from(from: PredicateKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n471:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\\n472:     }\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n476:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n477:         tcx.mk_predicate(from)\\n478:     }\\n479: }\\n480: \\n481: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n482:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n483:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from)))\\n484:     }\\n485: }\\n486: \\n487: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n488:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n489:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(PredicateKind::Clause))\\n490:     }\\n491: }\\n492: \\n493: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, Clause<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n494:     fn upcast_from(from: Clause<'tcx>, _tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n495:         from.as_predicate()\\n496:     }\\n497: }\\n498: \\n499: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n500:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n501:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from))).expect_clause()\\n502:     }\\n503: }\\n504: \\n505: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n506:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n507:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(|clause| PredicateKind::Clause(clause))).expect_clause()\\n508:     }\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n512:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n513:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\\n514:     }\\n515: }\\n516: \\n517: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n518:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n519:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n520:         p.expect_clause()\\n521:     }\\n522: }\\n523: \\n524: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n525:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n526:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n527:         pred.upcast(tcx)\\n528:     }\\n529: }\\n530: \\n531: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n532:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n533:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n534:         pred.upcast(tcx)\\n535:     }\\n536: }\\n537: \\n538: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n539:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n540:         PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(from)).upcast(tcx)\\n541:     }\\n542: }\\n543: \\n544: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n545:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n546:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n547:     }\\n548: }\\n549: \\n550: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n551:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n552:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n553:         p.expect_clause()\\n554:     }\\n555: }\\n556: \\n557: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n558:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n559:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n560:         p.expect_clause()\\n561:     }\\n562: }\\n563: \\n564: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n565:     fn upcast_from(from: RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n566:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n567:     }\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n571:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n572:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n573:     }\\n574: }\\n575: \\n576: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n577:     fn upcast_from(from: TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n578:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n579:     }\\n580: }\\n581: \\n582: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n583:     fn upcast_from(from: ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n584:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n585:     }\\n586: }\\n587: \\n588: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n589:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n590:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n591:     }\\n592: }\\n593: \\n594: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n595:     fn upcast_from(from: ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n596:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n597:         p.expect_clause()\\n598:     }\\n599: }\\n600: \\n601: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n602:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n603:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n604:         p.expect_clause()\\n605:     }\\n606: }\\n607: \\n608: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\\n609:     for Predicate<'tcx>\\n610: {\\n611:     fn upcast_from(\\n612:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\\n613:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n614:     ) -> Self {\\n615:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\\n616:     }\\n617: }\\n618: \\n619: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\\n620:     for Clause<'tcx>\\n621: {\\n622:     fn upcast_from(\\n623:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\\n624:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n625:     ) -> Self {\\n626:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\\n627:     }\\n628: }\\n629: \\n630: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, NormalizesTo<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n631:     fn upcast_from(from: NormalizesTo<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n632:         PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(from).upcast(tcx)\\n633:     }\\n634: }\\n635: \\n636: impl<'tcx> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n637:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n638:         let predicate = self.kind();\\n639:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n640:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\\n641:             _ => None,\\n642:         }\\n643:     }\\n644: \\n645:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n646:         let predicate = self.kind();\\n647:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n648:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\\n649:             _ => None,\\n650:         }\\n651:     }\\n652: \\n653:     /// Matches a `PredicateKind::Clause` and turns it into a `Clause`, otherwise returns `None`.\\n654:     pub fn as_clause(self) -> Option<Clause<'tcx>> {\\n655:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n656:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Some(self.expect_clause()),\\n657:             _ => None,\\n658:         }\\n659:     }\\n660: \\n661:     /// Assert that the predicate is a clause.\\n662:     pub fn expect_clause(self) -> Clause<'tcx> {\\n663:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n664:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Clause(self.0),\\n665:             _ => bug!(\\\"{self} is not a clause\\\"),\\n666:         }\\n667:     }\\n668: }\\n669: \\n670: // Some types are used a lot. Make sure they don't unintentionally get bigger.\\n671: #[cfg(target_pointer_width = \\\"64\\\")]\\n672: mod size_asserts {\\n673:     use rustc_data_structures::static_assert_size;\\n674: \\n675:     use super::*;\\n676:     // tidy-alphabetical-start\\n677:     static_assert_size!(PredicateKind<'_>, 32);\\n678:     static_assert_size!(WithCachedTypeInfo<PredicateKind<'_>>, 56);\\n679:     // tidy-alphabetical-end\\n680: }\\n681: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs\"}"

"{\"content\":\"710:     /// practical obstacles. Among other things, `FnCtxt::coerce` presently\\n711:     /// records adjustments that are required on the HIR in order to perform\\n712:     /// the coercion, and we don't currently have a way to manage that.)\\n713:     pub fn coerce_predicate(\\n714:         &self,\\n715:         cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\\n716:         param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\\n717:         predicate: ty::PolyCoercePredicate<'tcx>,\\n718:     ) -> Result<InferResult<'tcx, ()>, (TyVid, TyVid)> {\\n719:         let subtype_predicate = predicate.map_bound(|p| ty::SubtypePredicate {\\n720:             a_is_expected: false, // when coercing from `a` to `b`, `b` is expected\\n721:             a: p.a,\\n722:             b: p.b,\\n723:         });\\n724:         self.subtype_predicate(cause, param_env, subtype_predicate)\\n725:     }\\n726: \\n727:     pub fn subtype_predicate(\\n728:         &self,\\n729:         cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\\n730:         param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\\n731:         predicate: ty::PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx>,\\n732:     ) -> Result<InferResult<'tcx, ()>, (TyVid, TyVid)> {\\n733:         // Check for two unresolved inference variables, in which case we can\\n734:         // make no progress. This is partly a micro-optimization, but it's\\n735:         // also an opportunity to \\\"sub-unify\\\" the variables. This isn't\\n736:         // *necessary* to prevent cycles, because they would eventually be sub-unified\\n737:         // anyhow during generalization, but it helps with diagnostics (we can detect\\n738:         // earlier that they are sub-unified).\\n739:         //\\n740:         // Note that we can just skip the binders here because\\n741:         // type variables can't (at present, at\\n742:         // least) capture any of the things bound by this binder.\\n743:         //\\n744:         // Note that this sub here is not just for diagnostics - it has semantic\\n745:         // effects as well.\\n746:         let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\\n747:         let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\\n748:         match (r_a.kind(), r_b.kind()) {\\n749:             (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\\n750:                 self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\\n751:                 return Err((a_vid, b_vid));\\n752:             }\\n753:             _ => {}\\n754:         }\\n755: \\n756:         self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\\n757:             if a_is_expected {\\n758:                 Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\\n759:             } else {\\n760:                 Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\\n761:             }\\n762:         })\\n763:     }\\n764: \\n765:     /// Number of type variables created so far.\\n766:     pub fn num_ty_vars(&self) -> usize {\\n767:         self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().num_vars()\\n768:     }\\n769: \\n770:     pub fn next_ty_vid(&self, span: Span) -> TyVid {\\n771:         self.next_ty_vid_with_origin(TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None })\\n772:     }\\n773: \\n774:     pub fn next_ty_vid_with_origin(&self, origin: TypeVariableOrigin) -> TyVid {\\n775:         self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().new_var(self.universe(), origin)\\n776:     }\\n777: \\n778:     pub fn next_ty_vid_in_universe(&self, span: Span, universe: ty::UniverseIndex) -> TyVid {\\n779:         let origin = TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None };\\n780:         self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().new_var(universe, origin)\\n781: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs%3FL710-780\"}"

"{\"content\":\"340:                 obligation.predicate.kind().no_bound_vars()\\n341:             })\\n342:             .filter_map(|atom| {\\n343:                 // We consider both subtyping and coercion to imply 'flow' from\\n344:                 // some position in the code `a` to a different position `b`.\\n345:                 // This is then used to determine which variables interact with\\n346:                 // live code, and as such must fall back to `()` to preserve\\n347:                 // soundness.\\n348:                 //\\n349:                 // In practice currently the two ways that this happens is\\n350:                 // coercion and subtyping.\\n351:                 let (a, b) = match atom {\\n352:                     ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\\n353:                     ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\\n354:                         (a, b)\\n355:                     }\\n356:                     _ => return None,\\n357:                 };\\n358: \\n359:                 let a_vid = self.root_vid(a)?;\\n360:                 let b_vid = self.root_vid(b)?;\\n361:                 Some((a_vid, b_vid))\\n362:             })\\n363:             .collect();\\n364:         debug!(\\\"create_coercion_graph: coercion_edges={:?}\\\", coercion_edges);\\n365:         let num_ty_vars = self.num_ty_vars();\\n366: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs%3FL340-365\"}"

58. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# SubtypePredicate \u2192 SubtypeRelation Refactoring - Complete Analysis\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis refactoring renames `SubtypePredicate` to `SubtypeRelation` and renames its fields `a` \u2192 `sub_ty` and `b` \u2192 `super_ty` across 13 core files in the Rust compiler to improve semantic clarity. The change affects type system definitions, predicate handling, and error reporting across 9 compiler crates.\n\n---\n\n## Files Examined\n\n### 1. Core Definition Files (Must Change First)\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs** \u2014 Original struct definition: `pub struct SubtypePredicate<I: Interner>` with fields `a_is_expected`, `a`, `b`\n- **compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs** \u2014 Public API mirror: simplified `pub struct SubtypePredicate` with fields `a`, `b`\n- **compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs** \u2014 Type aliases: `SubtypePredicate<'tcx>` and `PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx>`\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate_kind.rs** \u2014 Enum variant: `PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate<I>)`\n\n### 2. Pattern Matching/Destructuring Sites (All Usages Must Update)\n- **compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs** \u2014 Lines 719-723, 746-747, 756-759\n  - Conversion from `CoercePredicate`, field access via `.a`/`.b`, destructuring in closures\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/flags.rs** \u2014 Line 394\n  - Pattern match: `SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }`\n- **compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs** \u2014 Line 353\n  - Pattern match: `SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }`\n- **compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs** \u2014 Line 93\n  - Pattern match: `SubtypePredicate { a, b, a_is_expected: _ }`\n- **compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/ambiguity.rs** \u2014 Line 503\n  - Destructuring: `let ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b } = data;`\n- **compiler/rustc_next_trait_solver/src/solve/mod.rs** \u2014 Lines 112-115, 121-128\n  - Construction with struct literal, field access `.a`/`.b`\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/relate/solver_relating.rs** \u2014 Lines 200-203, 213-216\n  - Construction with variance-based field ordering\n- **compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs** \u2014 Lines 141-145, 155-159\n  - Construction with variance-based field ordering (Covariant vs Contravariant)\n\n### 3. Display/Printing Implementations\n- **compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs** \u2014 Lines 3257-3262\n  - Prints fields `self.a` and `self.b` with `<:` operator\n\n### 4. Supporting Infrastructure (Re-exports, Traits, Bounds)\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/interner.rs** \u2014 Trait bounds referencing `SubtypePredicate`\n- **compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/ir_print.rs** \u2014 Print trait bounds for `SubtypePredicate`\n- **compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/mod.rs** \u2014 Re-exports from rustc_type_ir\n\n---\n\n## Dependency Chain\n\n### Layer 1: Definitions (Foundation)\n1. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs` (Generic `SubtypePredicate<I>`)\n2. `compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs` (Public API version)\n3. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate_kind.rs` (Uses in `PredicateKind::Subtype`)\n\n### Layer 2: Type Aliases & Re-exports (Convenience)\n4. `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs` (Aliases for `'tcx` version)\n5. `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/mod.rs` (Re-exports public definitions)\n\n### Layer 3: Trait Bounds & Infrastructure\n6. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/interner.rs` (IrPrint bounds)\n7. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/ir_print.rs` (Printing trait definitions)\n\n### Layer 4: Usage Sites (All must update field names)\n8. `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs` (Type inference, field access)\n9. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/flags.rs` (Type flags computation)\n10. `compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs` (Coercion graph)\n11. `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs` (Error reporting)\n12. `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/ambiguity.rs` (Ambiguity handling)\n13. `compiler/rustc_next_trait_solver/src/solve/mod.rs` (Next-gen trait solver)\n14. `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/relate/solver_relating.rs` (Type relating, variance)\n15. `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs` (Type relating, variance)\n16. `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs` (Display implementation)\n\n---\n\n## Code Changes Required\n\n### Change Type Summary\n- **Struct/Type Name Changes**: 2 (in two locations: rustc_type_ir, rustc_public)\n- **Field Name Changes**: 4+ per file \u00d7 13-16 files (all destructuring and construction sites)\n- **Type Alias Updates**: 2 (names stay same, struct name changes)\n- **Pattern Match Updates**: 8 files with pattern matching expressions\n- **Constructor Call Updates**: 6 files with struct construction\n\n### Change Pattern Categories\n\n#### Pattern 1: Struct Definition (2 files)\n```diff\n# OLD:\npub struct SubtypePredicate<I: Interner> {\n    pub a_is_expected: bool,\n    pub a: I::Ty,\n    pub b: I::Ty,\n}\n\n# NEW:\npub struct SubtypeRelation<I: Interner> {\n    pub a_is_expected: bool,\n    pub sub_ty: I::Ty,\n    pub super_ty: I::Ty,\n}\n```\n\n#### Pattern 2: Pattern Matching (8 files)\n```diff\n# OLD:\nlet ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b } = ...;\n\n# NEW:\nlet ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected, sub_ty, super_ty } = ...;\n```\n\n#### Pattern 3: Field Construction (6 files)\n```diff\n# OLD:\nty::SubtypePredicate {\n    a_is_expected: false,\n    a: some_type,\n    b: other_type,\n}\n\n# NEW:\nty::SubtypeRelation {\n    a_is_expected: false,\n    sub_ty: some_type,\n    super_ty: other_type,\n}\n```\n\n#### Pattern 4: Field Access (4 files)\n```diff\n# OLD:\nlet r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\nlet r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\n\n# NEW:\nlet r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().sub_ty);\nlet r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().super_ty);\n```\n\n#### Pattern 5: Display/Printing (1 file)\n```diff\n# OLD:\nty::SubtypePredicate<'tcx> {\n    self.a.print(p)?;\n    write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n    p.reset_type_limit();\n    self.b.print(p)?;\n}\n\n# NEW:\nty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {\n    self.sub_ty.print(p)?;\n    write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n    p.reset_type_limit();\n    self.super_ty.print(p)?;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Analysis: Refactoring Strategy\n\n### Why This Change?\nThe current naming (`a` and `b`) is semantically opaque. The new naming (`sub_ty` for subtype, `super_ty` for supertype) immediately clarifies that:\n- `sub_ty <: super_ty` (subtype relation)\n- The `a_is_expected` flag controls which is presented as \"expected\" in diagnostics\n- This matches the documentation: \"Encodes that `a` must be a subtype of `b`\" \u2014 but now it's explicit in code\n\n### Affected Crates (9 total)\n1. `rustc_type_ir` \u2014 Core type IR definitions\n2. `rustc_public` \u2014 Public API layer\n3. `rustc_middle` \u2014 Mid-level type system\n4. `rustc_infer` \u2014 Type inference engine\n5. `rustc_type_ir` (relate module) \u2014 Type relation algorithms\n6. `rustc_hir_typeck` \u2014 HIR type checking\n7. `rustc_trait_selection` \u2014 Trait solver and error reporting\n8. `rustc_next_trait_solver` \u2014 Next-generation solver\n9. (Implicit) All downstream users get the new names via re-exports\n\n### Scope & Complexity\n- **Total files to modify**: 13 core files + supporting infrastructure\n- **Line count affected**: ~25 distinct code sections\n- **Breaking changes**: None within the same crate (all internal to Rust compiler)\n- **Cross-crate impact**: Minimal \u2014 mostly internal compiler-only APIs\n- **Test coverage**: Existing tests validate functionality; naming changes don't alter logic\n\n### Verification Approach\n1. **Compilation check**: `cargo check` on affected crates\n2. **Type safety**: Rust compiler ensures all pattern matches are exhaustive\n3. **Functional tests**: Existing test suite validates behavior\n4. **Grep verification**: Search for residual `SubtypePredicate` (should only find in Subtype variant name or comments)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n### Field Renaming Rationale\n- `a` \u2192 `sub_ty`: The type that must be a **sub**type\n- `b` \u2192 `super_ty`: The type that must be a **super**type\n- `a_is_expected` \u2192 (unchanged): Flag controlling diagnostic presentation, distinct semantic meaning\n\n### Variance-Based Field Swapping\nFiles `type_relating.rs` and `solver_relating.rs` have special handling:\n```rust\n// Contravariant case swaps the fields:\nty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: false, sub_ty: b, super_ty: a }\n```\nThis is intentional and correct \u2014 contravariance reverses the subtype relationship. Verify comments are preserved.\n\n### Enum Variant Name\nThe `PredicateKind::Subtype` variant **name stays as-is** (only the struct type inside changes). This is intentional because:\n- The variant name `Subtype` is a semantic label (not a struct name)\n- Only the type parameter changes: `PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypeRelation<I>)` vs `PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypePredicate<I>)`\n\n---\n\n## Files Requiring Changes - Complete Checklist\n\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs` (Struct def + impl, 1 occurrence)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs` (Public struct def, 1 occurrence)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs` (Type alias, 2 occurrences)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate_kind.rs` (Variant type, 1 occurrence)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs` (5 occurrences: construction + field access + destructuring)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/flags.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/ambiguity.rs` (1 occurrence: destructuring)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_next_trait_solver/src/solve/mod.rs` (3 occurrences: construction + field access)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/relate/solver_relating.rs` (2 occurrences: construction with variance)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs` (2 occurrences: construction with variance)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs` (1 occurrence: field access in printing)\n\n**Total: 13 files, ~25 code sections**\n\n---\n\n## Expected Outcome\n\nAfter this refactoring:\n1. The struct is named `SubtypeRelation<I>` instead of `SubtypePredicate<I>`\n2. Fields are `sub_ty` and `super_ty` instead of `a` and `b`\n3. All pattern matches, destructuring, and field accesses are updated\n4. The code compiles without errors\n5. All existing tests pass (behavior unchanged, only naming improved)\n6. Documentation comments and variant names remain appropriate\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md"
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: use std::fmt;\\n2: use std::hash::Hash;\\n3: \\n4: use derive_where::derive_where;\\n5: #[cfg(feature = \\\"nightly\\\")]\\n6: use rustc_macros::{\\n7:     Decodable, Decodable_NoContext, Encodable, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext,\\n8: };\\n9: use rustc_type_ir_macros::{Lift_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, TypeVisitable_Generic};\\n10: \\n11: use crate::inherent::*;\\n12: use crate::lift::Lift;\\n13: use crate::upcast::{Upcast, UpcastFrom};\\n14: use crate::visit::TypeVisitableExt as _;\\n15: use crate::{self as ty, Interner};\\n16: \\n17: /// `A: 'region`\\n18: #[derive_where(Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner, A)]\\n19: #[derive_where(Copy; I: Interner, A: Copy)]\\n20: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic)]\\n21: #[cfg_attr(\\n22:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n23:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n24: )]\\n25: pub struct OutlivesPredicate<I: Interner, A>(pub A, pub I::Region);\\n26: \\n27: impl<I: Interner, A: Eq> Eq for OutlivesPredicate<I, A> {}\\n28: \\n29: // FIXME: We manually derive `Lift` because the `derive(Lift_Generic)` doesn't\\n30: // understand how to turn `A` to `A::Lifted` in the output `type Lifted`.\\n31: impl<I: Interner, U: Interner, A> Lift<U> for OutlivesPredicate<I, A>\\n32: where\\n33:     A: Lift<U>,\\n34:     I::Region: Lift<U, Lifted = U::Region>,\\n35: {\\n36:     type Lifted = OutlivesPredicate<U, A::Lifted>;\\n37: \\n38:     fn lift_to_interner(self, cx: U) -> Option<Self::Lifted> {\\n39:         Some(OutlivesPredicate(self.0.lift_to_interner(cx)?, self.1.lift_to_interner(cx)?))\\n40:     }\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: /// A complete reference to a trait. These take numerous guises in syntax,\\n44: /// but perhaps the most recognizable form is in a where-clause:\\n45: /// ```ignore (illustrative)\\n46: /// T: Foo<U>\\n47: /// ```\\n48: /// This would be represented by a trait-reference where the `DefId` is the\\n49: /// `DefId` for the trait `Foo` and the args define `T` as parameter 0,\\n50: /// and `U` as parameter 1.\\n51: ///\\n52: /// Trait references also appear in object types like `Foo<U>`, but in\\n53: /// that case the `Self` parameter is absent from the generic parameters.\\n54: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\\n55: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n56: #[cfg_attr(\\n57:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n58:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n59: )]\\n60: pub struct TraitRef<I: Interner> {\\n61:     pub def_id: I::TraitId,\\n62:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\\n63:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `TraitRef` without\\n64:     /// calling [`TraitRef::new_from_args`].\\n65:     _use_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: impl<I: Interner> Eq for TraitRef<I> {}\\n69: \\n70: impl<I: Interner> TraitRef<I> {\\n71:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, trait_def_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> Self {\\n72:         interner.debug_assert_args_compatible(trait_def_id.into(), args);\\n73:         Self { def_id: trait_def_id, args, _use_trait_ref_new_instead: () }\\n74:     }\\n75: \\n76:     pub fn new(\\n77:         interner: I,\\n78:         trait_def_id: I::TraitId,\\n79:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\\n80:     ) -> Self {\\n81:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\\n82:         Self::new_from_args(interner, trait_def_id, args)\\n83:     }\\n84: \\n85:     pub fn from_assoc(interner: I, trait_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> TraitRef<I> {\\n86:         let generics = interner.generics_of(trait_id.into());\\n87:         TraitRef::new(interner, trait_id, args.iter().take(generics.count()))\\n88:     }\\n89: \\n90:     /// Returns a `TraitRef` of the form `P0: Foo<P1..Pn>` where `Pi`\\n91:     /// are the parameters defined on trait.\\n92:     pub fn identity(interner: I, def_id: I::TraitId) -> TraitRef<I> {\\n93:         TraitRef::new_from_args(\\n94:             interner,\\n95:             def_id,\\n96:             I::GenericArgs::identity_for_item(interner, def_id.into()),\\n97:         )\\n98:     }\\n99: \\n100:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\\n101:     "content": "1: use std::fmt;\n2: use std::hash::Hash;\n3: \n4: use derive_where::derive_where;\n5: #[cfg(feature = \"nightly\")]\n6: use rustc_macros::{\n7:     Decodable, Decodable_NoContext, Encodable, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext,\n8: };\n9: use rustc_type_ir_macros::{Lift_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, TypeVisitable_Generic};\n10: \n11: use crate::inherent::*;\n12: use crate::lift::Lift;\n13: use crate::upcast::{Upcast, UpcastFrom};\n14: use crate::visit::TypeVisitableExt as _;\n15: use crate::{self as ty, Interner};\n16: \n17: /// `A: 'region`\n18: #[derive_where(Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner, A)]\n19: #[derive_where(Copy; I: Interner, A: Copy)]\n20: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic)]\n21: #[cfg_attr(\n22:     feature = \"nightly\",\n23:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n24: )]\n25: pub struct OutlivesPredicate<I: Interner, A>(pub A, pub I::Region);\n26: \n27: impl<I: Interner, A: Eq> Eq for OutlivesPredicate<I, A> {}\n28: \n29: // FIXME: We manually derive `Lift` because the `derive(Lift_Generic)` doesn't\n30: // understand how to turn `A` to `A::Lifted` in the output `type Lifted`.\n31: impl<I: Interner, U: Interner, A> Lift<U> for OutlivesPredicate<I, A>\n32: where\n33:     A: Lift<U>,\n34:     I::Region: Lift<U, Lifted = U::Region>,\n35: {\n36:     type Lifted = OutlivesPredicate<U, A::Lifted>;\n37: \n38:     fn lift_to_interner(self, cx: U) -> Option<Self::Lifted> {\n39:         Some(OutlivesPredicate(self.0.lift_to_interner(cx)?, self.1.lift_to_interner(cx)?))\n40:     }\n41: }\n42: \n43: /// A complete reference to a trait.
