with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\\n101:         TraitRef::new(\\n102:             interner,\\n103:             self.def_id,\\n104:             [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)),\\n105:         )\\n106:     }\\n107: \\n108:     #[inline]\\n109:     pub fn self_ty(&self) -> I::Ty {\\n110:         self.args.type_at(0)\\n111:     }\\n112: }\\n113: \\n114: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>> {\\n115:     pub fn self_ty(&self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\\n116:         self.map_bound_ref(|tr| tr.self_ty())\\n117:     }\\n118: \\n119:     pub fn def_id(&self) -> I::TraitId {\\n120:         self.skip_binder().def_id\\n121:     }\\n122: \\n123:     pub fn to_host_effect_clause(self, cx: I, constness: BoundConstness) -> I::Clause {\\n124:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| {\\n125:             ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(HostEffectPredicate { trait_ref, constness })\\n126:         })\\n127:         .upcast(cx)\\n128:     }\\n129: }\\n130: \\n131: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\\n132: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n133: #[cfg_attr(\\n134:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n135:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n136: )]\\n137: pub struct TraitPredicate<I: Interner> {\\n138:     pub trait_ref: TraitRef<I>,\\n139: \\n140:     /// If polarity is Positive: we are proving that the trait is implemented.\\n141:     ///\\n142:     /// If polarity is Negative: we are proving that a negative impl of this trait\\n143:     /// exists. (Note that coherence also checks whether negative impls of supertraits\\n144:     /// exist via a series of predicates.)\\n145:     pub polarity: PredicatePolarity,\\n146: }\\n147: \\n148: impl<I: Interner> Eq for TraitPredicate<I> {}\\n149: \\n150: impl<I: Interner> TraitPredicate<I> {\\n151:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\\n152:         Self {\\n153:             trait_ref: self.trait_ref.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty),\\n154:             polarity: self.polarity,\\n155:         }\\n156:     }\\n157: \\n158:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\\n159:         self.trait_ref.def_id\\n160:     }\\n161: \\n162:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\\n163:         self.trait_ref.self_ty()\\n164:     }\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, TraitPredicate<I>> {\\n168:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\\n169:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\\n170:         self.skip_binder().def_id()\\n171:     }\\n172: \\n173:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\\n174:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.self_ty())\\n175:     }\\n176: \\n177:     #[inline]\\n178:     pub fn polarity(self) -> PredicatePolarity {\\n179:         self.skip_binder().polarity\\n180:     }\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: impl<I: Interner> UpcastFrom<I, TraitRef<I>> for TraitPredicate<I> {\\n184:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<I>, _tcx: I) -> Self {\\n185:         TraitPredicate { trait_ref: from, polarity: PredicatePolarity::Positive }\\n186:     }\\n187: }\\n188: \\n189: impl<I: Interner> UpcastFrom<I, ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>>> for ty::Binder<I, TraitPredicate<I>> {\\n190:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>>, _tcx: I) -> Self {\\n191:         from.map_bound(|trait_ref| TraitPredicate {\\n192:             trait_ref,\\n193:             polarity: PredicatePolarity::Positive,\\n194:         })\\n195:     }\\n196: }\\n197: \\n198: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for TraitPredicate<I> {\\n199:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n200:         write!(f, \\\"TraitPredicate({:?}, polarity:{:?})\\\", self.trait_ref, self.polarity)\\n201:     }\\n202: }\\n203: \\n204: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\\n205: #[cfg_attr(\\n206:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n207:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n208: )]\\n209: pub enum ImplPolarity {\\n210:     /// `impl Trait for Type`\\n211:     Positive,\\n212:     /// `impl !Trait for Type`\\n213:     Negative,\\n214:     /// `#[rustc_reservation_impl] impl Trait for Type`\\n215:     ///\\n216:     /// This is a \\\"stability hack\\\", not a real Rust feature.\\n217:     /// See #64631 for details.\\n218:     Reservation,\\n219: }\\n220: \\n221: impl fmt::Display for ImplPolarity {\\n222:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n223:         match self {\\n224:             Self::Positive => f.write_str(\\\"positive\\\"),\\n225:             Self::Negative => f.write_str(\\\"negative\\\"),\\n226:             Self::Reservation => f.write_str(\\\"reservation\\\"),\\n227:         }\\n228:     }\\n229: }\\n230: \\n231: impl ImplPolarity {\\n232:     /// The polarity marker in front of the impl trait ref if applicable.\\n233:     pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {\\n234:         match self {\\n235:             Self::Positive => \\\"\\\",\\n236:             Self::Negative => \\\"!\\\",\\n237:             Self::Reservation => \\\"\\\",\\n238:         }\\n239:     }\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: /// Polarity for a trait predicate. May either be negative or positive.\\n243: /// Distinguished from [`ImplPolarity`] since we never compute goals with\\n244: /// \\\"reservation\\\" level.\\n245: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\\n246: #[cfg_attr(\\n247:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n248:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n249: )]\\n250: pub enum PredicatePolarity {\\n251:     /// `Type: Trait`\\n252:     Positive,\\n253:     /// `Type: !Trait`\\n254:     Negative,\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: impl PredicatePolarity {\\n258:     /// Flips polarity by turning `Positive` into `Negative` and `Negative` into `Positive`.\\n259:     pub fn flip(&self) -> PredicatePolarity {\\n260:         match self {\\n261:             PredicatePolarity::Positive => PredicatePolarity::Negative,\\n262:             PredicatePolarity::Negative => PredicatePolarity::Positive,\\n263:         }\\n264:     }\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: impl fmt::Display for PredicatePolarity {\\n268:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n269:         match self {\\n270:             Self::Positive => f.write_str(\\\"positive\\\"),\\n271:             Self::Negative => f.write_str(\\\"negative\\\"),\\n272:         }\\n273:     }\\n274: }\\n275: \\n276: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n277: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n278: #[cfg_attr(\\n279:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n280:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n281: )]\\n282: pub enum ExistentialPredicate<I: Interner> {\\n283:     /// E.g., `Iterator`.\\n284:     Trait(ExistentialTraitRef<I>),\\n285:     /// E.g., `Iterator::Item = T`.\\n286:     Projection(ExistentialProjection<I>),\\n287:     /// E.g., `Send`.\\n288:     AutoTrait(I::TraitId),\\n289: }\\n290: \\n291: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialPredicate<I> {}\\n292: \\n293: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialPredicate<I>> {\\n294:     /// Given an existential predicate like `?Self: PartialEq<u32>` (e.g., derived from `dyn PartialEq<u32>`),\\n295:     /// and a concrete type `self_ty`, returns a full predicate where the existentially quantified variable `?Self`\\n296:     /// has been replaced with `self_ty` (e.g., `self_ty: PartialEq<u32>`, in our example).\\n297:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> I::Clause {\\n298:         match self.skip_binder() {\\n299:             ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => self.rebind(tr).with_self_ty(cx, self_ty).upcast(cx),\\n300:             ExistentialPredicate::Projection(p) => {\\n301:                 self.rebind(p.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty)).upcast(cx)\\n302:             }\\n303:             ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => {\\n304:                 let generics = cx.generics_of(did.into());\\n305:                 let trait_ref = if generics.count() == 1 {\\n306:                     ty::TraitRef::new(cx, did, [self_ty])\\n307:                 } else {\\n308:                     // If this is an ill-formed auto trait, then synthesize\\n309:                     // new error args for the missing generics.\\n310:                     let err_args =\\n311:                         GenericArgs::extend_with_error(cx, did.into(), &[self_ty.into()]);\\n312:                     ty::TraitRef::new_from_args(cx, did, err_args)\\n313:                 };\\n314:                 self.rebind(trait_ref).upcast(cx)\\n315:             }\\n316:         }\\n317:     }\\n318: }\\n319: \\n320: /// An existential reference to a trait, where `Self` is erased.\\n321: /// For example, the trait object `Trait<'a, 'b, X, Y>` is:\\n322: /// ```ignore (illustrative)\\n323: /// exists T. T: Trait<'a, 'b, X, Y>\\n324: /// ```\\n325: /// The generic parameters don't include the erased `Self`, only trait\\n326: /// type and lifetime parameters (`[X, Y]` and `['a, 'b]` above).\\n327: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\\n328: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n329: #[cfg_attr(\\n330:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n331:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n332: )]\\n333: pub struct ExistentialTraitRef<I: Interner> {\\n334:     pub def_id: I::TraitId,\\n335:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\\n336:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `ExistentialTraitRef` without\\n337:     /// calling [`ExistentialTraitRef::new_from_args`].\\n338:     _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\\n339: }\\n340: \\n341: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialTraitRef<I> {}\\n342: \\n343: impl<I: Interner> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\\n344:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, trait_def_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> Self {\\n345:         interner.debug_assert_existential_args_compatible(trait_def_id.into(), args);\\n346:         Self { def_id: trait_def_id, args, _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: () }\\n347:     }\\n348: \\n349:     pub fn new(\\n350:         interner: I,\\n351:         trait_def_id: I::TraitId,\\n352:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\\n353:     ) -> Self {\\n354:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\\n355:         Self::new_from_args(interner, trait_def_id, args)\\n356:     }\\n357: \\n358:     pub fn erase_self_ty(interner: I, trait_ref: TraitRef<I>) -> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\\n359:         // Assert there is a Self.\\n360:         trait_ref.args.type_at(0);\\n361: \\n362:         ExistentialTraitRef {\\n363:             def_id: trait_ref.def_id,\\n364:             args: interner.mk_args(&trait_ref.args.as_slice()[1..]),\\n365:             _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\\n366:         }\\n367:     }\\n368: \\n369:     /// Object types don't have a self type specified. Therefore, when\\n370:     /// we convert the principal trait-ref into a normal trait-ref,\\n371:     /// you must give *some* self type. A common choice is `mk_err()`\\n372:     /// or some placeholder type.\\n373:     pub fn with_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> TraitRef<I> {\\n374:         // otherwise the escaping vars would be captured by the binder\\n375:         // debug_assert!(!self_ty.has_escaping_bound_vars());\\n376: \\n377:         TraitRef::new(interner, self.def_id, [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter()))\\n378:     }\\n379: }\\n380: \\n381: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialTraitRef<I>> {\\n382:     pub fn def_id(&self) -> I::TraitId {\\n383:         self.skip_binder().def_id\\n384:     }\\n385: \\n386:     /// Object types don't have a self type specified. Therefore, when\\n387:     /// we convert the principal trait-ref into a normal trait-ref,\\n388:     /// you must give *some* self type. A common choice is `mk_err()`\\n389:     /// or some placeholder type.\\n390:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>> {\\n391:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty))\\n392:     }\\n393: }\\n394: \\n395: /// A `ProjectionPredicate` for an `ExistentialTraitRef`.\\n396: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n397: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n398: #[cfg_attr(\\n399:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n400:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n401: )]\\n402: pub struct ExistentialProjection<I: Interner> {\\n403:     pub def_id: I::DefId,\\n404:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\\n405:     pub term: I::Term,\\n406: \\n407:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `ExistentialProjection`\\n408:     /// without using [`ExistentialProjection::new_from_args`].\\n409:     use_existential_projection_new_instead: (),\\n410: }\\n411: \\n412: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialProjection<I> {}\\n413: \\n414: impl<I: Interner> ExistentialProjection<I> {\\n415:     pub fn new_from_args(\\n416:         interner: I,\\n417:         def_id: I::DefId,\\n418:         args: I::GenericArgs,\\n419:         term: I::Term,\\n420:     ) -> ExistentialProjection<I> {\\n421:         interner.debug_assert_existential_args_compatible(def_id, args);\\n422:         Self { def_id, args, term, use_existential_projection_new_instead: () }\\n423:     }\\n424: \\n425:     pub fn new(\\n426:         interner: I,\\n427:         def_id: I::DefId,\\n428:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\\n429:         term: I::Term,\\n430:     ) -> ExistentialProjection<I> {\\n431:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\\n432:         Self::new_from_args(interner, def_id, args, term)\\n433:     }\\n434: \\n435:     /// Extracts the underlying existential trait reference from this projection.\\n436:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `exists T. <T as Iterator>::Item == X`,\\n437:     /// then this function would return an `exists T. T: Iterator` existential trait\\n438:     /// reference.\\n439:     pub fn trait_ref(&self, interner: I) -> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\\n440:         let def_id = interner.parent(self.def_id);\\n441:         let args_count = interner.generics_of(def_id).count() - 1;\\n442:         let args = interner.mk_args(&self.args.as_slice()[..args_count]);\\n443:         ExistentialTraitRef::new_from_args(interner, def_id.try_into().unwrap(), args)\\n444:     }\\n445: \\n446:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\\n447:         // otherwise the escaping regions would be captured by the binders\\n448:         debug_assert!(!self_ty.has_escaping_bound_vars());\\n449: \\n450:         ProjectionPredicate {\\n451:             projection_term: AliasTerm::new(\\n452:                 interner,\\n453:                 self.def_id,\\n454:                 [self_ty.into()].iter().chain(self.args.iter()),\\n455:             ),\\n456:             term: self.term,\\n457:         }\\n458:     }\\n459: \\n460:     pub fn erase_self_ty(interner: I, projection_predicate: ProjectionPredicate<I>) -> Self {\\n461:         // Assert there is a Self.\\n462:         projection_predicate.projection_term.args.type_at(0);\\n463: \\n464:         Self {\\n465:             def_id: projection_predicate.projection_term.def_id,\\n466:             args: interner.mk_args(&projection_predicate.projection_term.args.as_slice()[1..]),\\n467:             term: projection_predicate.term,\\n468:             use_existential_projection_new_instead: (),\\n469:         }\\n470:     }\\n471: }\\n472: \\n473: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialProjection<I>> {\\n474:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ty::Binder<I, ProjectionPredicate<I>> {\\n475:         self.map_bound(|p| p.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty))\\n476:     }\\n477: \\n478:     pub fn item_def_id(&self) -> I::DefId {\\n479:         self.skip_binder().def_id\\n480:     }\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\\n484: #[cfg_attr(feature = \\\"nightly\\\", derive(Encodable, Decodable, HashStable_NoContext))]\\n485: pub enum AliasTermKind {\\n486:     /// A projection `<Type as Trait>::AssocType`.\\n487:     /// Can get normalized away if monomorphic enough.\\n488:     ProjectionTy,\\n489:     /// An associated type in an inherent `impl`\\n490:     InherentTy,\\n491:     /// An opaque type (usually from `impl Trait` in type aliases or function return types)\\n492:     /// Can only be normalized away in PostAnalysis mode or its defining scope.\\n493:     OpaqueTy,\\n494:     /// A free type alias that actually checks its trait bounds.\\n495:     /// Currently only used if the type alias references opaque types.\\n496:     /// Can always be normalized away.\\n497:     FreeTy,\\n498: \\n499:     /// An unevaluated anonymous constants.\\n500:     UnevaluatedConst,\\n501:     /// An unevaluated const coming from an associated const.\\n502:     ProjectionConst,\\n503:     /// A top level const item not part of a trait or impl.\\n504:     FreeConst,\\n505:     /// An associated const in an inherent `impl`\\n506:     InherentConst,\\n507: }\\n508: \\n509: impl AliasTermKind {\\n510:     pub fn descr(self) -> &'static str {\\n511:         match self {\\n512:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => \\\"associated type\\\",\\n513:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => \\\"associated const\\\",\\n514:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy => \\\"inherent associated type\\\",\\n515:             AliasTermKind::InherentConst => \\\"inherent associated const\\\",\\n516:             AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy => \\\"opaque type\\\",\\n517:             AliasTermKind::FreeTy => \\\"type alias\\\",\\n518:             AliasTermKind::FreeConst => \\\"unevaluated constant\\\",\\n519:             AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst => \\\"unevaluated constant\\\",\\n520:         }\\n521:     }\\n522: \\n523:     pub fn is_type(self) -> bool {\\n524:         match self {\\n525:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy\\n526:             | AliasTermKind::InherentTy\\n527:             | AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\\n528:             | AliasTermKind::FreeTy => true,\\n529: \\n530:             AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\\n531:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst\\n532:             | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\\n533:             | AliasTermKind::FreeConst => false,\\n534:         }\\n535:     }\\n536: }\\n537: \\n538: impl From<ty::AliasTyKind> for AliasTermKind {\\n539:     fn from(value: ty::AliasTyKind) -> Self {\\n540:         match value {\\n541:             ty::Projection => AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy,\\n542:             ty::Opaque => AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy,\\n543:             ty::Free => AliasTermKind::FreeTy,\\n544:             ty::Inherent => AliasTermKind::InherentTy,\\n545:         }\\n546:     }\\n547: }\\n548: \\n549: /// Represents the unprojected term of a projection goal.\\n550: ///\\n551: /// * For a projection, this would be `<Ty as Trait<...>>::N<...>`.\\n552: /// * For an inherent projection, this would be `Ty::N<...>`.\\n553: /// * For an opaque type, there is no explicit syntax.\\n554: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n555: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n556: #[cfg_attr(\\n557:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n558:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n559: )]\\n560: pub struct AliasTerm<I: Interner> {\\n561:     /// The parameters of the associated or opaque item.\\n562:     ///\\n563:     /// For a projection, these are the generic parameters for the trait and the\\n564:     /// GAT parameters, if there are any.\\n565:     ///\\n566:     /// For an inherent projection, they consist of the self type and the GAT parameters,\\n567:     /// if there are any.\\n568:     ///\\n569:     /// For RPIT the generic parameters are for the generics of the function,\\n570:     /// while for TAIT it is used for the generic parameters of the alias.\\n571:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\\n572: \\n573:     /// The `DefId` of the `TraitItem` or `ImplItem` for the associated type `N` depending on whether\\n574:     /// this is a projection or an inherent projection or the `DefId` of the `OpaqueType` item if\\n575:     /// this is an opaque.\\n576:     ///\\n577:     /// During codegen, `interner.type_of(def_id)` can be used to get the type of the\\n578:     /// underlying type if the type is an opaque.\\n579:     ///\\n580:     /// Note that if this is an associated type, this is not the `DefId` of the\\n581:     /// `TraitRef` containing this associated type, which is in `interner.associated_item(def_id).container`,\\n582:     /// aka. `interner.parent(def_id)`.\\n583:     pub def_id: I::DefId,\\n584: \\n585:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `AliasTerm` without using [`AliasTerm::new_from_args`].\\n586:     #[derive_where(skip(Debug))]\\n587:     _use_alias_term_new_instead: (),\\n588: }\\n589: \\n590: impl<I: Interner> Eq for AliasTerm<I> {}\\n591: \\n592: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\\n593:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, def_id: I::DefId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> AliasTerm<I> {\\n594:         interner.debug_assert_args_compatible(def_id, args);\\n595:         AliasTerm { def_id, args, _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\\n596:     }\\n597: \\n598:     pub fn new(\\n599:         interner: I,\\n600:         def_id: I::DefId,\\n601:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\\n602:     ) -> AliasTerm<I> {\\n603:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\\n604:         Self::new_from_args(interner, def_id, args)\\n605:     }\\n606: \\n607:     pub fn expect_ty(self, interner: I) -> ty::AliasTy<I> {\\n608:         match self.kind(interner) {\\n609:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy\\n610:             | AliasTermKind::InherentTy\\n611:             | AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\\n612:             | AliasTermKind::FreeTy => {}\\n613:             AliasTermKind::InherentConst\\n614:             | AliasTermKind::FreeConst\\n615:             | AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\\n616:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => {\\n617:                 panic!(\\\"Cannot turn `UnevaluatedConst` into `AliasTy`\\\")\\n618:             }\\n619:         }\\n620:         ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () }\\n621:     }\\n622: \\n623:     pub fn kind(self, interner: I) -> AliasTermKind {\\n624:         interner.alias_term_kind(self)\\n625:     }\\n626: \\n627:     pub fn to_term(self, interner: I) -> I::Term {\\n628:         match self.kind(interner) {\\n629:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => Ty::new_alias(\\n630:                 interner,\\n631:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Projection,\\n632:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\\n633:             )\\n634:             .into(),\\n635:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy => Ty::new_alias(\\n636:                 interner,\\n637:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Inherent,\\n638:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\\n639:             )\\n640:             .into(),\\n641:             AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy => Ty::new_alias(\\n642:                 interner,\\n643:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Opaque,\\n644:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\\n645:             )\\n646:             .into(),\\n647:             AliasTermKind::FreeTy => Ty::new_alias(\\n648:                 interner,\\n649:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Free,\\n650:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\\n651:             )\\n652:             .into(),\\n653:             AliasTermKind::FreeConst\\n654:             | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\\n655:             | AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\\n656:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => I::Const::new_unevaluated(\\n657:                 interner,\\n658:                 ty::UnevaluatedConst::new(self.def_id.try_into().unwrap(), self.args),\\n659:             )\\n660:             .into(),\\n661:         }\\n662:     }\\n663: }\\n664: \\n665: /// The following methods work only with (trait) associated term projections.\\n666: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\\n667:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\\n668:         self.args.type_at(0)\\n669:     }\\n670: \\n671:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\\n672:         AliasTerm::new(\\n673:             interner,\\n674:             self.def_id,\\n675:             [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)),\\n676:         )\\n677:     }\\n678: \\n679:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\\n680:         assert!(\\n681:             matches!(\\n682:                 self.kind(interner),\\n683:                 AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst\\n684:             ),\\n685:             \\\"expected a projection\\\"\\n686:         );\\n687:         interner.parent(self.def_id).try_into().unwrap()\\n688:     }\\n689: \\n690:     /// Extracts the underlying trait reference and own args from this projection.\\n691:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as StreamingIterator>::Item<'a>`,\\n692:     /// then this function would return a `T: StreamingIterator` trait reference and\\n693:     /// `['a]` as the own args.\\n694:     pub fn trait_ref_and_own_args(self, interner: I) -> (TraitRef<I>, I::GenericArgsSlice) {\\n695:         interner.trait_ref_and_own_args_for_alias(self.def_id, self.args)\\n696:     }\\n697: \\n698:     /// Extracts the underlying trait reference from this projection.\\n699:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as Iterator>::Item`,\\n700:     /// then this function would return a `T: Iterator` trait reference.\\n701:     ///\\n702:     /// WARNING: This will drop the args for generic associated types\\n703:     /// consider calling [Self::trait_ref_and_own_args] to get those\\n704:     /// as well.\\n705:     pub fn trait_ref(self, interner: I) -> TraitRef<I> {\\n706:         self.trait_ref_and_own_args(interner).0\\n707:     }\\n708: \\n709:     /// Extract the own args from this projection.\\n710:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as StreamingIterator>::Item<'a>`,\\n711:     /// then this function would return the slice `['a]` as the own args.\\n712:     pub fn own_args(self, interner: I) -> I::GenericArgsSlice {\\n713:         self.trait_ref_and_own_args(interner).1\\n714:     }\\n715: }\\n716: \\n717: /// The following methods work only with inherent associated term projections.\\n718: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\\n719:     /// Transform the generic parameters to have the given `impl` args as the base and the GAT args on top of that.\\n720:     ///\\n721:     /// Does the following transformation:\\n722:     ///\\n723:     /// ```text\\n724:     /// [Self, P_0...P_m] -> [I_0...I_n, P_0...P_m]\\n725:     ///\\n726:     ///     I_i impl args\\n727:     ///     P_j GAT args\\n728:     /// ```\\n729:     pub fn rebase_inherent_args_onto_impl(\\n730:         self,\\n731:         impl_args: I::GenericArgs,\\n732:         interner: I,\\n733:     ) -> I::GenericArgs {\\n734:         debug_assert!(matches!(\\n735:             self.kind(interner),\\n736:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\\n737:         ));\\n738:         interner.mk_args_from_iter(impl_args.iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)))\\n739:     }\\n740: }\\n741: \\n742: impl<I: Interner> From<ty::AliasTy<I>> for AliasTerm<I> {\\n743:     fn from(ty: ty::AliasTy<I>) -> Self {\\n744:         AliasTerm { args: ty.args, def_id: ty.def_id, _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\\n745:     }\\n746: }\\n747: \\n748: impl<I: Interner> From<ty::UnevaluatedConst<I>> for AliasTerm<I> {\\n749:     fn from(ct: ty::UnevaluatedConst<I>) -> Self {\\n750:         AliasTerm { args: ct.args, def_id: ct.def.into(), _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\\n751:     }\\n752: }\\n753: \\n754: /// This kind of predicate has no *direct* correspondent in the\\n755: /// syntax, but it roughly corresponds to the syntactic forms:\\n756: ///\\n757: /// 1. `T: TraitRef<..., Item = Type>`\\n758: /// 2. `<T as TraitRef<...>>::Item == Type` (NYI)\\n759: ///\\n760: /// In particular, form #1 is \\\"desugared\\\" to the combination of a\\n761: /// normal trait predicate (`T: TraitRef<...>`) and one of these\\n762: /// predicates. Form #2 is a broader form in that it also permits\\n763: /// equality between arbitrary types. Processing an instance of\\n764: /// Form #2 eventually yields one of these `ProjectionPredicate`\\n765: /// instances to normalize the LHS.\\n766: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\\n767: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n768: #[cfg_attr(\\n769:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n770:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n771: )]\\n772: pub struct ProjectionPredicate<I: Interner> {\\n773:     pub projection_term: AliasTerm<I>,\\n774:     pub term: I::Term,\\n775: }\\n776: \\n777: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ProjectionPredicate<I> {}\\n778: \\n779: impl<I: Interner> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\\n780:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\\n781:         self.projection_term.self_ty()\\n782:     }\\n783: \\n784:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\\n785:         Self {\\n786:             projection_term: self.projection_term.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty),\\n787:             ..self\\n788:         }\\n789:     }\\n790: \\n791:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\\n792:         self.projection_term.trait_def_id(interner)\\n793:     }\\n794: \\n795:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::DefId {\\n796:         self.projection_term.def_id\\n797:     }\\n798: }\\n799: \\n800: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ProjectionPredicate<I>> {\\n801:     /// Returns the `DefId` of the trait of the associated item being projected.\\n802:     #[inline]\\n803:     pub fn trait_def_id(&self, cx: I) -> I::TraitId {\\n804:         self.skip_binder().projection_term.trait_def_id(cx)\\n805:     }\\n806: \\n807:     pub fn term(&self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Term> {\\n808:         self.map_bound(|predicate| predicate.term)\\n809:     }\\n810: \\n811:     /// The `DefId` of the `TraitItem` for the associated type.\\n812:     ///\\n813:     /// Note that this is not the `DefId` of the `TraitRef` containing this\\n814:     /// associated type, which is in `tcx.associated_item(projection_def_id()).container`.\\n815:     pub fn item_def_id(&self) -> I::DefId {\\n816:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\\n817:         self.skip_binder().projection_term.def_id\\n818:     }\\n819: }\\n820: \\n821: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for ProjectionPredicate<I> {\\n822:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n823:         write!(f, \\\"ProjectionPredicate({:?}, {:?})\\\", self.projection_term, self.term)\\n824:     }\\n825: }\\n826: \\n827: /// Used by the new solver to normalize an alias. This always expects the `term` to\\n828: /// be an unconstrained inference variable which is used as the output.\\n829: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\\n830: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n831: #[cfg_attr(\\n832:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n833:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n834: )]\\n835: pub struct NormalizesTo<I: Interner> {\\n836:     pub alias: AliasTerm<I>,\\n837:     pub term: I::Term,\\n838: }\\n839: \\n840: impl<I: Interner> Eq for NormalizesTo<I> {}\\n841: \\n842: impl<I: Interner> NormalizesTo<I> {\\n843:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\\n844:         self.alias.self_ty()\\n845:     }\\n846: \\n847:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> NormalizesTo<I> {\\n848:         Self { alias: self.alias.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty), ..self }\\n849:     }\\n850: \\n851:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\\n852:         self.alias.trait_def_id(interner)\\n853:     }\\n854: \\n855:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::DefId {\\n856:         self.alias.def_id\\n857:     }\\n858: }\\n859: \\n860: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for NormalizesTo<I> {\\n861:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n862:         write!(f, \\\"NormalizesTo({:?}, {:?})\\\", self.alias, self.term)\\n863:     }\\n864: }\\n865: \\n866: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n867: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n868: #[cfg_attr(\\n869:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n870:     derive(Encodable_NoContext, Decodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n871: )]\\n872: pub struct HostEffectPredicate<I: Interner> {\\n873:     pub trait_ref: ty::TraitRef<I>,\\n874:     pub constness: BoundConstness,\\n875: }\\n876: \\n877: impl<I: Interner> Eq for HostEffectPredicate<I> {}\\n878: \\n879: impl<I: Interner> HostEffectPredicate<I> {\\n880:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\\n881:         self.trait_ref.self_ty()\\n882:     }\\n883: \\n884:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\\n885:         Self { trait_ref: self.trait_ref.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty), ..self }\\n886:     }\\n887: \\n888:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\\n889:         self.trait_ref.def_id\\n890:     }\\n891: }\\n892: \\n893: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, HostEffectPredicate<I>> {\\n894:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\\n895:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\\n896:         self.skip_binder().def_id()\\n897:     }\\n898: \\n899:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\\n900:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.self_ty())\\n901:     }\\n902: \\n903:     #[inline]\\n904:     pub fn constness(self) -> BoundConstness {\\n905:         self.skip_binder().constness\\n906:     }\\n907: }\\n908: \\n909: /// Encodes that `a` must be a subtype of `b`. The `a_is_expected` flag indicates\\n910: /// whether the `a` type is the type that we should label as \\\"expected\\\" when\\n911: /// presenting user diagnostics.\\n912: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n913: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n914: #[cfg_attr(\\n915:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n916:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n917: )]\\n918: pub struct SubtypePredicate<I: Interner> {\\n919:     pub a_is_expected: bool,\\n920:     pub a: I::Ty,\\n921:     pub b: I::Ty,\\n922: }\\n923: \\n924: impl<I: Interner> Eq for SubtypePredicate<I> {}\\n925: \\n926: /// Encodes that we have to coerce *from* the `a` type to the `b` type.\\n927: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\\n928: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\\n929: #[cfg_attr(\\n930:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n931:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n932: )]\\n933: pub struct CoercePredicate<I: Interner> {\\n934:     pub a: I::Ty,\\n935:     pub b: I::Ty,\\n936: }\\n937: \\n938: impl<I: Interner> Eq for CoercePredicate<I> {}\\n939: \\n940: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]\\n941: #[cfg_attr(\\n942:     feature = \\\"nightly\\\",\\n943:     derive(Encodable_NoContext, Decodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\\n944: )]\\n945: pub enum BoundConstness {\\n946:     /// `Type: const Trait`\\n947:     ///\\n948:     /// A bound is required to be unconditionally const, even in a runtime function.\\n949:     Const,\\n950:     /// `Type: [const] Trait`\\n951:     ///\\n952:     /// Requires resolving to const only when we are in a const context.\\n953:     Maybe,\\n954: }\\n955: \\n956: impl BoundConstness {\\n957:     pub fn satisfies(self, goal: BoundConstness) -> bool {\\n958:         match (self, goal) {\\n959:             (BoundConstness::Const, BoundConstness::Const | BoundConstness::Maybe) => true,\\n960:             (BoundConstness::Maybe, BoundConstness::Maybe) => true,\\n961:             (BoundConstness::Maybe, BoundConstness::Const) => false,\\n962:         }\\n963:     }\\n964: \\n965:     pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {\\n966:         match self {\\n967:             Self::Const => \\\"const\\\",\\n968:             Self::Maybe => \\\"[const]\\\",\\n969:         }\\n970:     }\\n971: }\\n972: \\n973: impl fmt::Display for BoundConstness {\\n974:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n975:         match self {\\n976:             Self::Const => f.write_str(\\\"const\\\"),\\n977:             Self::Maybe => f.write_str(\\\"[const]\\\"),\\n978:         }\\n979:     }\\n980: }\\n981: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: use std::fmt;\n2: use std::hash::Hash;\n3: \n4: use derive_where::derive_where;\n5: #[cfg(feature = \"nightly\")]\n6: use rustc_macros::{\n7:     Decodable, Decodable_NoContext, Encodable, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext,\n8: };\n9: use rustc_type_ir_macros::{Lift_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, TypeVisitable_Generic};\n10: \n11: use crate::inherent::*;\n12: use crate::lift::Lift;\n13: use crate::upcast::{Upcast, UpcastFrom};\n14: use crate::visit::TypeVisitableExt as _;\n15: use crate::{self as ty, Interner};\n16: \n17: /// `A: 'region`\n18: #[derive_where(Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner, A)]\n19: #[derive_where(Copy; I: Interner, A: Copy)]\n20: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic)]\n21: #[cfg_attr(\n22:     feature = \"nightly\",\n23:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n24: )]\n25: pub struct OutlivesPredicate<I: Interner, A>(pub A, pub I::Region);\n26: \n27: impl<I: Interner, A: Eq> Eq for OutlivesPredicate<I, A> {}\n28: \n29: // FIXME: We manually derive `Lift` because the `derive(Lift_Generic)` doesn't\n30: // understand how to turn `A` to `A::Lifted` in the output `type Lifted`.\n31: impl<I: Interner, U: Interner, A> Lift<U> for OutlivesPredicate<I, A>\n32: where\n33:     A: Lift<U>,\n34:     I::Region: Lift<U, Lifted = U::Region>,\n35: {\n36:     type Lifted = OutlivesPredicate<U, A::Lifted>;\n37: \n38:     fn lift_to_interner(self, cx: U) -> Option<Self::Lifted> {\n39:         Some(OutlivesPredicate(self.0.lift_to_interner(cx)?, self.1.lift_to_interner(cx)?))\n40:     }\n41: }\n42: \n43: /// A complete reference to a trait. These take numerous guises in syntax,\n44: /// but perhaps the most recognizable form is in a where-clause:\n45: /// ```ignore (illustrative)\n46: /// T: Foo<U>\n47: /// ```\n48: /// This would be represented by a trait-reference where the `DefId` is the\n49: /// `DefId` for the trait `Foo` and the args define `T` as parameter 0,\n50: /// and `U` as parameter 1.\n51: ///\n52: /// Trait references also appear in object types like `Foo<U>`, but in\n53: /// that case the `Self` parameter is absent from the generic parameters.\n54: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\n55: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n56: #[cfg_attr(\n57:     feature = \"nightly\",\n58:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n59: )]\n60: pub struct TraitRef<I: Interner> {\n61:     pub def_id: I::TraitId,\n62:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\n63:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `TraitRef` without\n64:     /// calling [`TraitRef::new_from_args`].\n65:     _use_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\n66: }\n67: \n68: impl<I: Interner> Eq for TraitRef<I> {}\n69: \n70: impl<I: Interner> TraitRef<I> {\n71:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, trait_def_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> Self {\n72:         interner.debug_assert_args_compatible(trait_def_id.into(), args);\n73:         Self { def_id: trait_def_id, args, _use_trait_ref_new_instead: () }\n74:     }\n75: \n76:     pub fn new(\n77:         interner: I,\n78:         trait_def_id: I::TraitId,\n79:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\n80:     ) -> Self {\n81:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\n82:         Self::new_from_args(interner, trait_def_id, args)\n83:     }\n84: \n85:     pub fn from_assoc(interner: I, trait_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> TraitRef<I> {\n86:         let generics = interner.generics_of(trait_id.into());\n87:         TraitRef::new(interner, trait_id, args.iter().take(generics.count()))\n88:     }\n89: \n90:     /// Returns a `TraitRef` of the form `P0: Foo<P1..Pn>` where `Pi`\n91:     /// are the parameters defined on trait.\n92:     pub fn identity(interner: I, def_id: I::TraitId) -> TraitRef<I> {\n93:         TraitRef::new_from_args(\n94:             interner,\n95:             def_id,\n96:             I::GenericArgs::identity_for_item(interner, def_id.into()),\n97:         )\n98:     }\n99: \n100:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\n101:         TraitRef::new(\n102:             interner,\n103:             self.def_id,\n104:             [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)),\n105:         )\n106:     }\n107: \n108:     #[inline]\n109:     pub fn self_ty(&self) -> I::Ty {\n110:         self.args.type_at(0)\n111:     }\n112: }\n113: \n114: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>> {\n115:     pub fn self_ty(&self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\n116:         self.map_bound_ref(|tr| tr.self_ty())\n117:     }\n118: \n119:     pub fn def_id(&self) -> I::TraitId {\n120:         self.skip_binder().def_id\n121:     }\n122: \n123:     pub fn to_host_effect_clause(self, cx: I, constness: BoundConstness) -> I::Clause {\n124:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| {\n125:             ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(HostEffectPredicate { trait_ref, constness })\n126:         })\n127:         .upcast(cx)\n128:     }\n129: }\n130: \n131: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\n132: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n133: #[cfg_attr(\n134:     feature = \"nightly\",\n135:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n136: )]\n137: pub struct TraitPredicate<I: Interner> {\n138:     pub trait_ref: TraitRef<I>,\n139: \n140:     /// If polarity is Positive: we are proving that the trait is implemented.\n141:     ///\n142:     /// If polarity is Negative: we are proving that a negative impl of this trait\n143:     /// exists. (Note that coherence also checks whether negative impls of supertraits\n144:     /// exist via a series of predicates.)\n145:     pub polarity: PredicatePolarity,\n146: }\n147: \n148: impl<I: Interner> Eq for TraitPredicate<I> {}\n149: \n150: impl<I: Interner> TraitPredicate<I> {\n151:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\n152:         Self {\n153:             trait_ref: self.trait_ref.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty),\n154:             polarity: self.polarity,\n155:         }\n156:     }\n157: \n158:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\n159:         self.trait_ref.def_id\n160:     }\n161: \n162:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\n163:         self.trait_ref.self_ty()\n164:     }\n165: }\n166: \n167: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, TraitPredicate<I>> {\n168:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\n169:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\n170:         self.skip_binder().def_id()\n171:     }\n172: \n173:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\n174:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.self_ty())\n175:     }\n176: \n177:     #[inline]\n178:     pub fn polarity(self) -> PredicatePolarity {\n179:         self.skip_binder().polarity\n180:     }\n181: }\n182: \n183: impl<I: Interner> UpcastFrom<I, TraitRef<I>> for TraitPredicate<I> {\n184:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<I>, _tcx: I) -> Self {\n185:         TraitPredicate { trait_ref: from, polarity: PredicatePolarity::Positive }\n186:     }\n187: }\n188: \n189: impl<I: Interner> UpcastFrom<I, ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>>> for ty::Binder<I, TraitPredicate<I>> {\n190:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>>, _tcx: I) -> Self {\n191:         from.map_bound(|trait_ref| TraitPredicate {\n192:             trait_ref,\n193:             polarity: PredicatePolarity::Positive,\n194:         })\n195:     }\n196: }\n197: \n198: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for TraitPredicate<I> {\n199:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n200:         write!(f, \"TraitPredicate({:?}, polarity:{:?})\", self.trait_ref, self.polarity)\n201:     }\n202: }\n203: \n204: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\n205: #[cfg_attr(\n206:     feature = \"nightly\",\n207:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n208: )]\n209: pub enum ImplPolarity {\n210:     /// `impl Trait for Type`\n211:     Positive,\n212:     /// `impl !Trait for Type`\n213:     Negative,\n214:     /// `#[rustc_reservation_impl] impl Trait for Type`\n215:     ///\n216:     /// This is a \"stability hack\", not a real Rust feature.\n217:     /// See #64631 for details.\n218:     Reservation,\n219: }\n220: \n221: impl fmt::Display for ImplPolarity {\n222:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n223:         match self {\n224:             Self::Positive => f.write_str(\"positive\"),\n225:             Self::Negative => f.write_str(\"negative\"),\n226:             Self::Reservation => f.write_str(\"reservation\"),\n227:         }\n228:     }\n229: }\n230: \n231: impl ImplPolarity {\n232:     /// The polarity marker in front of the impl trait ref if applicable.\n233:     pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {\n234:         match self {\n235:             Self::Positive => \"\",\n236:             Self::Negative => \"!\",\n237:             Self::Reservation => \"\",\n238:         }\n239:     }\n240: }\n241: \n242: /// Polarity for a trait predicate. May either be negative or positive.\n243: /// Distinguished from [`ImplPolarity`] since we never compute goals with\n244: /// \"reservation\" level.\n245: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\n246: #[cfg_attr(\n247:     feature = \"nightly\",\n248:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n249: )]\n250: pub enum PredicatePolarity {\n251:     /// `Type: Trait`\n252:     Positive,\n253:     /// `Type: !Trait`\n254:     Negative,\n255: }\n256: \n257: impl PredicatePolarity {\n258:     /// Flips polarity by turning `Positive` into `Negative` and `Negative` into `Positive`.\n259:     pub fn flip(&self) -> PredicatePolarity {\n260:         match self {\n261:             PredicatePolarity::Positive => PredicatePolarity::Negative,\n262:             PredicatePolarity::Negative => PredicatePolarity::Positive,\n263:         }\n264:     }\n265: }\n266: \n267: impl fmt::Display for PredicatePolarity {\n268:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n269:         match self {\n270:             Self::Positive => f.write_str(\"positive\"),\n271:             Self::Negative => f.write_str(\"negative\"),\n272:         }\n273:     }\n274: }\n275: \n276: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n277: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n278: #[cfg_attr(\n279:     feature = \"nightly\",\n280:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n281: )]\n282: pub enum ExistentialPredicate<I: Interner> {\n283:     /// E.g., `Iterator`.\n284:     Trait(ExistentialTraitRef<I>),\n285:     /// E.g., `Iterator::Item = T`.\n286:     Projection(ExistentialProjection<I>),\n287:     /// E.g., `Send`.\n288:     AutoTrait(I::TraitId),\n289: }\n290: \n291: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialPredicate<I> {}\n292: \n293: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialPredicate<I>> {\n294:     /// Given an existential predicate like `?Self: PartialEq<u32>` (e.g., derived from `dyn PartialEq<u32>`),\n295:     /// and a concrete type `self_ty`, returns a full predicate where the existentially quantified variable `?Self`\n296:     /// has been replaced with `self_ty` (e.g., `self_ty: PartialEq<u32>`, in our example).\n297:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> I::Clause {\n298:         match self.skip_binder() {\n299:             ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => self.rebind(tr).with_self_ty(cx, self_ty).upcast(cx),\n300:             ExistentialPredicate::Projection(p) => {\n301:                 self.rebind(p.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty)).upcast(cx)\n302:             }\n303:             ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => {\n304:                 let generics = cx.generics_of(did.into());\n305:                 let trait_ref = if generics.count() == 1 {\n306:                     ty::TraitRef::new(cx, did, [self_ty])\n307:                 } else {\n308:                     // If this is an ill-formed auto trait, then synthesize\n309:                     // new error args for the missing generics.\n310:                     let err_args =\n311:                         GenericArgs::extend_with_error(cx, did.into(), &[self_ty.into()]);\n312:                     ty::TraitRef::new_from_args(cx, did, err_args)\n313:                 };\n314:                 self.rebind(trait_ref).upcast(cx)\n315:             }\n316:         }\n317:     }\n318: }\n319: \n320: /// An existential reference to a trait, where `Self` is erased.\n321: /// For example, the trait object `Trait<'a, 'b, X, Y>` is:\n322: /// ```ignore (illustrative)\n323: /// exists T. T: Trait<'a, 'b, X, Y>\n324: /// ```\n325: /// The generic parameters don't include the erased `Self`, only trait\n326: /// type and lifetime parameters (`[X, Y]` and `['a, 'b]` above).\n327: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\n328: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n329: #[cfg_attr(\n330:     feature = \"nightly\",\n331:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n332: )]\n333: pub struct ExistentialTraitRef<I: Interner> {\n334:     pub def_id: I::TraitId,\n335:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\n336:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `ExistentialTraitRef` without\n337:     /// calling [`ExistentialTraitRef::new_from_args`].\n338:     _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\n339: }\n340: \n341: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialTraitRef<I> {}\n342: \n343: impl<I: Interner> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\n344:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, trait_def_id: I::TraitId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> Self {\n345:         interner.debug_assert_existential_args_compatible(trait_def_id.into(), args);\n346:         Self { def_id: trait_def_id, args, _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: () }\n347:     }\n348: \n349:     pub fn new(\n350:         interner: I,\n351:         trait_def_id: I::TraitId,\n352:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\n353:     ) -> Self {\n354:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\n355:         Self::new_from_args(interner, trait_def_id, args)\n356:     }\n357: \n358:     pub fn erase_self_ty(interner: I, trait_ref: TraitRef<I>) -> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\n359:         // Assert there is a Self.\n360:         trait_ref.args.type_at(0);\n361: \n362:         ExistentialTraitRef {\n363:             def_id: trait_ref.def_id,\n364:             args: interner.mk_args(&trait_ref.args.as_slice()[1..]),\n365:             _use_existential_trait_ref_new_instead: (),\n366:         }\n367:     }\n368: \n369:     /// Object types don't have a self type specified. Therefore, when\n370:     /// we convert the principal trait-ref into a normal trait-ref,\n371:     /// you must give *some* self type. A common choice is `mk_err()`\n372:     /// or some placeholder type.\n373:     pub fn with_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> TraitRef<I> {\n374:         // otherwise the escaping vars would be captured by the binder\n375:         // debug_assert!(!self_ty.has_escaping_bound_vars());\n376: \n377:         TraitRef::new(interner, self.def_id, [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter()))\n378:     }\n379: }\n380: \n381: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialTraitRef<I>> {\n382:     pub fn def_id(&self) -> I::TraitId {\n383:         self.skip_binder().def_id\n384:     }\n385: \n386:     /// Object types don't have a self type specified. Therefore, when\n387:     /// we convert the principal trait-ref into a normal trait-ref,\n388:     /// you must give *some* self type. A common choice is `mk_err()`\n389:     /// or some placeholder type.\n390:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ty::Binder<I, TraitRef<I>> {\n391:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty))\n392:     }\n393: }\n394: \n395: /// A `ProjectionPredicate` for an `ExistentialTraitRef`.\n396: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n397: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n398: #[cfg_attr(\n399:     feature = \"nightly\",\n400:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n401: )]\n402: pub struct ExistentialProjection<I: Interner> {\n403:     pub def_id: I::DefId,\n404:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\n405:     pub term: I::Term,\n406: \n407:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `ExistentialProjection`\n408:     /// without using [`ExistentialProjection::new_from_args`].\n409:     use_existential_projection_new_instead: (),\n410: }\n411: \n412: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ExistentialProjection<I> {}\n413: \n414: impl<I: Interner> ExistentialProjection<I> {\n415:     pub fn new_from_args(\n416:         interner: I,\n417:         def_id: I::DefId,\n418:         args: I::GenericArgs,\n419:         term: I::Term,\n420:     ) -> ExistentialProjection<I> {\n421:         interner.debug_assert_existential_args_compatible(def_id, args);\n422:         Self { def_id, args, term, use_existential_projection_new_instead: () }\n423:     }\n424: \n425:     pub fn new(\n426:         interner: I,\n427:         def_id: I::DefId,\n428:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\n429:         term: I::Term,\n430:     ) -> ExistentialProjection<I> {\n431:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\n432:         Self::new_from_args(interner, def_id, args, term)\n433:     }\n434: \n435:     /// Extracts the underlying existential trait reference from this projection.\n436:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `exists T. <T as Iterator>::Item == X`,\n437:     /// then this function would return an `exists T. T: Iterator` existential trait\n438:     /// reference.\n439:     pub fn trait_ref(&self, interner: I) -> ExistentialTraitRef<I> {\n440:         let def_id = interner.parent(self.def_id);\n441:         let args_count = interner.generics_of(def_id).count() - 1;\n442:         let args = interner.mk_args(&self.args.as_slice()[..args_count]);\n443:         ExistentialTraitRef::new_from_args(interner, def_id.try_into().unwrap(), args)\n444:     }\n445: \n446:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\n447:         // otherwise the escaping regions would be captured by the binders\n448:         debug_assert!(!self_ty.has_escaping_bound_vars());\n449: \n450:         ProjectionPredicate {\n451:             projection_term: AliasTerm::new(\n452:                 interner,\n453:                 self.def_id,\n454:                 [self_ty.into()].iter().chain(self.args.iter()),\n455:             ),\n456:             term: self.term,\n457:         }\n458:     }\n459: \n460:     pub fn erase_self_ty(interner: I, projection_predicate: ProjectionPredicate<I>) -> Self {\n461:         // Assert there is a Self.\n462:         projection_predicate.projection_term.args.type_at(0);\n463: \n464:         Self {\n465:             def_id: projection_predicate.projection_term.def_id,\n466:             args: interner.mk_args(&projection_predicate.projection_term.args.as_slice()[1..]),\n467:             term: projection_predicate.term,\n468:             use_existential_projection_new_instead: (),\n469:         }\n470:     }\n471: }\n472: \n473: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ExistentialProjection<I>> {\n474:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, cx: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ty::Binder<I, ProjectionPredicate<I>> {\n475:         self.map_bound(|p| p.with_self_ty(cx, self_ty))\n476:     }\n477: \n478:     pub fn item_def_id(&self) -> I::DefId {\n479:         self.skip_binder().def_id\n480:     }\n481: }\n482: \n483: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Debug)]\n484: #[cfg_attr(feature = \"nightly\", derive(Encodable, Decodable, HashStable_NoContext))]\n485: pub enum AliasTermKind {\n486:     /// A projection `<Type as Trait>::AssocType`.\n487:     /// Can get normalized away if monomorphic enough.\n488:     ProjectionTy,\n489:     /// An associated type in an inherent `impl`\n490:     InherentTy,\n491:     /// An opaque type (usually from `impl Trait` in type aliases or function return types)\n492:     /// Can only be normalized away in PostAnalysis mode or its defining scope.\n493:     OpaqueTy,\n494:     /// A free type alias that actually checks its trait bounds.\n495:     /// Currently only used if the type alias references opaque types.\n496:     /// Can always be normalized away.\n497:     FreeTy,\n498: \n499:     /// An unevaluated anonymous constants.\n500:     UnevaluatedConst,\n501:     /// An unevaluated const coming from an associated const.\n502:     ProjectionConst,\n503:     /// A top level const item not part of a trait or impl.\n504:     FreeConst,\n505:     /// An associated const in an inherent `impl`\n506:     InherentConst,\n507: }\n508: \n509: impl AliasTermKind {\n510:     pub fn descr(self) -> &'static str {\n511:         match self {\n512:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => \"associated type\",\n513:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => \"associated const\",\n514:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy => \"inherent associated type\",\n515:             AliasTermKind::InherentConst => \"inherent associated const\",\n516:             AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy => \"opaque type\",\n517:             AliasTermKind::FreeTy => \"type alias\",\n518:             AliasTermKind::FreeConst => \"unevaluated constant\",\n519:             AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst => \"unevaluated constant\",\n520:         }\n521:     }\n522: \n523:     pub fn is_type(self) -> bool {\n524:         match self {\n525:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy\n526:             | AliasTermKind::InherentTy\n527:             | AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\n528:             | AliasTermKind::FreeTy => true,\n529: \n530:             AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\n531:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst\n532:             | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\n533:             | AliasTermKind::FreeConst => false,\n534:         }\n535:     }\n536: }\n537: \n538: impl From<ty::AliasTyKind> for AliasTermKind {\n539:     fn from(value: ty::AliasTyKind) -> Self {\n540:         match value {\n541:             ty::Projection => AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy,\n542:             ty::Opaque => AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy,\n543:             ty::Free => AliasTermKind::FreeTy,\n544:             ty::Inherent => AliasTermKind::InherentTy,\n545:         }\n546:     }\n547: }\n548: \n549: /// Represents the unprojected term of a projection goal.\n550: ///\n551: /// * For a projection, this would be `<Ty as Trait<...>>::N<...>`.\n552: /// * For an inherent projection, this would be `Ty::N<...>`.\n553: /// * For an opaque type, there is no explicit syntax.\n554: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n555: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n556: #[cfg_attr(\n557:     feature = \"nightly\",\n558:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n559: )]\n560: pub struct AliasTerm<I: Interner> {\n561:     /// The parameters of the associated or opaque item.\n562:     ///\n563:     /// For a projection, these are the generic parameters for the trait and the\n564:     /// GAT parameters, if there are any.\n565:     ///\n566:     /// For an inherent projection, they consist of the self type and the GAT parameters,\n567:     /// if there are any.\n568:     ///\n569:     /// For RPIT the generic parameters are for the generics of the function,\n570:     /// while for TAIT it is used for the generic parameters of the alias.\n571:     pub args: I::GenericArgs,\n572: \n573:     /// The `DefId` of the `TraitItem` or `ImplItem` for the associated type `N` depending on whether\n574:     /// this is a projection or an inherent projection or the `DefId` of the `OpaqueType` item if\n575:     /// this is an opaque.\n576:     ///\n577:     /// During codegen, `interner.type_of(def_id)` can be used to get the type of the\n578:     /// underlying type if the type is an opaque.\n579:     ///\n580:     /// Note that if this is an associated type, this is not the `DefId` of the\n581:     /// `TraitRef` containing this associated type, which is in `interner.associated_item(def_id).container`,\n582:     /// aka. `interner.parent(def_id)`.\n583:     pub def_id: I::DefId,\n584: \n585:     /// This field exists to prevent the creation of `AliasTerm` without using [`AliasTerm::new_from_args`].\n586:     #[derive_where(skip(Debug))]\n587:     _use_alias_term_new_instead: (),\n588: }\n589: \n590: impl<I: Interner> Eq for AliasTerm<I> {}\n591: \n592: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\n593:     pub fn new_from_args(interner: I, def_id: I::DefId, args: I::GenericArgs) -> AliasTerm<I> {\n594:         interner.debug_assert_args_compatible(def_id, args);\n595:         AliasTerm { def_id, args, _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\n596:     }\n597: \n598:     pub fn new(\n599:         interner: I,\n600:         def_id: I::DefId,\n601:         args: impl IntoIterator<Item: Into<I::GenericArg>>,\n602:     ) -> AliasTerm<I> {\n603:         let args = interner.mk_args_from_iter(args.into_iter().map(Into::into));\n604:         Self::new_from_args(interner, def_id, args)\n605:     }\n606: \n607:     pub fn expect_ty(self, interner: I) -> ty::AliasTy<I> {\n608:         match self.kind(interner) {\n609:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy\n610:             | AliasTermKind::InherentTy\n611:             | AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\n612:             | AliasTermKind::FreeTy => {}\n613:             AliasTermKind::InherentConst\n614:             | AliasTermKind::FreeConst\n615:             | AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\n616:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => {\n617:                 panic!(\"Cannot turn `UnevaluatedConst` into `AliasTy`\")\n618:             }\n619:         }\n620:         ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () }\n621:     }\n622: \n623:     pub fn kind(self, interner: I) -> AliasTermKind {\n624:         interner.alias_term_kind(self)\n625:     }\n626: \n627:     pub fn to_term(self, interner: I) -> I::Term {\n628:         match self.kind(interner) {\n629:             AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => Ty::new_alias(\n630:                 interner,\n631:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Projection,\n632:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\n633:             )\n634:             .into(),\n635:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy => Ty::new_alias(\n636:                 interner,\n637:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Inherent,\n638:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\n639:             )\n640:             .into(),\n641:             AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy => Ty::new_alias(\n642:                 interner,\n643:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Opaque,\n644:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\n645:             )\n646:             .into(),\n647:             AliasTermKind::FreeTy => Ty::new_alias(\n648:                 interner,\n649:                 ty::AliasTyKind::Free,\n650:                 ty::AliasTy { def_id: self.def_id, args: self.args, _use_alias_ty_new_instead: () },\n651:             )\n652:             .into(),\n653:             AliasTermKind::FreeConst\n654:             | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\n655:             | AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\n656:             | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => I::Const::new_unevaluated(\n657:                 interner,\n658:                 ty::UnevaluatedConst::new(self.def_id.try_into().unwrap(), self.args),\n659:             )\n660:             .into(),\n661:         }\n662:     }\n663: }\n664: \n665: /// The following methods work only with (trait) associated term projections.\n666: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\n667:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\n668:         self.args.type_at(0)\n669:     }\n670: \n671:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\n672:         AliasTerm::new(\n673:             interner,\n674:             self.def_id,\n675:             [self_ty.into()].into_iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)),\n676:         )\n677:     }\n678: \n679:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\n680:         assert!(\n681:             matches!(\n682:                 self.kind(interner),\n683:                 AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy | AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst\n684:             ),\n685:             \"expected a projection\"\n686:         );\n687:         interner.parent(self.def_id).try_into().unwrap()\n688:     }\n689: \n690:     /// Extracts the underlying trait reference and own args from this projection.\n691:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as StreamingIterator>::Item<'a>`,\n692:     /// then this function would return a `T: StreamingIterator` trait reference and\n693:     /// `['a]` as the own args.\n694:     pub fn trait_ref_and_own_args(self, interner: I) -> (TraitRef<I>, I::GenericArgsSlice) {\n695:         interner.trait_ref_and_own_args_for_alias(self.def_id, self.args)\n696:     }\n697: \n698:     /// Extracts the underlying trait reference from this projection.\n699:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as Iterator>::Item`,\n700:     /// then this function would return a `T: Iterator` trait reference.\n701:     ///\n702:     /// WARNING: This will drop the args for generic associated types\n703:     /// consider calling [Self::trait_ref_and_own_args] to get those\n704:     /// as well.\n705:     pub fn trait_ref(self, interner: I) -> TraitRef<I> {\n706:         self.trait_ref_and_own_args(interner).0\n707:     }\n708: \n709:     /// Extract the own args from this projection.\n710:     /// For example, if this is a projection of `<T as StreamingIterator>::Item<'a>`,\n711:     /// then this function would return the slice `['a]` as the own args.\n712:     pub fn own_args(self, interner: I) -> I::GenericArgsSlice {\n713:         self.trait_ref_and_own_args(interner).1\n714:     }\n715: }\n716: \n717: /// The following methods work only with inherent associated term projections.\n718: impl<I: Interner> AliasTerm<I> {\n719:     /// Transform the generic parameters to have the given `impl` args as the base and the GAT args on top of that.\n720:     ///\n721:     /// Does the following transformation:\n722:     ///\n723:     /// ```text\n724:     /// [Self, P_0...P_m] -> [I_0...I_n, P_0...P_m]\n725:     ///\n726:     ///     I_i impl args\n727:     ///     P_j GAT args\n728:     /// ```\n729:     pub fn rebase_inherent_args_onto_impl(\n730:         self,\n731:         impl_args: I::GenericArgs,\n732:         interner: I,\n733:     ) -> I::GenericArgs {\n734:         debug_assert!(matches!(\n735:             self.kind(interner),\n736:             AliasTermKind::InherentTy | AliasTermKind::InherentConst\n737:         ));\n738:         interner.mk_args_from_iter(impl_args.iter().chain(self.args.iter().skip(1)))\n739:     }\n740: }\n741: \n742: impl<I: Interner> From<ty::AliasTy<I>> for AliasTerm<I> {\n743:     fn from(ty: ty::AliasTy<I>) -> Self {\n744:         AliasTerm { args: ty.args, def_id: ty.def_id, _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\n745:     }\n746: }\n747: \n748: impl<I: Interner> From<ty::UnevaluatedConst<I>> for AliasTerm<I> {\n749:     fn from(ct: ty::UnevaluatedConst<I>) -> Self {\n750:         AliasTerm { args: ct.args, def_id: ct.def.into(), _use_alias_term_new_instead: () }\n751:     }\n752: }\n753: \n754: /// This kind of predicate has no *direct* correspondent in the\n755: /// syntax, but it roughly corresponds to the syntactic forms:\n756: ///\n757: /// 1. `T: TraitRef<..., Item = Type>`\n758: /// 2. `<T as TraitRef<...>>::Item == Type` (NYI)\n759: ///\n760: /// In particular, form #1 is \"desugared\" to the combination of a\n761: /// normal trait predicate (`T: TraitRef<...>`) and one of these\n762: /// predicates. Form #2 is a broader form in that it also permits\n763: /// equality between arbitrary types. Processing an instance of\n764: /// Form #2 eventually yields one of these `ProjectionPredicate`\n765: /// instances to normalize the LHS.\n766: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\n767: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n768: #[cfg_attr(\n769:     feature = \"nightly\",\n770:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n771: )]\n772: pub struct ProjectionPredicate<I: Interner> {\n773:     pub projection_term: AliasTerm<I>,\n774:     pub term: I::Term,\n775: }\n776: \n777: impl<I: Interner> Eq for ProjectionPredicate<I> {}\n778: \n779: impl<I: Interner> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\n780:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\n781:         self.projection_term.self_ty()\n782:     }\n783: \n784:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> ProjectionPredicate<I> {\n785:         Self {\n786:             projection_term: self.projection_term.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty),\n787:             ..self\n788:         }\n789:     }\n790: \n791:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\n792:         self.projection_term.trait_def_id(interner)\n793:     }\n794: \n795:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::DefId {\n796:         self.projection_term.def_id\n797:     }\n798: }\n799: \n800: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, ProjectionPredicate<I>> {\n801:     /// Returns the `DefId` of the trait of the associated item being projected.\n802:     #[inline]\n803:     pub fn trait_def_id(&self, cx: I) -> I::TraitId {\n804:         self.skip_binder().projection_term.trait_def_id(cx)\n805:     }\n806: \n807:     pub fn term(&self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Term> {\n808:         self.map_bound(|predicate| predicate.term)\n809:     }\n810: \n811:     /// The `DefId` of the `TraitItem` for the associated type.\n812:     ///\n813:     /// Note that this is not the `DefId` of the `TraitRef` containing this\n814:     /// associated type, which is in `tcx.associated_item(projection_def_id()).container`.\n815:     pub fn item_def_id(&self) -> I::DefId {\n816:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\n817:         self.skip_binder().projection_term.def_id\n818:     }\n819: }\n820: \n821: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for ProjectionPredicate<I> {\n822:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n823:         write!(f, \"ProjectionPredicate({:?}, {:?})\", self.projection_term, self.term)\n824:     }\n825: }\n826: \n827: /// Used by the new solver to normalize an alias. This always expects the `term` to\n828: /// be an unconstrained inference variable which is used as the output.\n829: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq; I: Interner)]\n830: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n831: #[cfg_attr(\n832:     feature = \"nightly\",\n833:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n834: )]\n835: pub struct NormalizesTo<I: Interner> {\n836:     pub alias: AliasTerm<I>,\n837:     pub term: I::Term,\n838: }\n839: \n840: impl<I: Interner> Eq for NormalizesTo<I> {}\n841: \n842: impl<I: Interner> NormalizesTo<I> {\n843:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\n844:         self.alias.self_ty()\n845:     }\n846: \n847:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> NormalizesTo<I> {\n848:         Self { alias: self.alias.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty), ..self }\n849:     }\n850: \n851:     pub fn trait_def_id(self, interner: I) -> I::TraitId {\n852:         self.alias.trait_def_id(interner)\n853:     }\n854: \n855:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::DefId {\n856:         self.alias.def_id\n857:     }\n858: }\n859: \n860: impl<I: Interner> fmt::Debug for NormalizesTo<I> {\n861:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n862:         write!(f, \"NormalizesTo({:?}, {:?})\", self.alias, self.term)\n863:     }\n864: }\n865: \n866: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n867: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n868: #[cfg_attr(\n869:     feature = \"nightly\",\n870:     derive(Encodable_NoContext, Decodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n871: )]\n872: pub struct HostEffectPredicate<I: Interner> {\n873:     pub trait_ref: ty::TraitRef<I>,\n874:     pub constness: BoundConstness,\n875: }\n876: \n877: impl<I: Interner> Eq for HostEffectPredicate<I> {}\n878: \n879: impl<I: Interner> HostEffectPredicate<I> {\n880:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> I::Ty {\n881:         self.trait_ref.self_ty()\n882:     }\n883: \n884:     pub fn with_replaced_self_ty(self, interner: I, self_ty: I::Ty) -> Self {\n885:         Self { trait_ref: self.trait_ref.with_replaced_self_ty(interner, self_ty), ..self }\n886:     }\n887: \n888:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\n889:         self.trait_ref.def_id\n890:     }\n891: }\n892: \n893: impl<I: Interner> ty::Binder<I, HostEffectPredicate<I>> {\n894:     pub fn def_id(self) -> I::TraitId {\n895:         // Ok to skip binder since trait `DefId` does not care about regions.\n896:         self.skip_binder().def_id()\n897:     }\n898: \n899:     pub fn self_ty(self) -> ty::Binder<I, I::Ty> {\n900:         self.map_bound(|trait_ref| trait_ref.self_ty())\n901:     }\n902: \n903:     #[inline]\n904:     pub fn constness(self) -> BoundConstness {\n905:         self.skip_binder().constness\n906:     }\n907: }\n908: \n909: /// Encodes that `a` must be a subtype of `b`. The `a_is_expected` flag indicates\n910: /// whether the `a` type is the type that we should label as \"expected\" when\n911: /// presenting user diagnostics.\n912: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n913: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n914: #[cfg_attr(\n915:     feature = \"nightly\",\n916:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n917: )]\n918: pub struct SubtypePredicate<I: Interner> {\n919:     pub a_is_expected: bool,\n920:     pub a: I::Ty,\n921:     pub b: I::Ty,\n922: }\n923: \n924: impl<I: Interner> Eq for SubtypePredicate<I> {}\n925: \n926: /// Encodes that we have to coerce *from* the `a` type to the `b` type.\n927: #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n928: #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n929: #[cfg_attr(\n930:     feature = \"nightly\",\n931:     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n932: )]\n933: pub struct CoercePredicate<I: Interner> {\n934:     pub a: I::Ty,\n935:     pub b: I::Ty,\n936: }\n937: \n938: impl<I: Interner> Eq for CoercePredicate<I> {}\n939: \n940: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]\n941: #[cfg_attr(\n942:     feature = \"nightly\",\n943:     derive(Encodable_NoContext, Decodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n944: )]\n945: pub enum BoundConstness {\n946:     /// `Type: const Trait`\n947:     ///\n948:     /// A bound is required to be unconditionally const, even in a runtime function.\n949:     Const,\n950:     /// `Type: [const] Trait`\n951:     ///\n952:     /// Requires resolving to const only when we are in a const context.\n953:     Maybe,\n954: }\n955: \n956: impl BoundConstness {\n957:     pub fn satisfies(self, goal: BoundConstness) -> bool {\n958:         match (self, goal) {\n959:             (BoundConstness::Const, BoundConstness::Const | BoundConstness::Maybe) => true,\n960:             (BoundConstness::Maybe, BoundConstness::Maybe) => true,\n961:             (BoundConstness::Maybe, BoundConstness::Const) => false,\n962:         }\n963:     }\n964: \n965:     pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {\n966:         match self {\n967:             Self::Const => \"const\",\n968:             Self::Maybe => \"[const]\",\n969:         }\n970:     }\n971: }\n972: \n973: impl fmt::Display for BoundConstness {\n974:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n975:         match self {\n976:             Self::Const => f.write_str(\"const\"),\n977:             Self::Maybe => f.write_str(\"[const]\"),\n978:         }\n979:     }\n980: }\n981: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs"
  }
}

Input
{
  "path": "compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7"

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: use std::fmt::{self, Debug, Display, Formatter};\\n2: use std::ops::Range;\\n3: \\n4: use serde::Serialize;\\n5: \\n6: use super::abi::ReprOptions;\\n7: use super::mir::{Body, Mutability, Safety};\\n8: use super::{DefId, Error, Symbol, with};\\n9: use crate::abi::{FnAbi, Layout};\\n10: use crate::crate_def::{CrateDef, CrateDefItems, CrateDefType};\\n11: use crate::mir::alloc::{AllocId, read_target_int, read_target_uint};\\n12: use crate::mir::mono::StaticDef;\\n13: use crate::target::MachineInfo;\\n14: use crate::{Filename, IndexedVal, Opaque, ThreadLocalIndex};\\n15: \\n16: #[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\\n17: pub struct Ty(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\\n18: \\n19: impl Debug for Ty {\\n20:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n21:         f.debug_struct(\\\"Ty\\\").field(\\\"id\\\", &self.0).field(\\\"kind\\\", &self.kind()).finish()\\n22:     }\\n23: }\\n24: \\n25: /// Constructors for `Ty`.\\n26: impl Ty {\\n27:     /// Create a new type from a given kind.\\n28:     pub fn from_rigid_kind(kind: RigidTy) -> Ty {\\n29:         with(|cx| cx.new_rigid_ty(kind))\\n30:     }\\n31: \\n32:     /// Create a new array type.\\n33:     pub fn try_new_array(elem_ty: Ty, size: u64) -> Result<Ty, Error> {\\n34:         Ok(Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Array(elem_ty,
