TyConst::try_from_target_usize(size)?)))\\n35:     }\\n36: \\n37:     /// Create a new array type from Const length.\\n38:     pub fn new_array_with_const_len(elem_ty: Ty, len: TyConst) -> Ty {\\n39:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Array(elem_ty, len))\\n40:     }\\n41: \\n42:     /// Create a new pointer type.\\n43:     pub fn new_ptr(pointee_ty: Ty, mutability: Mutability) -> Ty {\\n44:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::RawPtr(pointee_ty, mutability))\\n45:     }\\n46: \\n47:     /// Create a new reference type.\\n48:     pub fn new_ref(reg: Region, pointee_ty: Ty, mutability: Mutability) -> Ty {\\n49:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Ref(reg, pointee_ty, mutability))\\n50:     }\\n51: \\n52:     /// Create a new pointer type.\\n53:     pub fn new_tuple(tys: &[Ty]) -> Ty {\\n54:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Tuple(Vec::from(tys)))\\n55:     }\\n56: \\n57:     /// Create a new closure type.\\n58:     pub fn new_closure(def: ClosureDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\\n59:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Closure(def, args))\\n60:     }\\n61: \\n62:     /// Create a new coroutine type.\\n63:     pub fn new_coroutine(def: CoroutineDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\\n64:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Coroutine(def, args))\\n65:     }\\n66: \\n67:     /// Create a new closure type.\\n68:     pub fn new_coroutine_closure(def: CoroutineClosureDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\\n69:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::CoroutineClosure(def, args))\\n70:     }\\n71: \\n72:     /// Create a new box type that represents `Box<T>`, for the given inner type `T`.\\n73:     pub fn new_box(inner_ty: Ty) -> Ty {\\n74:         with(|cx| cx.new_box_ty(inner_ty))\\n75:     }\\n76: \\n77:     /// Create a type representing `usize`.\\n78:     pub fn usize_ty() -> Ty {\\n79:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Uint(UintTy::Usize))\\n80:     }\\n81: \\n82:     /// Create a type representing `bool`.\\n83:     pub fn bool_ty() -> Ty {\\n84:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Bool)\\n85:     }\\n86: \\n87:     /// Create a type representing a signed integer.\\n88:     pub fn signed_ty(inner: IntTy) -> Ty {\\n89:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Int(inner))\\n90:     }\\n91: \\n92:     /// Create a type representing an unsigned integer.\\n93:     pub fn unsigned_ty(inner: UintTy) -> Ty {\\n94:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Uint(inner))\\n95:     }\\n96: \\n97:     /// Get a type layout.\\n98:     pub fn layout(self) -> Result<Layout, Error> {\\n99:         with(|cx| cx.ty_layout(self))\\n100:     }\\n101: }\\n102: \\n103: impl Ty {\\n104:     pub fn kind(&self) -> TyKind {\\n105:         with(|context| context.ty_kind(*self))\\n106:     }\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: /// Represents a pattern in the type system\\n110: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n111: pub enum Pattern {\\n112:     Range { start: Option<TyConst>, end: Option<TyConst>, include_end: bool },\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: /// Represents a constant in the type system\\n116: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n117: pub struct TyConst {\\n118:     pub(crate) kind: TyConstKind,\\n119:     pub id: TyConstId,\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: impl TyConst {\\n123:     pub fn new(kind: TyConstKind, id: TyConstId) -> TyConst {\\n124:         Self { kind, id }\\n125:     }\\n126: \\n127:     /// Retrieve the constant kind.\\n128:     pub fn kind(&self) -> &TyConstKind {\\n129:         &self.kind\\n130:     }\\n131: \\n132:     /// Creates an interned usize constant.\\n133:     pub fn try_from_target_usize(val: u64) -> Result<Self, Error> {\\n134:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_ty_const_uint(val.into(), UintTy::Usize))\\n135:     }\\n136: \\n137:     /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\\n138:     pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\\n139:         with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize_ty(self))\\n140:     }\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n144: pub enum TyConstKind {\\n145:     Param(ParamConst),\\n146:     Bound(DebruijnIndex, BoundVar),\\n147:     Unevaluated(ConstDef, GenericArgs),\\n148: \\n149:     // FIXME: These should be a valtree\\n150:     Value(Ty, Allocation),\\n151:     ZSTValue(Ty),\\n152: }\\n153: \\n154: #[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\\n155: pub struct TyConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\\n156: \\n157: /// Represents a constant in MIR\\n158: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n159: pub struct MirConst {\\n160:     /// The constant kind.\\n161:     pub(crate) kind: ConstantKind,\\n162:     /// The constant type.\\n163:     pub(crate) ty: Ty,\\n164:     /// Used for internal tracking of the internal constant.\\n165:     pub id: MirConstId,\\n166: }\\n167: \\n168: impl MirConst {\\n169:     /// Build a constant. Note that this should only be used by the compiler.\\n170:     pub fn new(kind: ConstantKind, ty: Ty, id: MirConstId) -> MirConst {\\n171:         MirConst { kind, ty, id }\\n172:     }\\n173: \\n174:     /// Retrieve the constant kind.\\n175:     pub fn kind(&self) -> &ConstantKind {\\n176:         &self.kind\\n177:     }\\n178: \\n179:     /// Get the constant type.\\n180:     pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\\n181:         self.ty\\n182:     }\\n183: \\n184:     /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\\n185:     pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\\n186:         with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize(self))\\n187:     }\\n188: \\n189:     /// Create a constant that represents a new zero-sized constant of type T.\\n190:     /// Fails if the type is not a ZST or if it doesn't have a known size.\\n191:     pub fn try_new_zero_sized(ty: Ty) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\\n192:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_zst(ty))\\n193:     }\\n194: \\n195:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given string.\\n196:     ///\\n197:     /// Note that there is no guarantee today about duplication of the same constant.\\n198:     /// I.e.: Calling this function multiple times with the same argument may or may not return\\n199:     /// the same allocation.\\n200:     pub fn from_str(value: &str) -> MirConst {\\n201:         with(|cx| cx.new_const_str(value))\\n202:     }\\n203: \\n204:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given boolean value.\\n205:     pub fn from_bool(value: bool) -> MirConst {\\n206:         with(|cx| cx.new_const_bool(value))\\n207:     }\\n208: \\n209:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given unsigned integer.\\n210:     pub fn try_from_uint(value: u128, uint_ty: UintTy) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\\n211:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_uint(value, uint_ty))\\n212:     }\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\\n216: pub struct MirConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\\n217: \\n218: type Ident = Opaque;\\n219: \\n220: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n221: pub struct Region {\\n222:     pub kind: RegionKind,\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n226: pub enum RegionKind {\\n227:     ReEarlyParam(EarlyParamRegion),\\n228:     ReBound(DebruijnIndex, BoundRegion),\\n229:     ReStatic,\\n230:     RePlaceholder(Placeholder<BoundRegion>),\\n231:     ReErased,\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: pub(crate) type DebruijnIndex = u32;\\n235: \\n236: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n237: pub struct EarlyParamRegion {\\n238:     pub index: u32,\\n239:     pub name: Symbol,\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: pub(crate) type BoundVar = u32;\\n243: \\n244: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n245: pub struct BoundRegion {\\n246:     pub var: BoundVar,\\n247:     pub kind: BoundRegionKind,\\n248: }\\n249: \\n250: pub(crate) type UniverseIndex = u32;\\n251: \\n252: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n253: pub struct Placeholder<T> {\\n254:     pub universe: UniverseIndex,\\n255:     pub bound: T,\\n256: }\\n257: \\n258: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\\n259: pub struct Span(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\\n260: \\n261: impl Debug for Span {\\n262:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n263:         f.debug_struct(\\\"Span\\\")\\n264:             .field(\\\"id\\\", &self.0)\\n265:             .field(\\\"repr\\\", &with(|cx| cx.span_to_string(*self)))\\n266:             .finish()\\n267:     }\\n268: }\\n269: \\n270: impl Span {\\n271:     /// Return filename for diagnostic purposes\\n272:     pub fn get_filename(&self) -> Filename {\\n273:         with(|c| c.get_filename(self))\\n274:     }\\n275: \\n276:     /// Return lines that correspond to this `Span`\\n277:     pub fn get_lines(&self) -> LineInfo {\\n278:         with(|c| c.get_lines(self))\\n279:     }\\n280: \\n281:     /// Return the span location to be printed in diagnostic messages.\\n282:     ///\\n283:     /// This may leak local file paths and should not be used to build artifacts that may be\\n284:     /// distributed.\\n285:     pub fn diagnostic(&self) -> String {\\n286:         with(|c| c.span_to_string(*self))\\n287:     }\\n288: }\\n289: \\n290: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Serialize)]\\n291: /// Information you get from `Span` in a struct form.\\n292: /// Line and col start from 1.\\n293: pub struct LineInfo {\\n294:     pub start_line: usize,\\n295:     pub start_col: usize,\\n296:     pub end_line: usize,\\n297:     pub end_col: usize,\\n298: }\\n299: \\n300: impl LineInfo {\\n301:     pub fn from(lines: (usize, usize, usize, usize)) -> Self {\\n302:         LineInfo { start_line: lines.0, start_col: lines.1, end_line: lines.2, end_col: lines.3 }\\n303:     }\\n304: }\\n305: \\n306: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n307: pub enum TyKind {\\n308:     RigidTy(RigidTy),\\n309:     Alias(AliasKind, AliasTy),\\n310:     Param(ParamTy),\\n311:     Bound(usize, BoundTy),\\n312: }\\n313: \\n314: impl TyKind {\\n315:     pub fn rigid(&self) -> Option<&RigidTy> {\\n316:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(inner) = self { Some(inner) } else { None }\\n317:     }\\n318: \\n319:     #[inline]\\n320:     pub fn is_unit(&self) -> bool {\\n321:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(data)) if data.is_empty())\\n322:     }\\n323: \\n324:     #[inline]\\n325:     pub fn is_bool(&self) -> bool {\\n326:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool))\\n327:     }\\n328: \\n329:     #[inline]\\n330:     pub fn is_char(&self) -> bool {\\n331:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char))\\n332:     }\\n333: \\n334:     #[inline]\\n335:     pub fn is_trait(&self) -> bool {\\n336:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(_, _)))\\n337:     }\\n338: \\n339:     #[inline]\\n340:     pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\\n341:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Enum)\\n342:     }\\n343: \\n344:     #[inline]\\n345:     pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\\n346:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Struct)\\n347:     }\\n348: \\n349:     #[inline]\\n350:     pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\\n351:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Union)\\n352:     }\\n353: \\n354:     #[inline]\\n355:     pub fn is_adt(&self) -> bool {\\n356:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(..)))\\n357:     }\\n358: \\n359:     #[inline]\\n360:     pub fn is_ref(&self) -> bool {\\n361:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(..)))\\n362:     }\\n363: \\n364:     #[inline]\\n365:     pub fn is_fn(&self) -> bool {\\n366:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(..)))\\n367:     }\\n368: \\n369:     #[inline]\\n370:     pub fn is_fn_ptr(&self) -> bool {\\n371:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(..)))\\n372:     }\\n373: \\n374:     #[inline]\\n375:     pub fn is_primitive(&self) -> bool {\\n376:         matches!(\\n377:             self,\\n378:             TyKind::RigidTy(\\n379:                 RigidTy::Bool\\n380:                     | RigidTy::Char\\n381:                     | RigidTy::Int(_)\\n382:                     | RigidTy::Uint(_)\\n383:                     | RigidTy::Float(_)\\n384:             )\\n385:         )\\n386:     }\\n387: \\n388:     #[inline]\\n389:     pub fn is_float(&self) -> bool {\\n390:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(_)))\\n391:     }\\n392: \\n393:     #[inline]\\n394:     pub fn is_integral(&self) -> bool {\\n395:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_) | RigidTy::Uint(_)))\\n396:     }\\n397: \\n398:     #[inline]\\n399:     pub fn is_numeric(&self) -> bool {\\n400:         self.is_integral() || self.is_float()\\n401:     }\\n402: \\n403:     #[inline]\\n404:     pub fn is_signed(&self) -> bool {\\n405:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_)))\\n406:     }\\n407: \\n408:     #[inline]\\n409:     pub fn is_str(&self) -> bool {\\n410:         *self == TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str)\\n411:     }\\n412: \\n413:     #[inline]\\n414:     pub fn is_cstr(&self) -> bool {\\n415:         let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) = self else {\\n416:             return false;\\n417:         };\\n418:         with(|cx| cx.adt_is_cstr(*def))\\n419:     }\\n420: \\n421:     #[inline]\\n422:     pub fn is_slice(&self) -> bool {\\n423:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(_)))\\n424:     }\\n425: \\n426:     #[inline]\\n427:     pub fn is_array(&self) -> bool {\\n428:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(..)))\\n429:     }\\n430: \\n431:     #[inline]\\n432:     pub fn is_mutable_ptr(&self) -> bool {\\n433:         matches!(\\n434:             self,\\n435:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(_, Mutability::Mut))\\n436:                 | TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(_, _, Mutability::Mut))\\n437:         )\\n438:     }\\n439: \\n440:     #[inline]\\n441:     pub fn is_raw_ptr(&self) -> bool {\\n442:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(..)))\\n443:     }\\n444: \\n445:     /// Tests if this is any kind of primitive pointer type (reference, raw pointer, fn pointer).\\n446:     #[inline]\\n447:     pub fn is_any_ptr(&self) -> bool {\\n448:         self.is_ref() || self.is_raw_ptr() || self.is_fn_ptr()\\n449:     }\\n450: \\n451:     #[inline]\\n452:     pub fn is_coroutine(&self) -> bool {\\n453:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(..)))\\n454:     }\\n455: \\n456:     #[inline]\\n457:     pub fn is_closure(&self) -> bool {\\n458:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(..)))\\n459:     }\\n460: \\n461:     #[inline]\\n462:     pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\\n463:         match self {\\n464:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) => def.is_box(),\\n465:             _ => false,\\n466:         }\\n467:     }\\n468: \\n469:     #[inline]\\n470:     pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\\n471:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.is_simd())\\n472:     }\\n473: \\n474:     pub fn trait_principal(&self) -> Option<Binder<ExistentialTraitRef>> {\\n475:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(predicates, _)) = self {\\n476:             if let Some(Binder { value: ExistentialPredicate::Trait(trait_ref), bound_vars }) =\\n477:                 predicates.first()\\n478:             {\\n479:                 Some(Binder { value: trait_ref.clone(), bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() })\\n480:             } else {\\n481:                 None\\n482:             }\\n483:         } else {\\n484:             None\\n485:         }\\n486:     }\\n487: \\n488:     /// Returns the type of `ty[i]` for builtin types.\\n489:     pub fn builtin_index(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\\n490:         match self.rigid()? {\\n491:             RigidTy::Array(ty, _) | RigidTy::Slice(ty) => Some(*ty),\\n492:             _ => None,\\n493:         }\\n494:     }\\n495: \\n496:     /// Returns the type and mutability of `*ty` for builtin types.\\n497:     ///\\n498:     /// The parameter `explicit` indicates if this is an *explicit* dereference.\\n499:     /// Some types -- notably raw ptrs -- can only be dereferenced explicitly.\\n500:     pub fn builtin_deref(&self, explicit: bool) -> Option<TypeAndMut> {\\n501:         match self.rigid()? {\\n502:             RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\\n503:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\\n504:             }\\n505:             RigidTy::Ref(_, ty, mutability) => {\\n506:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\\n507:             }\\n508:             RigidTy::RawPtr(ty, mutability) if explicit => {\\n509:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\\n510:             }\\n511:             _ => None,\\n512:         }\\n513:     }\\n514: \\n515:     /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\\n516:     pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\\n517:         match self {\\n518:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\\n519:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\\n520:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\\n521:             _ => None,\\n522:         }\\n523:     }\\n524: \\n525:     /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\\n526:     pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\\n527:         self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\\n528:     }\\n529: \\n530:     /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\\n531:     pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\\n532:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\\n533:             Some((*def, args))\\n534:         } else {\\n535:             None\\n536:         }\\n537:     }\\n538: }\\n539: \\n540: pub struct TypeAndMut {\\n541:     pub ty: Ty,\\n542:     pub mutability: Mutability,\\n543: }\\n544: \\n545: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n546: pub enum RigidTy {\\n547:     Bool,\\n548:     Char,\\n549:     Int(IntTy),\\n550:     Uint(UintTy),\\n551:     Float(FloatTy),\\n552:     Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\\n553:     Foreign(ForeignDef),\\n554:     Str,\\n555:     Array(Ty, TyConst),\\n556:     Pat(Ty, Pattern),\\n557:     Slice(Ty),\\n558:     RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\\n559:     Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\\n560:     FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\\n561:     FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\\n562:     Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\\n563:     Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\\n564:     CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\\n565:     Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\\n566:     Never,\\n567:     Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\\n568:     CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\\n569: }\\n570: \\n571: impl RigidTy {\\n572:     /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\\n573:     pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\\n574:         with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\\n575:     }\\n576: }\\n577: \\n578: impl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\\n579:     fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\\n580:         TyKind::RigidTy(value)\\n581:     }\\n582: }\\n583: \\n584: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\\n585: pub enum IntTy {\\n586:     Isize,\\n587:     I8,\\n588:     I16,\\n589:     I32,\\n590:     I64,\\n591:     I128,\\n592: }\\n593: \\n594: impl IntTy {\\n595:     pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\\n596:         match self {\\n597:             IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\\n598:             IntTy::I8 => 1,\\n599:             IntTy::I16 => 2,\\n600:             IntTy::I32 => 4,\\n601:             IntTy::I64 => 8,\\n602:             IntTy::I128 => 16,\\n603:         }\\n604:     }\\n605: }\\n606: \\n607: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\\n608: pub enum UintTy {\\n609:     Usize,\\n610:     U8,\\n611:     U16,\\n612:     U32,\\n613:     U64,\\n614:     U128,\\n615: }\\n616: \\n617: impl UintTy {\\n618:     pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\\n619:         match self {\\n620:             UintTy::Usize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\\n621:             UintTy::U8 => 1,\\n622:             UintTy::U16 => 2,\\n623:             UintTy::U32 => 4,\\n624:             UintTy::U64 => 8,\\n625:             UintTy::U128 => 16,\\n626:         }\\n627:     }\\n628: }\\n629: \\n630: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\\n631: pub enum FloatTy {\\n632:     F16,\\n633:     F32,\\n634:     F64,\\n635:     F128,\\n636: }\\n637: \\n638: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\\n639: pub enum Movability {\\n640:     Static,\\n641:     Movable,\\n642: }\\n643: \\n644: crate_def! {\\n645:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n646:     pub ForeignModuleDef;\\n647: }\\n648: \\n649: impl ForeignModuleDef {\\n650:     pub fn module(&self) -> ForeignModule {\\n651:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_module(*self))\\n652:     }\\n653: }\\n654: \\n655: pub struct ForeignModule {\\n656:     pub def_id: ForeignModuleDef,\\n657:     pub abi: Abi,\\n658: }\\n659: \\n660: impl ForeignModule {\\n661:     pub fn items(&self) -> Vec<ForeignDef> {\\n662:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_items(self.def_id))\\n663:     }\\n664: }\\n665: \\n666: crate_def_with_ty! {\\n667:     /// Hold information about a ForeignItem in a crate.\\n668:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n669:     pub ForeignDef;\\n670: }\\n671: \\n672: impl ForeignDef {\\n673:     pub fn kind(&self) -> ForeignItemKind {\\n674:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_item_kind(*self))\\n675:     }\\n676: }\\n677: \\n678: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\\n679: pub enum ForeignItemKind {\\n680:     Fn(FnDef),\\n681:     Static(StaticDef),\\n682:     Type(Ty),\\n683: }\\n684: \\n685: crate_def_with_ty! {\\n686:     /// Hold information about a function definition in a crate.\\n687:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n688:     pub FnDef;\\n689: }\\n690: \\n691: impl FnDef {\\n692:     // Get the function body if available.\\n693:     pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\\n694:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0).then(|| ctx.mir_body(self.0)))\\n695:     }\\n696: \\n697:     // Check if the function body is available.\\n698:     pub fn has_body(&self) -> bool {\\n699:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0))\\n700:     }\\n701: \\n702:     /// Get the information of the intrinsic if this function is a definition of one.\\n703:     pub fn as_intrinsic(&self) -> Option<IntrinsicDef> {\\n704:         with(|cx| cx.intrinsic(self.def_id()))\\n705:     }\\n706: \\n707:     /// Check if the function is an intrinsic.\\n708:     #[inline]\\n709:     pub fn is_intrinsic(&self) -> bool {\\n710:         self.as_intrinsic().is_some()\\n711:     }\\n712: \\n713:     /// Get the function signature for this function definition.\\n714:     pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> PolyFnSig {\\n715:         let kind = self.ty().kind();\\n716:         kind.fn_sig().unwrap()\\n717:     }\\n718: }\\n719: \\n720: crate_def_with_ty! {\\n721:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n722:     pub IntrinsicDef;\\n723: }\\n724: \\n725: impl IntrinsicDef {\\n726:     /// Returns the plain name of the intrinsic.\\n727:     /// e.g., `transmute` for `core::intrinsics::transmute`.\\n728:     pub fn fn_name(&self) -> Symbol {\\n729:         with(|cx| cx.intrinsic_name(*self))\\n730:     }\\n731: \\n732:     /// Returns whether the intrinsic has no meaningful body and all backends\\n733:     /// need to shim all calls to it.\\n734:     pub fn must_be_overridden(&self) -> bool {\\n735:         with(|cx| !cx.has_body(self.0))\\n736:     }\\n737: }\\n738: \\n739: impl From<IntrinsicDef> for FnDef {\\n740:     fn from(def: IntrinsicDef) -> Self {\\n741:         FnDef(def.0)\\n742:     }\\n743: }\\n744: \\n745: crate_def! {\\n746:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n747:     pub ClosureDef;\\n748: }\\n749: \\n750: impl ClosureDef {\\n751:     /// Retrieves the body of the closure definition. Returns None if the body\\n752:     /// isn't available.\\n753:     pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\\n754:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0).then(|| ctx.mir_body(self.0)))\\n755:     }\\n756: }\\n757: \\n758: crate_def! {\\n759:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n760:     pub CoroutineDef;\\n761: }\\n762: \\n763: impl CoroutineDef {\\n764:     /// Retrieves the body of the coroutine definition. Returns None if the body\\n765:     /// isn't available.\\n766:     pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\\n767:         with(|cx| cx.has_body(self.0).then(|| cx.mir_body(self.0)))\\n768:     }\\n769: \\n770:     pub fn discriminant_for_variant(&self, args: &GenericArgs, idx: VariantIdx) -> Discr {\\n771:         with(|cx| cx.coroutine_discr_for_variant(*self, args, idx))\\n772:     }\\n773: }\\n774: \\n775: crate_def! {\\n776:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n777:     pub CoroutineClosureDef;\\n778: }\\n779: \\n780: crate_def! {\\n781:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n782:     pub ParamDef;\\n783: }\\n784: \\n785: crate_def! {\\n786:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n787:     pub BrNamedDef;\\n788: }\\n789: \\n790: crate_def! {\\n791:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n792:     pub AdtDef;\\n793: }\\n794: \\n795: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\\n796: pub enum AdtKind {\\n797:     Enum,\\n798:     Union,\\n799:     Struct,\\n800: }\\n801: \\n802: impl AdtDef {\\n803:     pub fn kind(&self) -> AdtKind {\\n804:         with(|cx| cx.adt_kind(*self))\\n805:     }\\n806: \\n807:     /// Retrieve the type of this Adt.\\n808:     pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\\n809:         with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.0))\\n810:     }\\n811: \\n812:     /// Retrieve the type of this Adt by instantiating and normalizing it with the given arguments.\\n813:     ///\\n814:     /// This will assume the type can be instantiated with these arguments.\\n815:     pub fn ty_with_args(&self, args: &GenericArgs) -> Ty {\\n816:         with(|cx| cx.def_ty_with_args(self.0, args))\\n817:     }\\n818: \\n819:     pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\\n820:         with(|cx| cx.adt_is_box(*self))\\n821:     }\\n822: \\n823:     pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\\n824:         with(|cx| cx.adt_is_simd(*self))\\n825:     }\\n826: \\n827:     /// The number of variants in this ADT.\\n828:     pub fn num_variants(&self) -> usize {\\n829:         with(|cx| cx.adt_variants_len(*self))\\n830:     }\\n831: \\n832:     /// Retrieve the variants in this ADT.\\n833:     pub fn variants(&self) -> Vec<VariantDef> {\\n834:         self.variants_iter().collect()\\n835:     }\\n836: \\n837:     /// Iterate over the variants in this ADT.\\n838:     pub fn variants_iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = VariantDef> {\\n839:         (0..self.num_variants())\\n840:             .map(|idx| VariantDef { idx: VariantIdx::to_val(idx), adt_def: *self })\\n841:     }\\n842: \\n843:     pub fn variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Option<VariantDef> {\\n844:         (idx.to_index() < self.num_variants()).then_some(VariantDef { idx, adt_def: *self })\\n845:     }\\n846: \\n847:     pub fn repr(&self) -> ReprOptions {\\n848:         with(|cx| cx.adt_repr(*self))\\n849:     }\\n850: \\n851:     pub fn discriminant_for_variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Discr {\\n852:         with(|cx| cx.adt_discr_for_variant(*self, idx))\\n853:     }\\n854: }\\n855: \\n856: pub struct Discr {\\n857:     pub val: u128,\\n858:     pub ty: Ty,\\n859: }\\n860: \\n861: /// Definition of a variant, which can be either a struct / union field or an enum variant.\\n862: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n863: pub struct VariantDef {\\n864:     /// The variant index.\\n865:     ///\\n866:     /// ## Warning\\n867:     /// Do not access this field directly!\\n868:     pub idx: VariantIdx,\\n869:     /// The data type where this variant comes from.\\n870:     /// For now, we use this to retrieve information about the variant itself so we don't need to\\n871:     /// cache more information.\\n872:     ///\\n873:     /// ## Warning\\n874:     /// Do not access this field directly!\\n875:     pub adt_def: AdtDef,\\n876: }\\n877: \\n878: impl VariantDef {\\n879:     pub fn name(&self) -> Symbol {\\n880:         with(|cx| cx.variant_name(*self))\\n881:     }\\n882: \\n883:     /// Retrieve all the fields in this variant.\\n884:     // We expect user to cache this and use it directly since today it is expensive to generate all\\n885:     // fields name.\\n886:     pub fn fields(&self) -> Vec<FieldDef> {\\n887:         with(|cx| cx.variant_fields(*self))\\n888:     }\\n889: }\\n890: \\n891: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n892: pub struct FieldDef {\\n893:     /// The field definition.\\n894:     ///\\n895:     /// ## Warning\\n896:     /// Do not access this field directly! This is public for the compiler to have access to it.\\n897:     pub def: DefId,\\n898: \\n899:     /// The field name.\\n900:     pub name: Symbol,\\n901: }\\n902: \\n903: impl FieldDef {\\n904:     /// Retrieve the type of this field instantiating and normalizing it with the given arguments.\\n905:     ///\\n906:     /// This will assume the type can be instantiated with these arguments.\\n907:     pub fn ty_with_args(&self, args: &GenericArgs) -> Ty {\\n908:         with(|cx| cx.def_ty_with_args(self.def, args))\\n909:     }\\n910: \\n911:     /// Retrieve the type of this field.\\n912:     pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\\n913:         with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.def))\\n914:     }\\n915: }\\n916: \\n917: impl Display for AdtKind {\\n918:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n919:         f.write_str(match self {\\n920:             AdtKind::Enum => \\\"enum\\\",\\n921:             AdtKind::Union => \\\"union\\\",\\n922:             AdtKind::Struct => \\\"struct\\\",\\n923:         })\\n924:     }\\n925: }\\n926: \\n927: impl AdtKind {\\n928:     pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\\n929:         matches!(self, AdtKind::Enum)\\n930:     }\\n931: \\n932:     pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\\n933:         matches!(self, AdtKind::Struct)\\n934:     }\\n935: \\n936:     pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\\n937:         matches!(self, AdtKind::Union)\\n938:     }\\n939: }\\n940: \\n941: crate_def! {\\n942:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n943:     pub AliasDef;\\n944: }\\n945: \\n946: crate_def! {\\n947:     /// A trait's definition.\\n948:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n949:     pub TraitDef;\\n950: }\\n951: \\n952: impl_crate_def_items! {\\n953:     TraitDef;\\n954: }\\n955: \\n956: impl TraitDef {\\n957:     pub fn declaration(trait_def: &TraitDef) -> TraitDecl {\\n958:         with(|cx| cx.trait_decl(trait_def))\\n959:     }\\n960: }\\n961: \\n962: crate_def! {\\n963:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n964:     pub GenericDef;\\n965: }\\n966: \\n967: crate_def_with_ty! {\\n968:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n969:     pub ConstDef;\\n970: }\\n971: \\n972: crate_def! {\\n973:     /// A trait impl definition.\\n974:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n975:     pub ImplDef;\\n976: }\\n977: \\n978: impl_crate_def_items! {\\n979:     ImplDef;\\n980: }\\n981: \\n982: impl ImplDef {\\n983:     /// Retrieve information about this implementation.\\n984:     pub fn trait_impl(&self) -> ImplTrait {\\n985:         with(|cx| cx.trait_impl(self))\\n986:     }\\n987: }\\n988: \\n989: crate_def! {\\n990:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n991:     pub RegionDef;\\n992: }\\n993: \\n994: crate_def! {\\n995:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n996:     pub CoroutineWitnessDef;\\n997: }\\n998: \\n999: /// A list of generic arguments.\\n1000: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1001: pub struct GenericArgs(pub Vec<GenericArgKind>);\\n1002: \\n1003: impl std::ops::Index<ParamTy> for GenericArgs {\\n1004:     type Output = Ty;\\n1005: \\n1006:     fn index(&self, index: ParamTy) -> &Self::Output {\\n1007:         self.0[index.index as usize].expect_ty()\\n1008:     }\\n1009: }\\n1010: \\n1011: impl std::ops::Index<ParamConst> for GenericArgs {\\n1012:     type Output = TyConst;\\n1013: \\n1014:     fn index(&self, index: ParamConst) -> &Self::Output {\\n1015:         self.0[index.index as usize].expect_const()\\n1016:     }\\n1017: }\\n1018: \\n1019: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1020: pub enum GenericArgKind {\\n1021:     Lifetime(Region),\\n1022:     Type(Ty),\\n1023:     Const(TyConst),\\n1024: }\\n1025: \\n1026: impl GenericArgKind {\\n1027:     /// Panic if this generic argument is not a type, otherwise\\n1028:     /// return the type.\\n1029:     #[track_caller]\\n1030:     pub fn expect_ty(&self) -> &Ty {\\n1031:         match self {\\n1032:             GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty,\\n1033:             _ => panic!(\\\"{self:?}\\\"),\\n1034:         }\\n1035:     }\\n1036: \\n1037:     /// Panic if this generic argument is not a const, otherwise\\n1038:     /// return the const.\\n1039:     #[track_caller]\\n1040:     pub fn expect_const(&self) -> &TyConst {\\n1041:         match self {\\n1042:             GenericArgKind::Const(c) => c,\\n1043:             _ => panic!(\\\"{self:?}\\\"),\\n1044:         }\\n1045:     }\\n1046: \\n1047:     /// Return the generic argument type if applicable, otherwise return `None`.\\n1048:     pub fn ty(&self) -> Option<&Ty> {\\n1049:         match self {\\n1050:             GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => Some(ty),\\n1051:             _ => None,\\n1052:         }\\n1053:     }\\n1054: }\\n1055: \\n1056: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1057: pub enum TermKind {\\n1058:     Type(Ty),\\n1059:     Const(TyConst),\\n1060: }\\n1061: \\n1062: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1063: pub enum AliasKind {\\n1064:     Projection,\\n1065:     Inherent,\\n1066:     Opaque,\\n1067:     Free,\\n1068: }\\n1069: \\n1070: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1071: pub struct AliasTy {\\n1072:     pub def_id: AliasDef,\\n1073:     pub args: GenericArgs,\\n1074: }\\n1075: \\n1076: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1077: pub struct AliasTerm {\\n1078:     pub def_id: AliasDef,\\n1079:     pub args: GenericArgs,\\n1080: }\\n1081: \\n1082: pub type PolyFnSig = Binder<FnSig>;\\n1083: \\n1084: impl PolyFnSig {\\n1085:     /// Compute a `FnAbi` suitable for indirect calls, i.e. to `fn` pointers.\\n1086:     ///\\n1087:     /// NB: this doesn't handle virtual calls - those should use `Instance::fn_abi`\\n1088:     /// instead, where the instance is an `InstanceKind::Virtual`.\\n1089:     pub fn fn_ptr_abi(self) -> Result<FnAbi, Error> {\\n1090:         with(|cx| cx.fn_ptr_abi(self))\\n1091:     }\\n1092: }\\n1093: \\n1094: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1095: pub struct FnSig {\\n1096:     pub inputs_and_output: Vec<Ty>,\\n1097:     pub c_variadic: bool,\\n1098:     pub safety: Safety,\\n1099:     pub abi: Abi,\\n1100: }\\n1101: \\n1102: impl FnSig {\\n1103:     pub fn output(&self) -> Ty {\\n1104:         self.inputs_and_output[self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\\n1105:     }\\n1106: \\n1107:     pub fn inputs(&self) -> &[Ty] {\\n1108:         &self.inputs_and_output[..self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\\n1109:     }\\n1110: }\\n1111: \\n1112: #[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Serialize)]\\n1113: pub enum Abi {\\n1114:     Rust,\\n1115:     C { unwind: bool },\\n1116:     Cdecl { unwind: bool },\\n1117:     Stdcall { unwind: bool },\\n1118:     Fastcall { unwind: bool },\\n1119:     Vectorcall { unwind: bool },\\n1120:     Thiscall { unwind: bool },\\n1121:     Aapcs { unwind: bool },\\n1122:     Win64 { unwind: bool },\\n1123:     SysV64 { unwind: bool },\\n1124:     PtxKernel,\\n1125:     Msp430Interrupt,\\n1126:     X86Interrupt,\\n1127:     GpuKernel,\\n1128:     EfiApi,\\n1129:     AvrInterrupt,\\n1130:     AvrNonBlockingInterrupt,\\n1131:     CCmseNonSecureCall,\\n1132:     CCmseNonSecureEntry,\\n1133:     System { unwind: bool },\\n1134:     RustCall,\\n1135:     Unadjusted,\\n1136:     RustCold,\\n1137:     RiscvInterruptM,\\n1138:     RiscvInterruptS,\\n1139:     RustInvalid,\\n1140:     Custom,\\n1141: }\\n1142: \\n1143: /// A binder represents a possibly generic type and its bound vars.\\n1144: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1145: pub struct Binder<T> {\\n1146:     pub value: T,\\n1147:     pub bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>,\\n1148: }\\n1149: \\n1150: impl<T> Binder<T> {\\n1151:     /// Create a new binder with the given bound vars.\\n1152:     pub fn bind_with_vars(value: T, bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>) -> Self {\\n1153:         Binder { value, bound_vars }\\n1154:     }\\n1155: \\n1156:     /// Create a new binder with no bounded variable.\\n1157:     pub fn dummy(value: T) -> Self {\\n1158:         Binder { value, bound_vars: vec![] }\\n1159:     }\\n1160: \\n1161:     pub fn skip_binder(self) -> T {\\n1162:         self.value\\n1163:     }\\n1164: \\n1165:     pub fn map_bound_ref<F, U>(&self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\\n1166:     where\\n1167:         F: FnOnce(&T) -> U,\\n1168:     {\\n1169:         let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\\n1170:         let new_value = f(value);\\n1171:         Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() }\\n1172:     }\\n1173: \\n1174:     pub fn map_bound<F, U>(self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\\n1175:     where\\n1176:         F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\\n1177:     {\\n1178:         let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\\n1179:         let new_value = f(value);\\n1180:         Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars }\\n1181:     }\\n1182: }\\n1183: \\n1184: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1185: pub struct EarlyBinder<T> {\\n1186:     pub value: T,\\n1187: }\\n1188: \\n1189: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1190: pub enum BoundVariableKind {\\n1191:     Ty(BoundTyKind),\\n1192:     Region(BoundRegionKind),\\n1193:     Const,\\n1194: }\\n1195: \\n1196: #[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Serialize)]\\n1197: pub enum BoundTyKind {\\n1198:     Anon,\\n1199:     Param(ParamDef, String),\\n1200: }\\n1201: \\n1202: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1203: pub enum BoundRegionKind {\\n1204:     BrAnon,\\n1205:     BrNamed(BrNamedDef, String),\\n1206:     BrEnv,\\n1207: }\\n1208: \\n1209: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1210: pub enum ExistentialPredicate {\\n1211:     Trait(ExistentialTraitRef),\\n1212:     Projection(ExistentialProjection),\\n1213:     AutoTrait(TraitDef),\\n1214: }\\n1215: \\n1216: /// An existential reference to a trait where `Self` is not included.\\n1217: ///\\n1218: /// The `generic_args` will include any other known argument.\\n1219: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1220: pub struct ExistentialTraitRef {\\n1221:     pub def_id: TraitDef,\\n1222:     pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\\n1223: }\\n1224: \\n1225: impl Binder<ExistentialTraitRef> {\\n1226:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, self_ty: Ty) -> Binder<TraitRef> {\\n1227:         self.map_bound_ref(|trait_ref| trait_ref.with_self_ty(self_ty))\\n1228:     }\\n1229: }\\n1230: \\n1231: impl ExistentialTraitRef {\\n1232:     pub fn with_self_ty(&self, self_ty: Ty) -> TraitRef {\\n1233:         TraitRef::new(self.def_id, self_ty, &self.generic_args)\\n1234:     }\\n1235: }\\n1236: \\n1237: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1238: pub struct ExistentialProjection {\\n1239:     pub def_id: TraitDef,\\n1240:     pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\\n1241:     pub term: TermKind,\\n1242: }\\n1243: \\n1244: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1245: pub struct ParamTy {\\n1246:     pub index: u32,\\n1247:     pub name: String,\\n1248: }\\n1249: \\n1250: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1251: pub struct BoundTy {\\n1252:     pub var: usize,\\n1253:     pub kind: BoundTyKind,\\n1254: }\\n1255: \\n1256: pub type Bytes = Vec<Option<u8>>;\\n1257: \\n1258: /// Size in bytes.\\n1259: pub type Size = usize;\\n1260: \\n1261: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1262: pub struct Prov(pub AllocId);\\n1263: \\n1264: pub type Align = u64;\\n1265: pub type Promoted = u32;\\n1266: pub type InitMaskMaterialized = Vec<u64>;\\n1267: \\n1268: /// Stores the provenance information of pointers stored in memory.\\n1269: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1270: pub struct ProvenanceMap {\\n1271:     /// Provenance in this map applies from the given offset for an entire pointer-size worth of\\n1272:     /// bytes. Two entries in this map are always at least a pointer size apart.\\n1273:     pub ptrs: Vec<(Size, Prov)>,\\n1274: }\\n1275: \\n1276: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1277: pub struct Allocation {\\n1278:     pub bytes: Bytes,\\n1279:     pub provenance: ProvenanceMap,\\n1280:     pub align: Align,\\n1281:     pub mutability: Mutability,\\n1282: }\\n1283: \\n1284: impl Allocation {\\n1285:     /// Get a vector of bytes for an Allocation that has been fully initialized\\n1286:     pub fn raw_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {\\n1287:         self.bytes\\n1288:             .iter()\\n1289:             .copied()\\n1290:             .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\\n1291:             .ok_or_else(|| error!(\\\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\\\", self.bytes))\\n1292:     }\\n1293: \\n1294:     /// Read a uint value from the specified range.\\n1295:     pub fn read_partial_uint(&self, range: Range<usize>) -> Result<u128, Error> {\\n1296:         if range.end - range.start > 16 {\\n1297:             return Err(error!(\\\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\\\"));\\n1298:         }\\n1299:         if range.end > self.bytes.len() {\\n1300:             return Err(error!(\\n1301:                 \\\"Range is out of bounds. Allocation length is `{}`, but requested range `{:?}`\\\",\\n1302:                 self.bytes.len(),\\n1303:                 range\\n1304:             ));\\n1305:         }\\n1306:         let raw = self.bytes[range]\\n1307:             .iter()\\n1308:             .copied()\\n1309:             .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\\n1310:             .ok_or_else(|| error!(\\\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\\\", self.bytes))?;\\n1311:         read_target_uint(&raw)\\n1312:     }\\n1313: \\n1314:     /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to an unassigned integer.\\n1315:     pub fn read_uint(&self) -> Result<u128, Error> {\\n1316:         if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\\n1317:             return Err(error!(\\\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\\\"));\\n1318:         }\\n1319:         let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\\n1320:         read_target_uint(&raw)\\n1321:     }\\n1322: \\n1323:     /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a signed integer.\\n1324:     pub fn read_int(&self) -> Result<i128, Error> {\\n1325:         if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\\n1326:             return Err(error!(\\\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\\\"));\\n1327:         }\\n1328:         let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\\n1329:         read_target_int(&raw)\\n1330:     }\\n1331: \\n1332:     /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a boolean.\\n1333:     pub fn read_bool(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\\n1334:         match self.read_int()? {\\n1335:             0 => Ok(false),\\n1336:             1 => Ok(true),\\n1337:             val => Err(error!(\\\"Unexpected value for bool: `{val}`\\\")),\\n1338:         }\\n1339:     }\\n1340: \\n1341:     /// Read this allocation as a pointer and return whether it represents a `null` pointer.\\n1342:     pub fn is_null(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\\n1343:         let len = self.bytes.len();\\n1344:         let ptr_len = MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes();\\n1345:         if len != ptr_len {\\n1346:             return Err(error!(\\\"Expected width of pointer (`{ptr_len}`), but found: `{len}`\\\"));\\n1347:         }\\n1348:         Ok(self.read_uint()? == 0 && self.provenance.ptrs.is_empty())\\n1349:     }\\n1350: }\\n1351: \\n1352: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1353: pub enum ConstantKind {\\n1354:     Ty(TyConst),\\n1355:     Allocated(Allocation),\\n1356:     Unevaluated(UnevaluatedConst),\\n1357:     Param(ParamConst),\\n1358:     /// Store ZST constants.\\n1359:     /// We have to special handle these constants since its type might be generic.\\n1360:     ZeroSized,\\n1361: }\\n1362: \\n1363: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1364: pub struct ParamConst {\\n1365:     pub index: u32,\\n1366:     pub name: String,\\n1367: }\\n1368: \\n1369: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1370: pub struct UnevaluatedConst {\\n1371:     pub def: ConstDef,\\n1372:     pub args: GenericArgs,\\n1373:     pub promoted: Option<Promoted>,\\n1374: }\\n1375: \\n1376: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\\n1377: pub enum TraitSpecializationKind {\\n1378:     None,\\n1379:     Marker,\\n1380:     AlwaysApplicable,\\n1381: }\\n1382: \\n1383: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1384: pub struct TraitDecl {\\n1385:     pub def_id: TraitDef,\\n1386:     pub safety: Safety,\\n1387:     pub paren_sugar: bool,\\n1388:     pub has_auto_impl: bool,\\n1389:     pub is_marker: bool,\\n1390:     pub is_coinductive: bool,\\n1391:     pub skip_array_during_method_dispatch: bool,\\n1392:     pub skip_boxed_slice_during_method_dispatch: bool,\\n1393:     pub specialization_kind: TraitSpecializationKind,\\n1394:     pub must_implement_one_of: Option<Vec<Ident>>,\\n1395:     pub implement_via_object: bool,\\n1396:     pub deny_explicit_impl: bool,\\n1397: }\\n1398: \\n1399: impl TraitDecl {\\n1400:     pub fn generics_of(&self) -> Generics {\\n1401:         with(|cx| cx.generics_of(self.def_id.0))\\n1402:     }\\n1403: \\n1404:     pub fn predicates_of(&self) -> GenericPredicates {\\n1405:         with(|cx| cx.predicates_of(self.def_id.0))\\n1406:     }\\n1407: \\n1408:     pub fn explicit_predicates_of(&self) -> GenericPredicates {\\n1409:         with(|cx| cx.explicit_predicates_of(self.def_id.0))\\n1410:     }\\n1411: }\\n1412: \\n1413: pub type ImplTrait = EarlyBinder<TraitRef>;\\n1414: \\n1415: /// A complete reference to a trait, i.e., one where `Self` is known.\\n1416: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1417: pub struct TraitRef {\\n1418:     pub def_id: TraitDef,\\n1419:     /// The generic arguments for this definition.\\n1420:     /// The first element must always be type, and it represents `Self`.\\n1421:     args: GenericArgs,\\n1422: }\\n1423: \\n1424: impl TraitRef {\\n1425:     pub fn new(def_id: TraitDef, self_ty: Ty, gen_args: &GenericArgs) -> TraitRef {\\n1426:         let mut args = vec![GenericArgKind::Type(self_ty)];\\n1427:         args.extend_from_slice(&gen_args.0);\\n1428:         TraitRef { def_id, args: GenericArgs(args) }\\n1429:     }\\n1430: \\n1431:     pub fn try_new(def_id: TraitDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Result<TraitRef, ()> {\\n1432:         match &args.0[..] {\\n1433:             [GenericArgKind::Type(_), ..] => Ok(TraitRef { def_id, args }),\\n1434:             _ => Err(()),\\n1435:         }\\n1436:     }\\n1437: \\n1438:     pub fn args(&self) -> &GenericArgs {\\n1439:         &self.args\\n1440:     }\\n1441: \\n1442:     pub fn self_ty(&self) -> Ty {\\n1443:         let GenericArgKind::Type(self_ty) = self.args.0[0] else {\\n1444:             panic!(\\\"Self must be a type, but found: {:?}\\\", self.args.0[0])\\n1445:         };\\n1446:         self_ty\\n1447:     }\\n1448: }\\n1449: \\n1450: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1451: pub struct Generics {\\n1452:     pub parent: Option<GenericDef>,\\n1453:     pub parent_count: usize,\\n1454:     pub params: Vec<GenericParamDef>,\\n1455:     pub param_def_id_to_index: Vec<(GenericDef, u32)>,\\n1456:     pub has_self: bool,\\n1457:     pub has_late_bound_regions: Option<Span>,\\n1458: }\\n1459: \\n1460: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1461: pub enum GenericParamDefKind {\\n1462:     Lifetime,\\n1463:     Type { has_default: bool, synthetic: bool },\\n1464:     Const { has_default: bool },\\n1465: }\\n1466: \\n1467: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1468: pub struct GenericParamDef {\\n1469:     pub name: super::Symbol,\\n1470:     pub def_id: GenericDef,\\n1471:     pub index: u32,\\n1472:     pub pure_wrt_drop: bool,\\n1473:     pub kind: GenericParamDefKind,\\n1474: }\\n1475: \\n1476: pub struct GenericPredicates {\\n1477:     pub parent: Option<TraitDef>,\\n1478:     pub predicates: Vec<(PredicateKind, Span)>,\\n1479: }\\n1480: \\n1481: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1482: pub enum PredicateKind {\\n1483:     Clause(ClauseKind),\\n1484:     DynCompatible(TraitDef),\\n1485:     SubType(SubtypePredicate),\\n1486:     Coerce(CoercePredicate),\\n1487:     ConstEquate(TyConst, TyConst),\\n1488:     Ambiguous,\\n1489:     AliasRelate(TermKind, TermKind, AliasRelationDirection),\\n1490: }\\n1491: \\n1492: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1493: pub enum ClauseKind {\\n1494:     Trait(TraitPredicate),\\n1495:     RegionOutlives(RegionOutlivesPredicate),\\n1496:     TypeOutlives(TypeOutlivesPredicate),\\n1497:     Projection(ProjectionPredicate),\\n1498:     ConstArgHasType(TyConst, Ty),\\n1499:     WellFormed(TermKind),\\n1500:     ConstEvaluatable(TyConst),\\n1501: }\\n1502: \\n1503: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1504: pub enum ClosureKind {\\n1505:     Fn,\\n1506:     FnMut,\\n1507:     FnOnce,\\n1508: }\\n1509: \\n1510: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1511: pub struct SubtypePredicate {\\n1512:     pub a: Ty,\\n1513:     pub b: Ty,\\n1514: }\\n1515: \\n1516: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1517: pub struct CoercePredicate {\\n1518:     pub a: Ty,\\n1519:     pub b: Ty,\\n1520: }\\n1521: \\n1522: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1523: pub enum AliasRelationDirection {\\n1524:     Equate,\\n1525:     Subtype,\\n1526: }\\n1527: \\n1528: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1529: pub struct TraitPredicate {\\n1530:     pub trait_ref: TraitRef,\\n1531:     pub polarity: PredicatePolarity,\\n1532: }\\n1533: \\n1534: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1535: pub struct OutlivesPredicate<A, B>(pub A, pub B);\\n1536: \\n1537: pub type RegionOutlivesPredicate = OutlivesPredicate<Region, Region>;\\n1538: pub type TypeOutlivesPredicate = OutlivesPredicate<Ty, Region>;\\n1539: \\n1540: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1541: pub struct ProjectionPredicate {\\n1542:     pub projection_term: AliasTerm,\\n1543:     pub term: TermKind,\\n1544: }\\n1545: \\n1546: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1547: pub enum ImplPolarity {\\n1548:     Positive,\\n1549:     Negative,\\n1550:     Reservation,\\n1551: }\\n1552: \\n1553: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1554: pub enum PredicatePolarity {\\n1555:     Positive,\\n1556:     Negative,\\n1557: }\\n1558: \\n1559: macro_rules! index_impl {\\n1560:     ($name:ident) => {\\n1561:         impl crate::IndexedVal for $name {\\n1562:             fn to_val(index: usize) -> Self {\\n1563:                 $name(index, $crate::ThreadLocalIndex)\\n1564:             }\\n1565:             fn to_index(&self) -> usize {\\n1566:                 self.0\\n1567:             }\\n1568:         }\\n1569:         $crate::ty::serialize_index_impl!($name);\\n1570:     };\\n1571: }\\n1572: macro_rules! serialize_index_impl {\\n1573:     ($name:ident) => {\\n1574:         impl ::serde::Serialize for $name {\\n1575:             fn serialize<S>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>\\n1576:             where\\n1577:                 S: ::serde::Serializer,\\n1578:             {\\n1579:                 let n: usize = self.0; // Make sure we're serializing an int.\\n1580:                 ::serde::Serialize::serialize(&n, serializer)\\n1581:             }\\n1582:         }\\n1583:     };\\n1584: }\\n1585: pub(crate) use {index_impl, serialize_index_impl};\\n1586: \\n1587: index_impl!(TyConstId);\\n1588: index_impl!(MirConstId);\\n1589: index_impl!(Ty);\\n1590: index_impl!(Span);\\n1591: \\n1592: /// The source-order index of a variant in a type.\\n1593: ///\\n1594: /// For example, in the following types,\\n1595: /// ```ignore(illustrative)\\n1596: /// enum Demo1 {\\n1597: ///    Variant0 { a: bool, b: i32 },\\n1598: ///    Variant1 { c: u8, d: u64 },\\n1599: /// }\\n1600: /// struct Demo2 { e: u8, f: u16, g: u8 }\\n1601: /// ```\\n1602: /// `a` is in the variant with the `VariantIdx` of `0`,\\n1603: /// `c` is in the variant with the `VariantIdx` of `1`, and\\n1604: /// `g` is in the variant with the `VariantIdx` of `0`.\\n1605: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\\n1606: pub struct VariantIdx(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\\n1607: \\n1608: index_impl!(VariantIdx);\\n1609: \\n1610: crate_def! {\\n1611:     /// Hold information about an Opaque definition, particularly useful in `RPITIT`.\\n1612:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n1613:     pub OpaqueDef;\\n1614: }\\n1615: \\n1616: crate_def! {\\n1617:     #[derive(Serialize)]\\n1618:     pub AssocDef;\\n1619: }\\n1620: \\n1621: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1622: pub struct AssocItem {\\n1623:     pub def_id: AssocDef,\\n1624:     pub kind: AssocKind,\\n1625:     pub container: AssocContainer,\\n1626: }\\n1627: \\n1628: #[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Debug, Eq, Serialize)]\\n1629: pub enum AssocTypeData {\\n1630:     Normal(Symbol),\\n1631:     /// The associated type comes from an RPITIT. It has no name, and the\\n1632:     /// `ImplTraitInTraitData` provides additional information about its\\n1633:     /// source.\\n1634:     Rpitit(ImplTraitInTraitData),\\n1635: }\\n1636: \\n1637: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1638: pub enum AssocKind {\\n1639:     Const { name: Symbol },\\n1640:     Fn { name: Symbol, has_self: bool },\\n1641:     Type { data: AssocTypeData },\\n1642: }\\n1643: \\n1644: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\\n1645: pub enum AssocContainer {\\n1646:     InherentImpl,\\n1647:     /// The `AssocDef` points to the trait item being implemented.\\n1648:     TraitImpl(AssocDef),\\n1649:     Trait,\\n1650: }\\n1651: \\n1652: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\\n1653: pub enum ImplTraitInTraitData {\\n1654:     Trait { fn_def_id: FnDef, opaque_def_id: OpaqueDef },\\n1655:     Impl { fn_def_id: FnDef },\\n1656: }\\n1657: \\n1658: impl AssocItem {\\n1659:     pub fn is_impl_trait_in_trait(&self) -> bool {\\n1660:         matches!(self.kind, AssocKind::Type { data: AssocTypeData::Rpitit(_) })\\n1661:     }\\n1662: }\\n1663: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: use std::fmt::{self, Debug, Display, Formatter};\n2: use std::ops::Range;\n3: \n4: use serde::Serialize;\n5: \n6: use super::abi::ReprOptions;\n7: use super::mir::{Body, Mutability, Safety};\n8: use super::{DefId, Error, Symbol, with};\n9: use crate::abi::{FnAbi, Layout};\n10: use crate::crate_def::{CrateDef, CrateDefItems, CrateDefType};\n11: use crate::mir::alloc::{AllocId, read_target_int, read_target_uint};\n12: use crate::mir::mono::StaticDef;\n13: use crate::target::MachineInfo;\n14: use crate::{Filename, IndexedVal, Opaque, ThreadLocalIndex};\n15: \n16: #[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\n17: pub struct Ty(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n18: \n19: impl Debug for Ty {\n20:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n21:         f.debug_struct(\"Ty\").field(\"id\", &self.0).field(\"kind\", &self.kind()).finish()\n22:     }\n23: }\n24: \n25: /// Constructors for `Ty`.\n26: impl Ty {\n27:     /// Create a new type from a given kind.\n28:     pub fn from_rigid_kind(kind: RigidTy) -> Ty {\n29:         with(|cx| cx.new_rigid_ty(kind))\n30:     }\n31: \n32:     /// Create a new array type.\n33:     pub fn try_new_array(elem_ty: Ty, size: u64) -> Result<Ty, Error> {\n34:         Ok(Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Array(elem_ty, TyConst::try_from_target_usize(size)?)))\n35:     }\n36: \n37:     /// Create a new array type from Const length.\n38:     pub fn new_array_with_const_len(elem_ty: Ty, len: TyConst) -> Ty {\n39:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Array(elem_ty, len))\n40:     }\n41: \n42:     /// Create a new pointer type.\n43:     pub fn new_ptr(pointee_ty: Ty, mutability: Mutability) -> Ty {\n44:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::RawPtr(pointee_ty, mutability))\n45:     }\n46: \n47:     /// Create a new reference type.\n48:     pub fn new_ref(reg: Region, pointee_ty: Ty, mutability: Mutability) -> Ty {\n49:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Ref(reg, pointee_ty, mutability))\n50:     }\n51: \n52:     /// Create a new pointer type.\n53:     pub fn new_tuple(tys: &[Ty]) -> Ty {\n54:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Tuple(Vec::from(tys)))\n55:     }\n56: \n57:     /// Create a new closure type.\n58:     pub fn new_closure(def: ClosureDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\n59:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Closure(def, args))\n60:     }\n61: \n62:     /// Create a new coroutine type.\n63:     pub fn new_coroutine(def: CoroutineDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\n64:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Coroutine(def, args))\n65:     }\n66: \n67:     /// Create a new closure type.\n68:     pub fn new_coroutine_closure(def: CoroutineClosureDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Ty {\n69:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::CoroutineClosure(def, args))\n70:     }\n71: \n72:     /// Create a new box type that represents `Box<T>`, for the given inner type `T`.\n73:     pub fn new_box(inner_ty: Ty) -> Ty {\n74:         with(|cx| cx.new_box_ty(inner_ty))\n75:     }\n76: \n77:     /// Create a type representing `usize`.\n78:     pub fn usize_ty() -> Ty {\n79:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Uint(UintTy::Usize))\n80:     }\n81: \n82:     /// Create a type representing `bool`.\n83:     pub fn bool_ty() -> Ty {\n84:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Bool)\n85:     }\n86: \n87:     /// Create a type representing a signed integer.\n88:     pub fn signed_ty(inner: IntTy) -> Ty {\n89:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Int(inner))\n90:     }\n91: \n92:     /// Create a type representing an unsigned integer.\n93:     pub fn unsigned_ty(inner: UintTy) -> Ty {\n94:         Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Uint(inner))\n95:     }\n96: \n97:     /// Get a type layout.\n98:     pub fn layout(self) -> Result<Layout, Error> {\n99:         with(|cx| cx.ty_layout(self))\n100:     }\n101: }\n102: \n103: impl Ty {\n104:     pub fn kind(&self) -> TyKind {\n105:         with(|context| context.ty_kind(*self))\n106:     }\n107: }\n108: \n109: /// Represents a pattern in the type system\n110: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\n111: pub enum Pattern {\n112:     Range { start: Option<TyConst>, end: Option<TyConst>, include_end: bool },\n113: }\n114: \n115: /// Represents a constant in the type system\n116: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n117: pub struct TyConst {\n118:     pub(crate) kind: TyConstKind,\n119:     pub id: TyConstId,\n120: }\n121: \n122: impl TyConst {\n123:     pub fn new(kind: TyConstKind, id: TyConstId) -> TyConst {\n124:         Self { kind, id }\n125:     }\n126: \n127:     /// Retrieve the constant kind.\n128:     pub fn kind(&self) -> &TyConstKind {\n129:         &self.kind\n130:     }\n131: \n132:     /// Creates an interned usize constant.\n133:     pub fn try_from_target_usize(val: u64) -> Result<Self, Error> {\n134:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_ty_const_uint(val.into(), UintTy::Usize))\n135:     }\n136: \n137:     /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n138:     pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n139:         with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize_ty(self))\n140:     }\n141: }\n142: \n143: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n144: pub enum TyConstKind {\n145:     Param(ParamConst),\n146:     Bound(DebruijnIndex, BoundVar),\n147:     Unevaluated(ConstDef, GenericArgs),\n148: \n149:     // FIXME: These should be a valtree\n150:     Value(Ty, Allocation),\n151:     ZSTValue(Ty),\n152: }\n153: \n154: #[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\n155: pub struct TyConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n156: \n157: /// Represents a constant in MIR\n158: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n159: pub struct MirConst {\n160:     /// The constant kind.\n161:     pub(crate) kind: ConstantKind,\n162:     /// The constant type.\n163:     pub(crate) ty: Ty,\n164:     /// Used for internal tracking of the internal constant.\n165:     pub id: MirConstId,\n166: }\n167: \n168: impl MirConst {\n169:     /// Build a constant. Note that this should only be used by the compiler.\n170:     pub fn new(kind: ConstantKind, ty: Ty, id: MirConstId) -> MirConst {\n171:         MirConst { kind, ty, id }\n172:     }\n173: \n174:     /// Retrieve the constant kind.\n175:     pub fn kind(&self) -> &ConstantKind {\n176:         &self.kind\n177:     }\n178: \n179:     /// Get the constant type.\n180:     pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n181:         self.ty\n182:     }\n183: \n184:     /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n185:     pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n186:         with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize(self))\n187:     }\n188: \n189:     /// Create a constant that represents a new zero-sized constant of type T.\n190:     /// Fails if the type is not a ZST or if it doesn't have a known size.\n191:     pub fn try_new_zero_sized(ty: Ty) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n192:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_zst(ty))\n193:     }\n194: \n195:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given string.\n196:     ///\n197:     /// Note that there is no guarantee today about duplication of the same constant.\n198:     /// I.e.: Calling this function multiple times with the same argument may or may not return\n199:     /// the same allocation.\n200:     pub fn from_str(value: &str) -> MirConst {\n201:         with(|cx| cx.new_const_str(value))\n202:     }\n203: \n204:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given boolean value.\n205:     pub fn from_bool(value: bool) -> MirConst {\n206:         with(|cx| cx.new_const_bool(value))\n207:     }\n208: \n209:     /// Build a new constant that represents the given unsigned integer.\n210:     pub fn try_from_uint(value: u128, uint_ty: UintTy) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n211:         with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_uint(value, uint_ty))\n212:     }\n213: }\n214: \n215: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n216: pub struct MirConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n217: \n218: type Ident = Opaque;\n219: \n220: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n221: pub struct Region {\n222:     pub kind: RegionKind,\n223: }\n224: \n225: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n226: pub enum RegionKind {\n227:     ReEarlyParam(EarlyParamRegion),\n228:     ReBound(DebruijnIndex, BoundRegion),\n229:     ReStatic,\n230:     RePlaceholder(Placeholder<BoundRegion>),\n231:     ReErased,\n232: }\n233: \n234: pub(crate) type DebruijnIndex = u32;\n235: \n236: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n237: pub struct EarlyParamRegion {\n238:     pub index: u32,\n239:     pub name: Symbol,\n240: }\n241: \n242: pub(crate) type BoundVar = u32;\n243: \n244: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n245: pub struct BoundRegion {\n246:     pub var: BoundVar,\n247:     pub kind: BoundRegionKind,\n248: }\n249: \n250: pub(crate) type UniverseIndex = u32;\n251: \n252: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\n253: pub struct Placeholder<T> {\n254:     pub universe: UniverseIndex,\n255:     pub bound: T,\n256: }\n257: \n258: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n259: pub struct Span(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n260: \n261: impl Debug for Span {\n262:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n263:         f.debug_struct(\"Span\")\n264:             .field(\"id\", &self.0)\n265:             .field(\"repr\", &with(|cx| cx.span_to_string(*self)))\n266:             .finish()\n267:     }\n268: }\n269: \n270: impl Span {\n271:     /// Return filename for diagnostic purposes\n272:     pub fn get_filename(&self) -> Filename {\n273:         with(|c| c.get_filename(self))\n274:     }\n275: \n276:     /// Return lines that correspond to this `Span`\n277:     pub fn get_lines(&self) -> LineInfo {\n278:         with(|c| c.get_lines(self))\n279:     }\n280: \n281:     /// Return the span location to be printed in diagnostic messages.\n282:     ///\n283:     /// This may leak local file paths and should not be used to build artifacts that may be\n284:     /// distributed.\n285:     pub fn diagnostic(&self) -> String {\n286:         with(|c| c.span_to_string(*self))\n287:     }\n288: }\n289: \n290: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Serialize)]\n291: /// Information you get from `Span` in a struct form.\n292: /// Line and col start from 1.\n293: pub struct LineInfo {\n294:     pub start_line: usize,\n295:     pub start_col: usize,\n296:     pub end_line: usize,\n297:     pub end_col: usize,\n298: }\n299: \n300: impl LineInfo {\n301:     pub fn from(lines: (usize, usize, usize, usize)) -> Self {\n302:         LineInfo { start_line: lines.0, start_col: lines.1, end_line: lines.2, end_col: lines.3 }\n303:     }\n304: }\n305: \n306: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\n307: pub enum TyKind {\n308:     RigidTy(RigidTy),\n309:     Alias(AliasKind, AliasTy),\n310:     Param(ParamTy),\n311:     Bound(usize, BoundTy),\n312: }\n313: \n314: impl TyKind {\n315:     pub fn rigid(&self) -> Option<&RigidTy> {\n316:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(inner) = self { Some(inner) } else { None }\n317:     }\n318: \n319:     #[inline]\n320:     pub fn is_unit(&self) -> bool {\n321:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(data)) if data.is_empty())\n322:     }\n323: \n324:     #[inline]\n325:     pub fn is_bool(&self) -> bool {\n326:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool))\n327:     }\n328: \n329:     #[inline]\n330:     pub fn is_char(&self) -> bool {\n331:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char))\n332:     }\n333: \n334:     #[inline]\n335:     pub fn is_trait(&self) -> bool {\n336:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(_, _)))\n337:     }\n338: \n339:     #[inline]\n340:     pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n341:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Enum)\n342:     }\n343: \n344:     #[inline]\n345:     pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\n346:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Struct)\n347:     }\n348: \n349:     #[inline]\n350:     pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\n351:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Union)\n352:     }\n353: \n354:     #[inline]\n355:     pub fn is_adt(&self) -> bool {\n356:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(..)))\n357:     }\n358: \n359:     #[inline]\n360:     pub fn is_ref(&self) -> bool {\n361:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(..)))\n362:     }\n363: \n364:     #[inline]\n365:     pub fn is_fn(&self) -> bool {\n366:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(..)))\n367:     }\n368: \n369:     #[inline]\n370:     pub fn is_fn_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n371:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(..)))\n372:     }\n373: \n374:     #[inline]\n375:     pub fn is_primitive(&self) -> bool {\n376:         matches!(\n377:             self,\n378:             TyKind::RigidTy(\n379:                 RigidTy::Bool\n380:                     | RigidTy::Char\n381:                     | RigidTy::Int(_)\n382:                     | RigidTy::Uint(_)\n383:                     | RigidTy::Float(_)\n384:             )\n385:         )\n386:     }\n387: \n388:     #[inline]\n389:     pub fn is_float(&self) -> bool {\n390:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(_)))\n391:     }\n392: \n393:     #[inline]\n394:     pub fn is_integral(&self) -> bool {\n395:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_) | RigidTy::Uint(_)))\n396:     }\n397: \n398:     #[inline]\n399:     pub fn is_numeric(&self) -> bool {\n400:         self.is_integral() || self.is_float()\n401:     }\n402: \n403:     #[inline]\n404:     pub fn is_signed(&self) -> bool {\n405:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_)))\n406:     }\n407: \n408:     #[inline]\n409:     pub fn is_str(&self) -> bool {\n410:         *self == TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str)\n411:     }\n412: \n413:     #[inline]\n414:     pub fn is_cstr(&self) -> bool {\n415:         let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) = self else {\n416:             return false;\n417:         };\n418:         with(|cx| cx.adt_is_cstr(*def))\n419:     }\n420: \n421:     #[inline]\n422:     pub fn is_slice(&self) -> bool {\n423:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(_)))\n424:     }\n425: \n426:     #[inline]\n427:     pub fn is_array(&self) -> bool {\n428:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(..)))\n429:     }\n430: \n431:     #[inline]\n432:     pub fn is_mutable_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n433:         matches!(\n434:             self,\n435:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(_, Mutability::Mut))\n436:                 | TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(_, _, Mutability::Mut))\n437:         )\n438:     }\n439: \n440:     #[inline]\n441:     pub fn is_raw_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n442:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(..)))\n443:     }\n444: \n445:     /// Tests if this is any kind of primitive pointer type (reference, raw pointer, fn pointer).\n446:     #[inline]\n447:     pub fn is_any_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n448:         self.is_ref() || self.is_raw_ptr() || self.is_fn_ptr()\n449:     }\n450: \n451:     #[inline]\n452:     pub fn is_coroutine(&self) -> bool {\n453:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(..)))\n454:     }\n455: \n456:     #[inline]\n457:     pub fn is_closure(&self) -> bool {\n458:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(..)))\n459:     }\n460: \n461:     #[inline]\n462:     pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\n463:         match self {\n464:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) => def.is_box(),\n465:             _ => false,\n466:         }\n467:     }\n468: \n469:     #[inline]\n470:     pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\n471:         matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.is_simd())\n472:     }\n473: \n474:     pub fn trait_principal(&self) -> Option<Binder<ExistentialTraitRef>> {\n475:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(predicates, _)) = self {\n476:             if let Some(Binder { value: ExistentialPredicate::Trait(trait_ref), bound_vars }) =\n477:                 predicates.first()\n478:             {\n479:                 Some(Binder { value: trait_ref.clone(), bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() })\n480:             } else {\n481:                 None\n482:             }\n483:         } else {\n484:             None\n485:         }\n486:     }\n487: \n488:     /// Returns the type of `ty[i]` for builtin types.\n489:     pub fn builtin_index(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n490:         match self.rigid()? {\n491:             RigidTy::Array(ty, _) | RigidTy::Slice(ty) => Some(*ty),\n492:             _ => None,\n493:         }\n494:     }\n495: \n496:     /// Returns the type and mutability of `*ty` for builtin types.\n497:     ///\n498:     /// The parameter `explicit` indicates if this is an *explicit* dereference.\n499:     /// Some types -- notably raw ptrs -- can only be dereferenced explicitly.\n500:     pub fn builtin_deref(&self, explicit: bool) -> Option<TypeAndMut> {\n501:         match self.rigid()? {\n502:             RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\n503:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\n504:             }\n505:             RigidTy::Ref(_, ty, mutability) => {\n506:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n507:             }\n508:             RigidTy::RawPtr(ty, mutability) if explicit => {\n509:                 Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n510:             }\n511:             _ => None,\n512:         }\n513:     }\n514: \n515:     /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\n516:     pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\n517:         match self {\n518:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\n519:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n520:             TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n521:             _ => None,\n522:         }\n523:     }\n524: \n525:     /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n526:     pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n527:         self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\n528:     }\n529: \n530:     /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\n531:     pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\n532:         if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\n533:             Some((*def, args))\n534:         } else {\n535:             None\n536:         }\n537:     }\n538: }\n539: \n540: pub struct TypeAndMut {\n541:     pub ty: Ty,\n542:     pub mutability: Mutability,\n543: }\n544: \n545: #[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\n546: pub enum RigidTy {\n547:     Bool,\n548:     Char,\n549:     Int(IntTy),\n550:     Uint(UintTy),\n551:     Float(FloatTy),\n552:     Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\n553:     Foreign(ForeignDef),\n554:     Str,\n555:     Array(Ty, TyConst),\n556:     Pat(Ty, Pattern),\n557:     Slice(Ty),\n558:     RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\n559:     Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\n560:     FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\n561:     FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\n562:     Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n563:     Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\n564:     CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n565:     Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\n566:     Never,\n567:     Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\n568:     CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\n569: }\n570: \n571: impl RigidTy {\n572:     /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n573:     pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n574:         with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\n575:     }\n576: }\n577: \n578: impl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\n579:     fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\n580:         TyKind::RigidTy(value)\n581:     }\n582: }\n583: \n584: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\n585: pub enum IntTy {\n586:     Isize,\n587:     I8,\n588:     I16,\n589:     I32,\n590:     I64,\n591:     I128,\n592: }\n593: \n594: impl IntTy {\n595:     pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n596:         match self {\n597:             IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n598:             IntTy::I8 => 1,\n599:             IntTy::I16 => 2,\n600:             IntTy::I32 => 4,\n601:             IntTy::I64 => 8,\n602:             IntTy::I128 => 16,\n603:         }\n604:     }\n605: }\n606: \n607: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\n608: pub enum UintTy {\n609:     Usize,\n610:     U8,\n611:     U16,\n612:     U32,\n613:     U64,\n614:     U128,\n615: }\n616: \n617: impl UintTy {\n618:     pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n619:         match self {\n620:             UintTy::Usize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n621:             UintTy::U8 => 1,\n622:             UintTy::U16 => 2,\n623:             UintTy::U32 => 4,\n624:             UintTy::U64 => 8,\n625:             UintTy::U128 => 16,\n626:         }\n627:     }\n628: }\n629: \n630: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\n631: pub enum FloatTy {\n632:     F16,\n633:     F32,\n634:     F64,\n635:     F128,\n636: }\n637: \n638: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\n639: pub enum Movability {\n640:     Static,\n641:     Movable,\n642: }\n643: \n644: crate_def! {\n645:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n646:     pub ForeignModuleDef;\n647: }\n648: \n649: impl ForeignModuleDef {\n650:     pub fn module(&self) -> ForeignModule {\n651:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_module(*self))\n652:     }\n653: }\n654: \n655: pub struct ForeignModule {\n656:     pub def_id: ForeignModuleDef,\n657:     pub abi: Abi,\n658: }\n659: \n660: impl ForeignModule {\n661:     pub fn items(&self) -> Vec<ForeignDef> {\n662:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_items(self.def_id))\n663:     }\n664: }\n665: \n666: crate_def_with_ty! {\n667:     /// Hold information about a ForeignItem in a crate.\n668:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n669:     pub ForeignDef;\n670: }\n671: \n672: impl ForeignDef {\n673:     pub fn kind(&self) -> ForeignItemKind {\n674:         with(|cx| cx.foreign_item_kind(*self))\n675:     }\n676: }\n677: \n678: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\n679: pub enum ForeignItemKind {\n680:     Fn(FnDef),\n681:     Static(StaticDef),\n682:     Type(Ty),\n683: }\n684: \n685: crate_def_with_ty! {\n686:     /// Hold information about a function definition in a crate.\n687:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n688:     pub FnDef;\n689: }\n690: \n691: impl FnDef {\n692:     // Get the function body if available.\n693:     pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\n694:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0).then(|| ctx.mir_body(self.0)))\n695:     }\n696: \n697:     // Check if the function body is available.\n698:     pub fn has_body(&self) -> bool {\n699:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0))\n700:     }\n701: \n702:     /// Get the information of the intrinsic if this function is a definition of one.\n703:     pub fn as_intrinsic(&self) -> Option<IntrinsicDef> {\n704:         with(|cx| cx.intrinsic(self.def_id()))\n705:     }\n706: \n707:     /// Check if the function is an intrinsic.\n708:     #[inline]\n709:     pub fn is_intrinsic(&self) -> bool {\n710:         self.as_intrinsic().is_some()\n711:     }\n712: \n713:     /// Get the function signature for this function definition.\n714:     pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> PolyFnSig {\n715:         let kind = self.ty().kind();\n716:         kind.fn_sig().unwrap()\n717:     }\n718: }\n719: \n720: crate_def_with_ty! {\n721:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n722:     pub IntrinsicDef;\n723: }\n724: \n725: impl IntrinsicDef {\n726:     /// Returns the plain name of the intrinsic.\n727:     /// e.g., `transmute` for `core::intrinsics::transmute`.\n728:     pub fn fn_name(&self) -> Symbol {\n729:         with(|cx| cx.intrinsic_name(*self))\n730:     }\n731: \n732:     /// Returns whether the intrinsic has no meaningful body and all backends\n733:     /// need to shim all calls to it.\n734:     pub fn must_be_overridden(&self) -> bool {\n735:         with(|cx| !cx.has_body(self.0))\n736:     }\n737: }\n738: \n739: impl From<IntrinsicDef> for FnDef {\n740:     fn from(def: IntrinsicDef) -> Self {\n741:         FnDef(def.0)\n742:     }\n743: }\n744: \n745: crate_def! {\n746:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n747:     pub ClosureDef;\n748: }\n749: \n750: impl ClosureDef {\n751:     /// Retrieves the body of the closure definition. Returns None if the body\n752:     /// isn't available.\n753:     pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\n754:         with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0).then(|| ctx.mir_body(self.0)))\n755:     }\n756: }\n757: \n758: crate_def! {\n759:     #[derive(Serialize)]\n760:     pub CoroutineDef;\n761: }\n762: \n763: impl CoroutineDef {\n764:     /// Retrieves the body of the coroutine definition.
