#[inline(always)]\\n91:     pub fn outer_exclusive_binder(self) -> DebruijnIndex {\\n92:         self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\\n93:     }\\n94: \\n95:     /// Flips the polarity of a Predicate.\\n96:     ///\\n97:     /// Given `T: Trait` predicate it returns `T: !Trait` and given `T: !Trait` returns `T: Trait`.\\n98:     pub fn flip_polarity(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Option<Predicate<'tcx>> {\\n99:         let kind = self\\n100:             .kind()\\n101:             .map_bound(|kind| match kind {\\n102:                 PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\\n103:                     trait_ref,\\n104:                     polarity,\\n105:                 })) => Some(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\\n106:                     trait_ref,\\n107:                     polarity: polarity.flip(),\\n108:                 }))),\\n109: \\n110:                 _ => None,\\n111:             })\\n112:             .transpose()?;\\n113: \\n114:         Some(tcx.mk_predicate(kind))\\n115:     }\\n116: \\n117:     /// Whether this projection can be soundly normalized.\\n118:     ///\\n119:     /// Wf predicates must not be normalized, as normalization\\n120:     /// can remove required bounds which would cause us to\\n121:     /// unsoundly accept some programs. See #91068.\\n122:     #[inline]\\n123:     pub fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\\n124:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n125:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::WellFormed(_)) | PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..) => {\\n126:                 false\\n127:             }\\n128:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(_))\\n129:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::HostEffect(..))\\n130:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(_))\\n131:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(_))\\n132:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(_))\\n133:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(..))\\n134:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_))\\n135:             | PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_)\\n136:             | PredicateKind::Subtype(_)\\n137:             | PredicateKind::Coerce(_)\\n138:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(_))\\n139:             | PredicateKind::ConstEquate(_, _)\\n140:             | PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..)\\n141:             | PredicateKind::Ambiguous => true,\\n142:         }\\n143:     }\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n147:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\\n148:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\\n149:             let pred = tcx.short_string(self, path);\\n150:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(pred))\\n151:         })\\n152:     }\\n153: }\\n154: \\n155: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Clause<'tcx> {\\n156:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\\n157:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\\n158:             let clause = tcx.short_string(self, path);\\n159:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(clause))\\n160:         })\\n161:     }\\n162: }\\n163: \\n164: /// A subset of predicates which can be assumed by the trait solver. They show up in\\n165: /// an item's where clauses, hence the name `Clause`, and may either be user-written\\n166: /// (such as traits) or may be inserted during lowering.\\n167: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\\n168: #[rustc_pass_by_value]\\n169: pub struct Clause<'tcx>(\\n170:     pub(super) Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>>>,\\n171: );\\n172: \\n173: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clause<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n174:     fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n175:         self.as_predicate()\\n176:     }\\n177: \\n178:     fn instantiate_supertrait(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n179:         self.instantiate_supertrait(tcx, trait_ref)\\n180:     }\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Clause<'tcx> {\\n184:     type Kind = ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>;\\n185: \\n186:     fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\\n187:         self.kind()\\n188:     }\\n189: }\\n190: \\n191: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\\n192:     pub fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n193:         Predicate(self.0)\\n194:     }\\n195: \\n196:     pub fn kind(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>> {\\n197:         self.0.internee.map_bound(|kind| match kind {\\n198:             PredicateKind::Clause(clause) => clause,\\n199:             _ => unreachable!(),\\n200:         })\\n201:     }\\n202: \\n203:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n204:         let clause = self.kind();\\n205:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n206:             Some(clause.rebind(trait_clause))\\n207:         } else {\\n208:             None\\n209:         }\\n210:     }\\n211: \\n212:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n213:         let clause = self.kind();\\n214:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Projection(projection_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n215:             Some(clause.rebind(projection_clause))\\n216:         } else {\\n217:             None\\n218:         }\\n219:     }\\n220: \\n221:     pub fn as_type_outlives_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n222:         let clause = self.kind();\\n223:         if let ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n224:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\\n225:         } else {\\n226:             None\\n227:         }\\n228:     }\\n229: \\n230:     pub fn as_region_outlives_clause(\\n231:         self,\\n232:     ) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\\n233:         let clause = self.kind();\\n234:         if let ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\\n235:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\\n236:         } else {\\n237:             None\\n238:         }\\n239:     }\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clauses<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ty::Clauses<'tcx> {}\\n243: \\n244: #[extension(pub trait ExistentialPredicateStableCmpExt<'tcx>)]\\n245: impl<'tcx> ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\\n246:     /// Compares via an ordering that will not change if modules are reordered or other changes are\\n247:     /// made to the tree. In particular, this ordering is preserved across incremental compilations.\\n248:     fn stable_cmp(&self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\\n249:         match (*self, *other) {\\n250:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => Ordering::Equal,\\n251:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref b)) => {\\n252:                 tcx.def_path_hash(a.def_id).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(b.def_id))\\n253:             }\\n254:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref b)) => {\\n255:                 tcx.def_path_hash(*a).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(*b))\\n256:             }\\n257:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\\n258:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => {\\n259:                 Ordering::Greater\\n260:             }\\n261:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\\n262:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_), _) => Ordering::Greater,\\n263:         }\\n264:     }\\n265: }\\n266: \\n267: pub type PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>;\\n268: \\n269: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::BoundExistentialPredicates<TyCtxt<'tcx>>\\n270:     for &'tcx ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>\\n271: {\\n272:     fn principal_def_id(self) -> Option<DefId> {\\n273:         self.principal_def_id()\\n274:     }\\n275: \\n276:     fn principal(self) -> Option<ty::PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>> {\\n277:         self.principal()\\n278:     }\\n279: \\n280:     fn auto_traits(self) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = DefId> {\\n281:         self.auto_traits()\\n282:     }\\n283: \\n284:     fn projection_bounds(\\n285:         self,\\n286:     ) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\\n287:         self.projection_bounds()\\n288:     }\\n289: }\\n290: \\n291: impl<'tcx> ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n292:     /// Returns the \\\"principal `DefId`\\\" of this set of existential predicates.\\n293:     ///\\n294:     /// A Rust trait object type consists (in addition to a lifetime bound)\\n295:     /// of a set of trait bounds, which are separated into any number\\n296:     /// of auto-trait bounds, and at most one non-auto-trait bound. The\\n297:     /// non-auto-trait bound is called the \\\"principal\\\" of the trait\\n298:     /// object.\\n299:     ///\\n300:     /// Only the principal can have methods or type parameters (because\\n301:     /// auto traits can have neither of them). This is important, because\\n302:     /// it means the auto traits can be treated as an unordered set (methods\\n303:     /// would force an order for the vtable, while relating traits with\\n304:     /// type parameters without knowing the order to relate them in is\\n305:     /// a rather non-trivial task).\\n306:     ///\\n307:     /// For example, in the trait object `dyn std::fmt::Debug + Sync`, the\\n308:     /// principal bound is `Some(std::fmt::Debug)`, while the auto-trait bounds\\n309:     /// are the set `{Sync}`.\\n310:     ///\\n311:     /// It is also possible to have a \\\"trivial\\\" trait object that\\n312:     /// consists only of auto traits, with no principal - for example,\\n313:     /// `dyn Send + Sync`. In that case, the set of auto-trait bounds\\n314:     /// is `{Send, Sync}`, while there is no principal. These trait objects\\n315:     /// have a \\\"trivial\\\" vtable consisting of just the size, alignment,\\n316:     /// and destructor.\\n317:     pub fn principal(&self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>> {\\n318:         self[0]\\n319:             .map_bound(|this| match this {\\n320:                 ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => Some(tr),\\n321:                 _ => None,\\n322:             })\\n323:             .transpose()\\n324:     }\\n325: \\n326:     pub fn principal_def_id(&self) -> Option<DefId> {\\n327:         self.principal().map(|trait_ref| trait_ref.skip_binder().def_id)\\n328:     }\\n329: \\n330:     #[inline]\\n331:     pub fn projection_bounds(\\n332:         &self,\\n333:     ) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\\n334:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| {\\n335:             predicate\\n336:                 .map_bound(|pred| match pred {\\n337:                     ExistentialPredicate::Projection(projection) => Some(projection),\\n338:                     _ => None,\\n339:                 })\\n340:                 .transpose()\\n341:         })\\n342:     }\\n343: \\n344:     #[inline]\\n345:     pub fn auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = DefId> {\\n346:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n347:             ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => Some(did),\\n348:             _ => None,\\n349:         })\\n350:     }\\n351: \\n352:     pub fn without_auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n353:         self.iter().filter(|predicate| {\\n354:             !matches!(predicate.as_ref().skip_binder(), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_))\\n355:         })\\n356:     }\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: pub type PolyTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>;\\n360: pub type PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>;\\n361: pub type PolyExistentialProjection<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>;\\n362: \\n363: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\\n364:     /// Performs a instantiation suitable for going from a\\n365:     /// poly-trait-ref to supertraits that must hold if that\\n366:     /// poly-trait-ref holds. This is slightly different from a normal\\n367:     /// instantiation in terms of what happens with bound regions. See\\n368:     /// lengthy comment below for details.\\n369:     pub fn instantiate_supertrait(\\n370:         self,\\n371:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n372:         trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>,\\n373:     ) -> Clause<'tcx> {\\n374:         // The interaction between HRTB and supertraits is not entirely\\n375:         // obvious. Let me walk you (and myself) through an example.\\n376:         //\\n377:         // Let's start with an easy case. Consider two traits:\\n378:         //\\n379:         //     trait Foo<'a>: Bar<'a,'a> { }\\n380:         //     trait Bar<'b,'c> { }\\n381:         //\\n382:         // Now, if we have a trait reference `for<'x> T: Foo<'x>`, then\\n383:         // we can deduce that `for<'x> T: Bar<'x,'x>`. Basically, if we\\n384:         // knew that `Foo<'x>` (for any 'x) then we also know that\\n385:         // `Bar<'x,'x>` (for any 'x). This more-or-less falls out from\\n386:         // normal instantiation.\\n387:         //\\n388:         // In terms of why this is sound, the idea is that whenever there\\n389:         // is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>`, it must show that `T:Bar<'a,'a>`\\n390:         // holds. So if there is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>` that applies to\\n391:         // all `'a`, then we must know that `T:Bar<'a,'a>` holds for all\\n392:         // `'a`.\\n393:         //\\n394:         // Another example to be careful of is this:\\n395:         //\\n396:         //     trait Foo1<'a>: for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b> { }\\n397:         //     trait Bar1<'b,'c> { }\\n398:         //\\n399:         // Here, if we have `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`, then what do we know?\\n400:         // The answer is that we know `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`. The\\n401:         // reason is similar to the previous example: any impl of\\n402:         // `T:Foo1<'x>` must show that `for<'b> T: Bar1<'x, 'b>`. So\\n403:         // basically we would want to collapse the bound lifetimes from\\n404:         // the input (`trait_ref`) and the supertraits.\\n405:         //\\n406:         // To achieve this in practice is fairly straightforward. Let's\\n407:         // consider the more complicated scenario:\\n408:         //\\n409:         // - We start out with `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`. In this case, `'x`\\n410:         //   has a De Bruijn index of 1. We want to produce `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`,\\n411:         //   where both `'x` and `'b` would have a DB index of 1.\\n412:         //   The instantiation from the input trait-ref is therefore going to be\\n413:         //   `'a => 'x` (where `'x` has a DB index of 1).\\n414:         // - The supertrait-ref is `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, where `'a` is an\\n415:         //   early-bound parameter and `'b` is a late-bound parameter with a\\n416:         //   DB index of 1.\\n417:         // - If we replace `'a` with `'x` from the input, it too will have\\n418:         //   a DB index of 1, and thus we'll have `for<'x,'b> Bar1<'x,'b>`\\n419:         //   just as we wanted.\\n420:         //\\n421:         // There is only one catch. If we just apply the instantiation `'a\\n422:         // => 'x` to `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, the instantiation code will\\n423:         // adjust the DB index because we instantiating into a binder (it\\n424:         // tries to be so smart...) resulting in `for<'x> for<'b>\\n425:         // Bar1<'x,'b>` (we have no syntax for this, so use your\\n426:         // imagination). Basically the 'x will have DB index of 2 and 'b\\n427:         // will have DB index of 1. Not quite what we want. So we apply\\n428:         // the instantiation to the *contents* of the trait reference,\\n429:         // rather than the trait reference itself (put another way, the\\n430:         // instantiation code expects equal binding levels in the values\\n431:         // from the instantiation and the value being instantiated into, and\\n432:         // this trick achieves that).\\n433: \\n434:         // Working through the second example:\\n435:         // trait_ref: for<'x> T: Foo1<'^0.0>; args: [T, '^0.0]\\n436:         // predicate: for<'b> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0>; args: [Self, 'a, '^0.0]\\n437:         // We want to end up with:\\n438:         //     for<'x, 'b> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n439:         // To do this:\\n440:         // 1) We must shift all bound vars in predicate by the length\\n441:         //    of trait ref's bound vars. So, we would end up with predicate like\\n442:         //    Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\\n443:         // 2) We can then apply the trait args to this, ending up with\\n444:         //    T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n445:         // 3) Finally, to create the final bound vars, we concatenate the bound\\n446:         //    vars of the trait ref with those of the predicate:\\n447:         //    ['x, 'b]\\n448:         let bound_pred = self.kind();\\n449:         let pred_bound_vars = bound_pred.bound_vars();\\n450:         let trait_bound_vars = trait_ref.bound_vars();\\n451:         // 1) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0> -> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\\n452:         let shifted_pred =\\n453:             tcx.shift_bound_var_indices(trait_bound_vars.len(), bound_pred.skip_binder());\\n454:         // 2) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1> -> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\\n455:         let new = EarlyBinder::bind(shifted_pred).instantiate(tcx, trait_ref.skip_binder().args);\\n456:         // 3) ['x] + ['b] -> ['x, 'b]\\n457:         let bound_vars =\\n458:             tcx.mk_bound_variable_kinds_from_iter(trait_bound_vars.iter().chain(pred_bound_vars));\\n459: \\n460:         // FIXME: Is it really perf sensitive to use reuse_or_mk_predicate here?\\n461:         tcx.reuse_or_mk_predicate(\\n462:             self.as_predicate(),\\n463:             ty::Binder::bind_with_vars(PredicateKind::Clause(new), bound_vars),\\n464:         )\\n465:         .expect_clause()\\n466:     }\\n467: }\\n468: \\n469: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PredicateKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n470:     fn upcast_from(from: PredicateKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n471:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\\n472:     }\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n476:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n477:         tcx.mk_predicate(from)\\n478:     }\\n479: }\\n480: \\n481: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n482:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n483:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from)))\\n484:     }\\n485: }\\n486: \\n487: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n488:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n489:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(PredicateKind::Clause))\\n490:     }\\n491: }\\n492: \\n493: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, Clause<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n494:     fn upcast_from(from: Clause<'tcx>, _tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n495:         from.as_predicate()\\n496:     }\\n497: }\\n498: \\n499: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n500:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n501:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from))).expect_clause()\\n502:     }\\n503: }\\n504: \\n505: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n506:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n507:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(|clause| PredicateKind::Clause(clause))).expect_clause()\\n508:     }\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n512:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n513:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\\n514:     }\\n515: }\\n516: \\n517: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n518:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n519:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n520:         p.expect_clause()\\n521:     }\\n522: }\\n523: \\n524: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n525:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n526:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n527:         pred.upcast(tcx)\\n528:     }\\n529: }\\n530: \\n531: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n532:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n533:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n534:         pred.upcast(tcx)\\n535:     }\\n536: }\\n537: \\n538: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n539:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n540:         PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(from)).upcast(tcx)\\n541:     }\\n542: }\\n543: \\n544: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n545:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n546:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n547:     }\\n548: }\\n549: \\n550: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n551:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n552:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n553:         p.expect_clause()\\n554:     }\\n555: }\\n556: \\n557: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n558:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n559:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n560:         p.expect_clause()\\n561:     }\\n562: }\\n563: \\n564: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n565:     fn upcast_from(from: RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n566:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n567:     }\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n571:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n572:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n573:     }\\n574: }\\n575: \\n576: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n577:     fn upcast_from(from: TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n578:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n579:     }\\n580: }\\n581: \\n582: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n583:     fn upcast_from(from: ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n584:         ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(from))).upcast(tcx)\\n585:     }\\n586: }\\n587: \\n588: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n589:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n590:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(p))).upcast(tcx)\\n591:     }\\n592: }\\n593: \\n594: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n595:     fn upcast_from(from: ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n596:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n597:         p.expect_clause()\\n598:     }\\n599: }\\n600: \\n601: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\\n602:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n603:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\\n604:         p.expect_clause()\\n605:     }\\n606: }\\n607: \\n608: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\\n609:     for Predicate<'tcx>\\n610: {\\n611:     fn upcast_from(\\n612:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\\n613:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n614:     ) -> Self {\\n615:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\\n616:     }\\n617: }\\n618: \\n619: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\\n620:     for Clause<'tcx>\\n621: {\\n622:     fn upcast_from(\\n623:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\\n624:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\\n625:     ) -> Self {\\n626:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\\n627:     }\\n628: }\\n629: \\n630: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, NormalizesTo<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\\n631:     fn upcast_from(from: NormalizesTo<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\\n632:         PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(from).upcast(tcx)\\n633:     }\\n634: }\\n635: \\n636: impl<'tcx> Predicate<'tcx> {\\n637:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n638:         let predicate = self.kind();\\n639:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n640:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\\n641:             _ => None,\\n642:         }\\n643:     }\\n644: \\n645:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> {\\n646:         let predicate = self.kind();\\n647:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\\n648:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\\n649:             _ => None,\\n650:         }\\n651:     }\\n652: \\n653:     /// Matches a `PredicateKind::Clause` and turns it into a `Clause`, otherwise returns `None`.\\n654:     pub fn as_clause(self) -> Option<Clause<'tcx>> {\\n655:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n656:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Some(self.expect_clause()),\\n657:             _ => None,\\n658:         }\\n659:     }\\n660: \\n661:     /// Assert that the predicate is a clause.\\n662:     pub fn expect_clause(self) -> Clause<'tcx> {\\n663:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\\n664:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Clause(self.0),\\n665:             _ => bug!(\\\"{self} is not a clause\\\"),\\n666:         }\\n667:     }\\n668: }\\n669: \\n670: // Some types are used a lot. Make sure they don't unintentionally get bigger.\\n671: #[cfg(target_pointer_width = \\\"64\\\")]\\n672: mod size_asserts {\\n673:     use rustc_data_structures::static_assert_size;\\n674: \\n675:     use super::*;\\n676:     // tidy-alphabetical-start\\n677:     static_assert_size!(PredicateKind<'_>, 32);\\n678:     static_assert_size!(WithCachedTypeInfo<PredicateKind<'_>>, 56);\\n679:     // tidy-alphabetical-end\\n680: }\\n681: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/rust--01f6ddf7@525258043d7f9dcc1b69d9abcb9bfea32826a219/-/blob/compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: use std::cmp::Ordering;\n2: \n3: use rustc_data_structures::intern::Interned;\n4: use rustc_hir::def_id::DefId;\n5: use rustc_macros::{HashStable, extension};\n6: use rustc_type_ir as ir;\n7: \n8: use crate::ty::{\n9:     self, DebruijnIndex, EarlyBinder, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeFlags, Upcast, UpcastFrom, WithCachedTypeInfo,\n10: };\n11: \n12: pub type TraitRef<'tcx> = ir::TraitRef<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n13: pub type AliasTerm<'tcx> = ir::AliasTerm<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n14: pub type ProjectionPredicate<'tcx> = ir::ProjectionPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n15: pub type ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n16: pub type ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialTraitRef<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n17: pub type ExistentialProjection<'tcx> = ir::ExistentialProjection<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n18: pub type TraitPredicate<'tcx> = ir::TraitPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n19: pub type HostEffectPredicate<'tcx> = ir::HostEffectPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n20: pub type ClauseKind<'tcx> = ir::ClauseKind<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n21: pub type PredicateKind<'tcx> = ir::PredicateKind<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n22: pub type NormalizesTo<'tcx> = ir::NormalizesTo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n23: pub type CoercePredicate<'tcx> = ir::CoercePredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n24: pub type SubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ir::SubtypePredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n25: pub type OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, T> = ir::OutlivesPredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>, T>;\n26: pub type RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>>;\n27: pub type TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>>;\n28: pub type ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, ty::GenericArg<'tcx>>;\n29: pub type PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredicate<'tcx>>;\n30: pub type PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>;\n31: pub type PolyTypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>;\n32: pub type PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, SubtypePredicate<'tcx>>;\n33: pub type PolyCoercePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, CoercePredicate<'tcx>>;\n34: pub type PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>>;\n35: \n36: /// A statement that can be proven by a trait solver. This includes things that may\n37: /// show up in where clauses, such as trait predicates and projection predicates,\n38: /// and also things that are emitted as part of type checking such as `DynCompatible`\n39: /// predicate which is emitted when a type is coerced to a trait object.\n40: ///\n41: /// Use this rather than `PredicateKind`, whenever possible.\n42: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\n43: #[rustc_pass_by_value]\n44: pub struct Predicate<'tcx>(\n45:     pub(super) Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>>>,\n46: );\n47: \n48: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Predicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n49:     fn as_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Clause<'tcx>> {\n50:         self.as_clause()\n51:     }\n52: \n53:     fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\n54:         self.allow_normalization()\n55:     }\n56: }\n57: \n58: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Predicate<'tcx> {\n59:     type Kind = ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::PredicateKind<'tcx>>;\n60: \n61:     fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\n62:         self.kind()\n63:     }\n64: }\n65: \n66: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::Flags for Predicate<'tcx> {\n67:     fn flags(&self) -> TypeFlags {\n68:         self.0.flags\n69:     }\n70: \n71:     fn outer_exclusive_binder(&self) -> ty::DebruijnIndex {\n72:         self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\n73:     }\n74: }\n75: \n76: impl<'tcx> Predicate<'tcx> {\n77:     /// Gets the inner `ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>`.\n78:     #[inline]\n79:     pub fn kind(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>> {\n80:         self.0.internee\n81:     }\n82: \n83:     // FIXME(compiler-errors): Think about removing this.\n84:     #[inline(always)]\n85:     pub fn flags(self) -> TypeFlags {\n86:         self.0.flags\n87:     }\n88: \n89:     // FIXME(compiler-errors): Think about removing this.\n90:     #[inline(always)]\n91:     pub fn outer_exclusive_binder(self) -> DebruijnIndex {\n92:         self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\n93:     }\n94: \n95:     /// Flips the polarity of a Predicate.\n96:     ///\n97:     /// Given `T: Trait` predicate it returns `T: !Trait` and given `T: !Trait` returns `T: Trait`.\n98:     pub fn flip_polarity(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Option<Predicate<'tcx>> {\n99:         let kind = self\n100:             .kind()\n101:             .map_bound(|kind| match kind {\n102:                 PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\n103:                     trait_ref,\n104:                     polarity,\n105:                 })) => Some(PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(TraitPredicate {\n106:                     trait_ref,\n107:                     polarity: polarity.flip(),\n108:                 }))),\n109: \n110:                 _ => None,\n111:             })\n112:             .transpose()?;\n113: \n114:         Some(tcx.mk_predicate(kind))\n115:     }\n116: \n117:     /// Whether this projection can be soundly normalized.\n118:     ///\n119:     /// Wf predicates must not be normalized, as normalization\n120:     /// can remove required bounds which would cause us to\n121:     /// unsoundly accept some programs. See #91068.\n122:     #[inline]\n123:     pub fn allow_normalization(self) -> bool {\n124:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\n125:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::WellFormed(_)) | PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..) => {\n126:                 false\n127:             }\n128:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(_))\n129:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::HostEffect(..))\n130:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(_))\n131:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(_))\n132:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(_))\n133:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(..))\n134:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_))\n135:             | PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_)\n136:             | PredicateKind::Subtype(_)\n137:             | PredicateKind::Coerce(_)\n138:             | PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(_))\n139:             | PredicateKind::ConstEquate(_, _)\n140:             | PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..)\n141:             | PredicateKind::Ambiguous => true,\n142:         }\n143:     }\n144: }\n145: \n146: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Predicate<'tcx> {\n147:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\n148:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n149:             let pred = tcx.short_string(self, path);\n150:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(pred))\n151:         })\n152:     }\n153: }\n154: \n155: impl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for Clause<'tcx> {\n156:     fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\n157:         ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n158:             let clause = tcx.short_string(self, path);\n159:             rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(clause))\n160:         })\n161:     }\n162: }\n163: \n164: /// A subset of predicates which can be assumed by the trait solver. They show up in\n165: /// an item's where clauses, hence the name `Clause`, and may either be user-written\n166: /// (such as traits) or may be inserted during lowering.\n167: #[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\n168: #[rustc_pass_by_value]\n169: pub struct Clause<'tcx>(\n170:     pub(super) Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>>>,\n171: );\n172: \n173: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clause<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n174:     fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\n175:         self.as_predicate()\n176:     }\n177: \n178:     fn instantiate_supertrait(self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>) -> Self {\n179:         self.instantiate_supertrait(tcx, trait_ref)\n180:     }\n181: }\n182: \n183: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Clause<'tcx> {\n184:     type Kind = ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>;\n185: \n186:     fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\n187:         self.kind()\n188:     }\n189: }\n190: \n191: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\n192:     pub fn as_predicate(self) -> Predicate<'tcx> {\n193:         Predicate(self.0)\n194:     }\n195: \n196:     pub fn kind(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>> {\n197:         self.0.internee.map_bound(|kind| match kind {\n198:             PredicateKind::Clause(clause) => clause,\n199:             _ => unreachable!(),\n200:         })\n201:     }\n202: \n203:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredicate<'tcx>>> {\n204:         let clause = self.kind();\n205:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\n206:             Some(clause.rebind(trait_clause))\n207:         } else {\n208:             None\n209:         }\n210:     }\n211: \n212:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ProjectionPredicate<'tcx>>> {\n213:         let clause = self.kind();\n214:         if let ty::ClauseKind::Projection(projection_clause) = clause.skip_binder() {\n215:             Some(clause.rebind(projection_clause))\n216:         } else {\n217:             None\n218:         }\n219:     }\n220: \n221:     pub fn as_type_outlives_clause(self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\n222:         let clause = self.kind();\n223:         if let ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\n224:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\n225:         } else {\n226:             None\n227:         }\n228:     }\n229: \n230:     pub fn as_region_outlives_clause(\n231:         self,\n232:     ) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, RegionOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>> {\n233:         let clause = self.kind();\n234:         if let ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(o) = clause.skip_binder() {\n235:             Some(clause.rebind(o))\n236:         } else {\n237:             None\n238:         }\n239:     }\n240: }\n241: \n242: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Clauses<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ty::Clauses<'tcx> {}\n243: \n244: #[extension(pub trait ExistentialPredicateStableCmpExt<'tcx>)]\n245: impl<'tcx> ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\n246:     /// Compares via an ordering that will not change if modules are reordered or other changes are\n247:     /// made to the tree. In particular, this ordering is preserved across incremental compilations.\n248:     fn stable_cmp(&self, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\n249:         match (*self, *other) {\n250:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => Ordering::Equal,\n251:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::Projection(ref b)) => {\n252:                 tcx.def_path_hash(a.def_id).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(b.def_id))\n253:             }\n254:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref a), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(ref b)) => {\n255:                 tcx.def_path_hash(*a).cmp(&tcx.def_path_hash(*b))\n256:             }\n257:             (ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\n258:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), ExistentialPredicate::Trait(_)) => {\n259:                 Ordering::Greater\n260:             }\n261:             (ExistentialPredicate::Projection(_), _) => Ordering::Less,\n262:             (ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_), _) => Ordering::Greater,\n263:         }\n264:     }\n265: }\n266: \n267: pub type PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>;\n268: \n269: impl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::BoundExistentialPredicates<TyCtxt<'tcx>>\n270:     for &'tcx ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>\n271: {\n272:     fn principal_def_id(self) -> Option<DefId> {\n273:         self.principal_def_id()\n274:     }\n275: \n276:     fn principal(self) -> Option<ty::PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>> {\n277:         self.principal()\n278:     }\n279: \n280:     fn auto_traits(self) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = DefId> {\n281:         self.auto_traits()\n282:     }\n283: \n284:     fn projection_bounds(\n285:         self,\n286:     ) -> impl IntoIterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\n287:         self.projection_bounds()\n288:     }\n289: }\n290: \n291: impl<'tcx> ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\n292:     /// Returns the \"principal `DefId`\" of this set of existential predicates.\n293:     ///\n294:     /// A Rust trait object type consists (in addition to a lifetime bound)\n295:     /// of a set of trait bounds, which are separated into any number\n296:     /// of auto-trait bounds, and at most one non-auto-trait bound. The\n297:     /// non-auto-trait bound is called the \"principal\" of the trait\n298:     /// object.\n299:     ///\n300:     /// Only the principal can have methods or type parameters (because\n301:     /// auto traits can have neither of them). This is important, because\n302:     /// it means the auto traits can be treated as an unordered set (methods\n303:     /// would force an order for the vtable, while relating traits with\n304:     /// type parameters without knowing the order to relate them in is\n305:     /// a rather non-trivial task).\n306:     ///\n307:     /// For example, in the trait object `dyn std::fmt::Debug + Sync`, the\n308:     /// principal bound is `Some(std::fmt::Debug)`, while the auto-trait bounds\n309:     /// are the set `{Sync}`.\n310:     ///\n311:     /// It is also possible to have a \"trivial\" trait object that\n312:     /// consists only of auto traits, with no principal - for example,\n313:     /// `dyn Send + Sync`. In that case, the set of auto-trait bounds\n314:     /// is `{Send, Sync}`, while there is no principal. These trait objects\n315:     /// have a \"trivial\" vtable consisting of just the size, alignment,\n316:     /// and destructor.\n317:     pub fn principal(&self) -> Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>> {\n318:         self[0]\n319:             .map_bound(|this| match this {\n320:                 ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => Some(tr),\n321:                 _ => None,\n322:             })\n323:             .transpose()\n324:     }\n325: \n326:     pub fn principal_def_id(&self) -> Option<DefId> {\n327:         self.principal().map(|trait_ref| trait_ref.skip_binder().def_id)\n328:     }\n329: \n330:     #[inline]\n331:     pub fn projection_bounds(\n332:         &self,\n333:     ) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>> {\n334:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| {\n335:             predicate\n336:                 .map_bound(|pred| match pred {\n337:                     ExistentialPredicate::Projection(projection) => Some(projection),\n338:                     _ => None,\n339:                 })\n340:                 .transpose()\n341:         })\n342:     }\n343: \n344:     #[inline]\n345:     pub fn auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = DefId> {\n346:         self.iter().filter_map(|predicate| match predicate.skip_binder() {\n347:             ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(did) => Some(did),\n348:             _ => None,\n349:         })\n350:     }\n351: \n352:     pub fn without_auto_traits(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>> {\n353:         self.iter().filter(|predicate| {\n354:             !matches!(predicate.as_ref().skip_binder(), ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_))\n355:         })\n356:     }\n357: }\n358: \n359: pub type PolyTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>;\n360: pub type PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>;\n361: pub type PolyExistentialProjection<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, ExistentialProjection<'tcx>>;\n362: \n363: impl<'tcx> Clause<'tcx> {\n364:     /// Performs a instantiation suitable for going from a\n365:     /// poly-trait-ref to supertraits that must hold if that\n366:     /// poly-trait-ref holds. This is slightly different from a normal\n367:     /// instantiation in terms of what happens with bound regions. See\n368:     /// lengthy comment below for details.\n369:     pub fn instantiate_supertrait(\n370:         self,\n371:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n372:         trait_ref: ty::PolyTraitRef<'tcx>,\n373:     ) -> Clause<'tcx> {\n374:         // The interaction between HRTB and supertraits is not entirely\n375:         // obvious. Let me walk you (and myself) through an example.\n376:         //\n377:         // Let's start with an easy case. Consider two traits:\n378:         //\n379:         //     trait Foo<'a>: Bar<'a,'a> { }\n380:         //     trait Bar<'b,'c> { }\n381:         //\n382:         // Now, if we have a trait reference `for<'x> T: Foo<'x>`, then\n383:         // we can deduce that `for<'x> T: Bar<'x,'x>`. Basically, if we\n384:         // knew that `Foo<'x>` (for any 'x) then we also know that\n385:         // `Bar<'x,'x>` (for any 'x). This more-or-less falls out from\n386:         // normal instantiation.\n387:         //\n388:         // In terms of why this is sound, the idea is that whenever there\n389:         // is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>`, it must show that `T:Bar<'a,'a>`\n390:         // holds. So if there is an impl of `T:Foo<'a>` that applies to\n391:         // all `'a`, then we must know that `T:Bar<'a,'a>` holds for all\n392:         // `'a`.\n393:         //\n394:         // Another example to be careful of is this:\n395:         //\n396:         //     trait Foo1<'a>: for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b> { }\n397:         //     trait Bar1<'b,'c> { }\n398:         //\n399:         // Here, if we have `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`, then what do we know?\n400:         // The answer is that we know `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`. The\n401:         // reason is similar to the previous example: any impl of\n402:         // `T:Foo1<'x>` must show that `for<'b> T: Bar1<'x, 'b>`. So\n403:         // basically we would want to collapse the bound lifetimes from\n404:         // the input (`trait_ref`) and the supertraits.\n405:         //\n406:         // To achieve this in practice is fairly straightforward. Let's\n407:         // consider the more complicated scenario:\n408:         //\n409:         // - We start out with `for<'x> T: Foo1<'x>`. In this case, `'x`\n410:         //   has a De Bruijn index of 1. We want to produce `for<'x,'b> T: Bar1<'x,'b>`,\n411:         //   where both `'x` and `'b` would have a DB index of 1.\n412:         //   The instantiation from the input trait-ref is therefore going to be\n413:         //   `'a => 'x` (where `'x` has a DB index of 1).\n414:         // - The supertrait-ref is `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, where `'a` is an\n415:         //   early-bound parameter and `'b` is a late-bound parameter with a\n416:         //   DB index of 1.\n417:         // - If we replace `'a` with `'x` from the input, it too will have\n418:         //   a DB index of 1, and thus we'll have `for<'x,'b> Bar1<'x,'b>`\n419:         //   just as we wanted.\n420:         //\n421:         // There is only one catch. If we just apply the instantiation `'a\n422:         // => 'x` to `for<'b> Bar1<'a,'b>`, the instantiation code will\n423:         // adjust the DB index because we instantiating into a binder (it\n424:         // tries to be so smart...) resulting in `for<'x> for<'b>\n425:         // Bar1<'x,'b>` (we have no syntax for this, so use your\n426:         // imagination). Basically the 'x will have DB index of 2 and 'b\n427:         // will have DB index of 1. Not quite what we want. So we apply\n428:         // the instantiation to the *contents* of the trait reference,\n429:         // rather than the trait reference itself (put another way, the\n430:         // instantiation code expects equal binding levels in the values\n431:         // from the instantiation and the value being instantiated into, and\n432:         // this trick achieves that).\n433: \n434:         // Working through the second example:\n435:         // trait_ref: for<'x> T: Foo1<'^0.0>; args: [T, '^0.0]\n436:         // predicate: for<'b> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0>; args: [Self, 'a, '^0.0]\n437:         // We want to end up with:\n438:         //     for<'x, 'b> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\n439:         // To do this:\n440:         // 1) We must shift all bound vars in predicate by the length\n441:         //    of trait ref's bound vars. So, we would end up with predicate like\n442:         //    Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\n443:         // 2) We can then apply the trait args to this, ending up with\n444:         //    T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\n445:         // 3) Finally, to create the final bound vars, we concatenate the bound\n446:         //    vars of the trait ref with those of the predicate:\n447:         //    ['x, 'b]\n448:         let bound_pred = self.kind();\n449:         let pred_bound_vars = bound_pred.bound_vars();\n450:         let trait_bound_vars = trait_ref.bound_vars();\n451:         // 1) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.0> -> Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1>\n452:         let shifted_pred =\n453:             tcx.shift_bound_var_indices(trait_bound_vars.len(), bound_pred.skip_binder());\n454:         // 2) Self: Bar1<'a, '^0.1> -> T: Bar1<'^0.0, '^0.1>\n455:         let new = EarlyBinder::bind(shifted_pred).instantiate(tcx, trait_ref.skip_binder().args);\n456:         // 3) ['x] + ['b] -> ['x, 'b]\n457:         let bound_vars =\n458:             tcx.mk_bound_variable_kinds_from_iter(trait_bound_vars.iter().chain(pred_bound_vars));\n459: \n460:         // FIXME: Is it really perf sensitive to use reuse_or_mk_predicate here?\n461:         tcx.reuse_or_mk_predicate(\n462:             self.as_predicate(),\n463:             ty::Binder::bind_with_vars(PredicateKind::Clause(new), bound_vars),\n464:         )\n465:         .expect_clause()\n466:     }\n467: }\n468: \n469: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PredicateKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n470:     fn upcast_from(from: PredicateKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n471:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\n472:     }\n473: }\n474: \n475: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n476:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, PredicateKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n477:         tcx.mk_predicate(from)\n478:     }\n479: }\n480: \n481: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n482:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n483:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from)))\n484:     }\n485: }\n486: \n487: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n488:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n489:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(PredicateKind::Clause))\n490:     }\n491: }\n492: \n493: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, Clause<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n494:     fn upcast_from(from: Clause<'tcx>, _tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n495:         from.as_predicate()\n496:     }\n497: }\n498: \n499: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ClauseKind<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n500:     fn upcast_from(from: ClauseKind<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n501:         tcx.mk_predicate(ty::Binder::dummy(PredicateKind::Clause(from))).expect_clause()\n502:     }\n503: }\n504: \n505: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n506:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ClauseKind<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n507:         tcx.mk_predicate(from.map_bound(|clause| PredicateKind::Clause(clause))).expect_clause()\n508:     }\n509: }\n510: \n511: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n512:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n513:         ty::Binder::dummy(from).upcast(tcx)\n514:     }\n515: }\n516: \n517: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitRef<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n518:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitRef<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n519:         let p: Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\n520:         p.expect_clause()\n521:     }\n522: }\n523: \n524: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n525:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n526:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\n527:         pred.upcast(tcx)\n528:     }\n529: }\n530: \n531: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n532:     fn upcast_from(from: ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRef<'tcx>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n533:         let pred: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\n534:         pred.upcast(tcx)\n535:     }\n536: }\n537: \n538: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n539:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n540:         PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(from)).upcast(tcx)\n541:     }\n542: }\n543: \n544: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n545:     fn upcast_from(from: PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n546:         from.map_bound(|p| PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(p))).upcast(tcx)\n547:     }\n548: }\n549: \n550: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TraitPredicate<'tcx>> for Clause<'tcx> {\n551:     fn upcast_from(from: TraitPredicate<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n552: PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(p))).upcast(tcx)\n573:     }\n574: }\n575: \n576: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, TypeOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n577:     fn Predicate<'tcx> = from.upcast(tcx);\n604:         p.expect_clause()\n605:     }\n606: }\n607: \n608: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\n609:     for Predicate<'tcx>\n610: {\n611:     fn upcast_from(\n612:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\n613:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n614:     ) -> Self {\n615:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\n616:     }\n617: }\n618: \n619: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>>\n620:     for Clause<'tcx>\n621: {\n622:     fn upcast_from(\n623:         from: ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx>>,\n624:         tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n625:     ) -> Self {\n626:         from.map_bound(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect).upcast(tcx)\n627:     }\n628: }\n629: \n630: impl<'tcx> UpcastFrom<TyCtxt<'tcx>, NormalizesTo<'tcx>> for Predicate<'tcx> {\n631:     fn upcast_from(from: NormalizesTo<'tcx>, tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n632:         PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(from).upcast(tcx)\n633:     }\n634: }\n635: \n636: impl<'tcx> Predicate<'tcx> {\n637:     pub fn as_trait_clause(self) -> Option<PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>> {\n638:         let predicate = self.kind();\n639:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\n640:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Trait(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\n641:             _ => None,\n642:         }\n643:     }\n644: \n645:     pub fn as_projection_clause(self) -> Option<PolyProjectionPredicate<'tcx>> {\n646:         let predicate = self.kind();\n647:         match predicate.skip_binder() {\n648:             PredicateKind::Clause(ClauseKind::Projection(t)) => Some(predicate.rebind(t)),\n649:             _ => None,\n650:         }\n651:     }\n652: \n653:     /// Matches a `PredicateKind::Clause` and turns it into a `Clause`, otherwise returns `None`.\n654:     pub fn as_clause(self) -> Option<Clause<'tcx>> {\n655:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\n656:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Some(self.expect_clause()),\n657:             _ => None,\n658:         }\n659:     }\n660: \n661:     /// Assert that the predicate is a clause.\n662:     pub fn expect_clause(self) -> Clause<'tcx> {\n663:         match self.kind().skip_binder() {\n664:             PredicateKind::Clause(..) => Clause(self.0),\n665:             _ => bug!(\"{self} is not a clause\"),\n666:         }\n667:     }\n668: }\n669: \n670: // Some types are used a lot. Make sure they don't unintentionally get bigger.\n671: #[cfg(target_pointer_width = \"64\")]\n672: mod size_asserts {\n673:     use rustc_data_structures::static_assert_size;\n674: \n675:     use super::*;\n676:     // tidy-alphabetical-start\n677:     static_assert_size!(PredicateKind<'_>, 32);\n678:     static_assert_size!(WithCachedTypeInfo<PredicateKind<'_>>, 56);\n679:     // tidy-alphabetical-end\n680: }\n681: ",

  "new_string": "## Implementation Notes\n\n### Field Renaming Rationale\n- `a` \u2192 `sub_ty`: The type that must be a **sub**type\n- `b` \u2192 `super_ty`: The type that must be a **super**type\n- `a_is_expected` \u2192 (unchanged): Flag controlling diagnostic presentation, distinct semantic meaning\n\n### Variance-Based Field Swapping\nFiles `type_relating.rs` and `solver_relating.rs` have special handling:\n```rust\n// Contravariant case swaps the fields:\nty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: false, sub_ty: b, super_ty: a }\n```\nThis is intentional and correct \u2014 contravariance reverses the subtype relationship. Verify comments are preserved.\n\n### Enum Variant Name\nThe `PredicateKind::Subtype` variant **name stays as-is** (only the struct type inside changes). This is intentional because:\n- The variant name `Subtype` is a semantic label (not a struct name)\n- Only the type parameter changes: `PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypeRelation<I>)` vs `PredicateKind::Subtype(SubtypePredicate<I>)`\n\n---\n\n## Complete Code Changes\n\n### 1. compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate.rs (Lines 909-924)\n\n```diff\n-/// Encodes that `a` must be a subtype of `b`. The `a_is_expected` flag indicates\n-/// whether the `a` type is the type that we should label as \"expected\" when\n+/// Encodes that `sub_ty` must be a subtype of `super_ty`. The `a_is_expected` flag indicates\n+/// whether the `sub_ty` type is the type that we should label as \"expected\" when\n /// presenting user diagnostics.\n #[derive_where(Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Debug; I: Interner)]\n #[derive(TypeVisitable_Generic, TypeFoldable_Generic, Lift_Generic)]\n #[cfg_attr(\n     feature = \"nightly\",\n     derive(Decodable_NoContext, Encodable_NoContext, HashStable_NoContext)\n )]\n-pub struct SubtypePredicate<I: Interner> {\n+pub struct SubtypeRelation<I: Interner> {\n     pub a_is_expected: bool,\n-    pub a: I::Ty,\n-    pub b: I::Ty,\n+    pub sub_ty: I::Ty,\n+    pub super_ty: I::Ty,\n }\n\n-impl<I: Interner> Eq for SubtypePredicate<I> {}\n+impl<I: Interner> Eq for SubtypeRelation<I> {}\n```\n\n### 2. compiler/rustc_public/src/ty.rs (Lines 1510-1513)\n\n```diff\n-#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\n-pub struct SubtypePredicate {\n-    pub a: Ty,\n-    pub b: Ty,\n+#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\n+pub struct SubtypeRelation {\n+    pub sub_ty: Ty,\n+    pub super_ty: Ty,\n }\n```\n\n### 3. compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/predicate.rs (Lines 24, 32)\n\n```diff\n-pub type SubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ir::SubtypePredicate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n+pub type SubtypeRelation<'tcx> = ir::SubtypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>>;\n```\n\n```diff\n-pub type PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, SubtypePredicate<'tcx>>;\n+pub type PolySubtypeRelation<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, SubtypeRelation<'tcx>>;\n pub type PolyCoercePredicate<'tcx> = ty::Binder<'tcx, CoercePredicate<'tcx>>;\n```\n\n**Note**: Update all references to `PolySubtypePredicate` to `PolySubtypeRelation` throughout compiler\n\n### 4. compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/predicate_kind.rs (Line 78)\n\n```diff\n     /// This obligation is created most often when we have two\n     /// unresolved type variables and hence don't have enough\n     /// information to process the subtyping obligation yet.\n-    Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate<I>),\n+    Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation<I>),\n```\n\n### 5. compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs\n\n```diff\n# Lines 719-723:\n-        let subtype_predicate = predicate.map_bound(|p| ty::SubtypePredicate {\n+        let subtype_predicate = predicate.map_bound(|p| ty::SubtypeRelation {\n             a_is_expected: false, // when coercing from `a` to `b`, `b` is expected\n-            a: p.a,\n-            b: p.b,\n+            sub_ty: p.sub_ty,\n+            super_ty: p.super_ty,\n         });\n\n# Lines 746-747:\n-        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\n-        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\n+        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().sub_ty);\n+        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().super_ty);\n\n# Lines 756-759:\n-        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\n+        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected, sub_ty, super_ty }| {\n             if a_is_expected {\n-                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\n+                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, sub_ty, super_ty))\n             } else {\n-                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\n+                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, super_ty, sub_ty))\n             }\n         })\n```\n\n### 6. compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/flags.rs (Line 394)\n\n```diff\n-            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n+            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty }) => {\n-                self.add_ty(a);\n-                self.add_ty(b);\n+                self.add_ty(sub_ty);\n+                self.add_ty(super_ty);\n```\n\n### 7. compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs (Line 353)\n\n```diff\n-                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n+                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty }) => {\n-                        (a, b)\n+                        (sub_ty, super_ty)\n```\n\n### 8. compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs (Line 93)\n\n```diff\n-                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a, b, a_is_expected: _ })\n+                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { sub_ty, super_ty, a_is_expected: _ })\n-                    | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n+                    | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n```\n\n### 9. compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/ambiguity.rs (Line 503)\n\n```diff\n-                let ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b } = data;\n+                let ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty } = data;\n```\n\n### 10. compiler/rustc_next_trait_solver/src/solve/mod.rs\n\n```diff\n# Lines 112-115:\n                 predicate: ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                     a_is_expected: false,\n-                    a: goal.predicate.a,\n-                    b: goal.predicate.b,\n+                    sub_ty: goal.predicate.sub_ty,\n+                    super_ty: goal.predicate.super_ty,\n                 },\n\n# Lines 121-128:\n-    fn compute_subtype_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::SubtypePredicate<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n-        match (goal.predicate.a.kind(), goal.predicate.b.kind()) {\n+    fn compute_subtype_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::SubtypeRelation<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n+        match (goal.predicate.sub_ty.kind(), goal.predicate.super_ty.kind()) {\n```\n\n### 11. compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/relate/solver_relating.rs\n\n```diff\n# Lines 200-203:\n                             self.param_env,\n-                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n+                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                 a_is_expected: true,\n-                                a,\n-                                b,\n+                                sub_ty: a,\n+                                super_ty: b,\n\n# Lines 213-216:\n                             self.param_env,\n-                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n+                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                 a_is_expected: false,\n-                                a: b,\n-                                b: a,\n+                                sub_ty: b,\n+                                super_ty: a,\n```\n\n### 12. compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs\n\nSimilar changes to solver_relating.rs at lines 141-145 and 155-159\n\n### 13. compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs (Lines 3257-3262)\n\n```diff\n-    ty::SubtypePredicate<'tcx> {\n-        self.a.print(p)?;\n+    ty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {\n+        self.sub_ty.print(p)?;\n         write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n         p.reset_type_limit();\n-        self.b.print(p)?;\n+        self.super_ty.print(p)?;\n     }\n```\n\n### Global Replacements Needed\n\n**In multiple files** (interner.rs, ir_print.rs, mod.rs):\n- Replace all `SubtypePredicate` with `SubtypeRelation` (struct name references)\n- Keep `PolySubtypePredicate` \u2192 `PolySubtypeRelation` for type aliases\n- Keep `PredicateKind::Subtype` variant name (only the type parameter changes)\n\n---\n\n## Files Requiring Changes - Complete Checklist\n\n- [x] Core definitions prepared (4 files: predicate.rs, ty.rs in public, predicate.rs in middle, predicate_kind.rs)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/mod.rs` (5 occurrences: construction + field access + destructuring)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/flags.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/fallback.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs` (1 occurrence: pattern match)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/ambiguity.rs` (1 occurrence: destructuring)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_next_trait_solver/src/solve/mod.rs` (3 occurrences: construction + field access)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_type_ir/src/relate/solver_relating.rs` (2 occurrences: construction with variance)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs` (2 occurrences: construction with variance)\n- [ ] `compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs` (1 occurrence: field access in printing)\n- [ ] Supporting files (interner.rs, ir_print.rs, mod.rs)\n\n**Total: 13 files, ~25 code sections**\n\n---\n\n## Expected Outcome\n\nAfter this refactoring:\n1. The struct is named `SubtypeRelation<I>` instead of `SubtypePredicate<I>`\n2. Fields are `sub_ty` and `super_ty` instead of `a` and `b`\n3. All pattern matches, destructuring, and field accesses are updated\n4. The code compiles without errors\n5. All existing tests pass (behavior unchanged, only naming improved)\n6. Documentation comments and variant names remain appropriate",

sections**\n\n---\n\n## Expected Outcome\n\nAfter this refactoring:\n1.
